blocks:
Sūrat al-An‘ām

Meccan, and it is 165 verses

Sūrat al-An‘ām

It is Meccan, except for six verses: وَما قَدَرُوا اللهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ ** until the end of three verses, قُلْ تَعالَوْا أَتْلُ ما حَرَّمَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ ** until the end of three verses, for they were revealed in Medina. And the number of its verses is one hundred and sixty-five verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ. (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful.)

(١) الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ** (1) All praise belongs to Allah who created the heavens and the earth, He described Himself with that by which He alerted [mankind] that He is the One deserving of praise, whether He is praised or not, so that it may be a proof against those who equate others with Him (العادلين به). وَجَعَلَ الظُّلُماتِ وَالنُّورَ ** and made the darkness and the light. He originated them (انشأهما). The difference between الخلق (creation) and الجعل (making/appointing) is that الخلق contains the meaning of predestination (التقدير), while الجعل contains the meaning of transformation (التصيير), like originating one thing from another. ثُمَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ ** Yet those who are faithless equate others with their Lord. It means that He created what none besides Him is capable of, yet they equate with Him that which is incapable of anything. And the meaning of ثمّ (then/yet) is to express the improbability (استبعاد) of their deviation (عدولهم) (١) after this clarity.

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith regarding the revelation of this verse: that it is a refutation against three types [of people]. When He said الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ ** (All praise belongs to Allah who created the heavens and the earth), it was a refutation against the الدهرية (Materialists/Atheists) who said that things have no beginning (لا بدْو لها) and are eternally existing (قائمة). Then He said وَجَعَلَ الظُّلُماتِ وَالنُّورَ ** (and made the darkness and the light), so it was a refutation against the الثنوية (Dualists).

(٢) Those who said that the Light (النّور) and the Darkness (الظّلمة) are the two managers (المدّبران). Then He said: ثُمَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ ** (Then those who are faithless ascribe equals to their Lord.) So, it was a refutation against the polytheists of the Arabs who said that our idols (أوثاننا) are gods (آلهة). (٢) هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ طِينٍ ** (It is He who created you from clay) Meaning, He began your creation from it. ثُمَّ قَضى أَجَلاً ** (then He determined a term) He decreed and predetermined a fixed term (أجلاً محتوماً) for your death, which neither advances nor delays. وَأَجَلٌ مُسَمًّى عِنْدَهُ ** (and there is a determined term with Him) Also for your death, which He erases and establishes another in its place, due to the wisdom of charity (الصدقة), supplication (الدّعاءِ), maintaining kinship ties (صلة الرحم), and other things that realize fear (الخوف) and hope (الرّجاء) and the necessities of servitude (العبودية). For by these and their opposites, life increases and decreases. And in this is the secret of al-Bada' (البداءِ). We have explained this fully in our book called al-Wafi, based on al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding its interpretation. He said: "There are two terms (أجلان): a fixed term (أجل محتوم) and a suspended term (أجل موقوف)." And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "The decreed term (الأجل المقضيّ) is the fixed term (المحتوم) which Allah has decreed and finalized. And the determined term (المسمّى) is that in which al-Bada' (البداء) occurs; He advances what He wills and delays what He wills. As for the fixed term (المحتوم), there is no advancing or delaying in it." ثُمَّ أَنْتُمْ تَمْتَرُونَ ** (yet you are skeptical.) You doubt concerning Him and concerning His resurrection of you. This is a denunciation of their skepticism after it has been established that He is their Creator, the Creator of their origins, and the One Who gives them life until their appointed terms (آجالهم). For indeed, the One Who was able to create the origins (الأصول) and gather them, and originate life (الحيوة) within them, and keep them alive as long as He wills, and suspend them in the term (الأجل) after fixing it, in fear (الخوف) and hope (الرّجاءِ), after decreeing the matter, was worthy of being worshipped, and He would be more capable of gathering the origins and reviving them a second time. Thus, the first Ayah is a proof of Monotheism (التوحيد), and the second Ayah is a proof of Monotheism (التوحيد) and the Resurrection (البعث) combined.

footnote (١) And they ascribed equals to Allah (*وعدلوا بالله*), meaning they associated partners with Him (*أشركوا به*) and made a likeness (*مثلاً*) for Him. From this is the narration of Ali (peace be upon him): "The ones who ascribe equals to You have lied, for they likened You to their idols (*أصنامهم*)."

footnote (٢) *Al-Thanawiyyah* (*الثّنويّة*) are those who affirm another eternal being (*قديماً*) alongside the Eternal (*القديم*). It is said that they are the sects of the Magians (*المجوس*) who affirm two principles: a principle for good and a principle for evil, which are the Light (*النّور*) and the Darkness (*الظّلمة*). They also affirm the prophethood of Abraham. And it is said that they are a group who say that every created thing is created for the first creation (*الخلق الأوّل*). The saying of the Imam (peace be upon him) regarding the description of the Almighty Truth (*الحقّ تعالى*) testifies to the invalidity of their statement: "He was not from a thing (*لا من شيءٍ كان*), nor did He create what was from a thing (*ولا من شيء خلق ما كان*)." By this, all the arguments and doubts (*شبههم*) of *al-Thanawiyyah* (*الثنويّة*) are refuted.

(٣) وَهُوَ اللهُ فِي السَّماواتِ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ ** (And He is Allah in the heavens and in the earth) He is the One worshipped in both (the heavens and the earth), and the One known by divinity (الإِلهية) and oneness (الوحدانية). This is similar to His saying: وَهُوَ الَّذِي فِي السَّماءِ إِلهٌ وَفِي الْأَرْضِ إِلهٌ ** (And He is the God in the heaven and the God in the earth) In al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "Likewise, He is in every place." It was asked: "By His essence (بذاته)?" He said: "Woe unto you! Places (الأماكن) are measures (أقدار). If you say 'in a place by His essence (بذاته)', it would necessitate that you say 'in measures (أقدار)' and other things besides that. Rather, He is distinct (باين) from His creation, encompassing what He created with knowledge (علماً), power (قدرةً), comprehensiveness (احاطةً), and authority (سلطاناً)." And His knowledge of what is on the earth is not less than what is in the heaven. Nothing is distant from Him, and things are equal in His sight in terms of knowledge (علماً), power (قدرة), authority (سلطاناً), dominion (ملكاً), and comprehensiveness (احاطةً). يَعْلَمُ سِرَّكُمْ وَجَهْرَكُمْ. (He knows your secrets and your disclosures.) Al-Qummi said: The secret (السّرّ) is what one keeps hidden within himself, and the disclosure (الجهر) is what he makes manifest. وَيَعْلَمُ ما تَكْسِبُونَ (And He knows what you earn) of good and evil, and He rewards for it and punishes.

(٤) وَما تَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ آيَةٍ مِنْ آياتِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَّا كانُوا عَنْها مُعْرِضِينَ ** (4) And there does not come to them any sign from the signs of their Lord but they turn away from it. abandoning the consideration of it, not paying attention to it. (٥) فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا بِالْحَقِ ** (5) Thus they have denied the truth (The truth is) what Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم brought **لَمَّا جاءَهُمْ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيهِمْ أَنْباءُ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** when it came to them, but soon there will come to them the news of what they used to ridicule. So there will become manifest to them what they used to ridicule when the punishment descends upon them.

(٦) أَلَمْ يَرَوْا كَمْ أَهْلَكْنا مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ مِنْ قَرْنٍ (6) Have they not regarded how many generations We destroyed before them meaning, from the people of a time (أهل زمان) مَكَّنَّاهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ whom We had established in the land We granted them expansiveness (البسطة) in their bodies and abundance (السّعة) in their wealth ما لَمْ نُمَكِّنْ لَكُمْ as We have not established you? that which We have not granted to you, O people of Mecca. And there is a shift (التفات) in the discourse وَأَرْسَلْنَا السَّماءَ We sent down the sky meaning, the rain (المطر) عَلَيْهِمْ مِدْراراً upon them in copious [showers], abundantly (مغزاراً) (1) وَجَعَلْنَا الْأَنْهارَ تَجْرِي مِنْ تَحْتِهِمْ and made the rivers run at their feet. So they lived in abundance (الخصب) (2) amidst the rivers and the fruits فَأَهْلَكْناهُمْ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ Yet We destroyed them for their sins, and that did not avail them anything. وَأَنْشَأْنا and raised up meaning, and We brought into existence (وأحدّثنا) مِنْ بَعْدِهِمْ قَرْناً آخَرِينَ after them other generations. as a replacement for them. This means that just as We decreed that We would destroy those before you, such as 'Ad and Thamud, and bring into existence others in their place, We have decreed that We shall do that to you.

(٧) وَلَوْ نَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكَ كِتاباً فِي قِرْطاسٍ ** (7) And even if We had sent down to you a book on paper written on paper فَلَمَسُوهُ بِأَيْدِيهِمْ ** and they touched it with their hands and it was not limited for them to mere sight, lest they say, "Our eyes were bewitched (سُكِّرَتْ أَبْصَارُنَا)." **لَقالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هذا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ ** those who disbelieved would have surely said, 'This is nothing but manifest magic' due to the greatness of their obstinacy and the hardness of their hearts.

(٨) وَقالُوا لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ ** (8) And they said, 'Why has not an angel been sent down to him?' (An angel) who would confirm him and speak to us that he is a prophet, due to His saying: لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ فَيَكُونَ مَعَهُ نَذِيراً وَلَوْ أَنْزَلْنا مَلَكاً لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ ** Why has not an angel been sent down to him, to be a warner along with him? Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been decided. (Meaning) their destruction would have become due, for the established way (سُنَّة) of Allah has proceeded thus concerning those before them. ثُمَّ لا يُنْظَرُونَ ** and they would not be granted any respite.** They would not be granted respite after its descent for the blink of an eye.

(٩) وَلَوْ جَعَلْناهُ مَلَكاً لَجَعَلْناهُ رَجُلاً (9) Had We made him an angel, We would have surely made him a man This is a second answer, or an answer to a second proposal, for they would sometimes say: لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ (Why has not an angel been sent down to him?) and sometimes they would say: لَوْ شاءَ رَبُّنا لَأَنْزَلَ مَلائِكَةً (Had our Lord wished, He would have surely sent down angels). The meaning is: Had We made an angel a companion (قريناً) for you, who would confirm you and whom they could observe, or had We made an angel in your place, as they proposed, We would have surely made him appear as a man, just as Gabriel was made to appear in the form of Dihyah, for human strength is unable to see the angel in his true form. وَلَلَبَسْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ما يَلْبِسُونَ (and We would have surely confused them with what they confuse [themselves with]). And We would have mixed up for them what they mix up for themselves, so they would say: "This is nothing but a human like us," and they would deny him just as they denied you, as mentioned in Tafsir al-Imam (peace be upon him) regarding Surat al-Baqarah.

And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: I asked Abu Ali ibn Muhammad (peace be upon them both): "Did the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم debate the Jews and the polytheists when they reproached Him, and did He argue with them?" He replied: "Many times. One day, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was sitting in the courtyard (بفناءِ) of the Ka'bah when Abdullah ibn Abi Umayyah al-Makhzumi began, saying: 'O Muhammad, you have made a great claim and spoken a terrifying statement! You allege that you are the Messenger of the Lord of the Worlds, yet it is not fitting for the Lord of the Worlds and the Creator of all creation that His Messenger should be a human like us. Had you been a prophet, there would have been an angel with you to confirm you, whom we could observe. Rather, if Allah intended to send a prophet to us, He would only send an angel, not a human like us. O Muhammad, you are nothing but bewitched (مسحوراً) and you are not a prophet!'" The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم then said: "O Allah, You are the Hearer of every sound and the Knower of everything. You know what Your servants have said." Then, [the revelation] descended upon Him: O Muhammad, وَقالُوا لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ وَلَوْ أَنْزَلْنا مَلَكاً لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ (And they say, 'Why has not an angel been sent down to him?' Had We sent down an angel, the matter would have been decided) up to His saying, the Almighty: وَلَلَبَسْنا عَلَيْهِمْ ما يَلْبِسُونَ (and We would have surely confused them with what they confuse [themselves with]).

Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "As for your saying to me, 'Had you been a prophet, there would have been an angel with you to confirm you, whom we could observe; rather, if He intended to send a prophet to us, He would only send an angel, not a human like us,'—the angel is not observed by your senses because he is of the same nature as this air (الهواءِ); there is no direct observation (لا عيان منه). Even if you were to observe him by increasing the strength of your eyesight, you would say, 'This is not an angel, but this is a human,' because he would only appear to you in the form of the human you are accustomed to, so that you might understand his speech and recognize his address and intention. How, then, would you know the truthfulness of the angel and that what he says is true? Rather, Allah only sent a human as a Messenger and manifested through his hand miracles (المعجزات) which are not inherent in the natures of humans—those whose inner hearts (ضمائر قلوبكم) you know—so that you may know, through your inability to produce what he brought, that it is a miracle, and that this is a testimony from Allah to his truthfulness. If an angel appeared to you, and there appeared through his hand something that humans are incapable of, there would be nothing in that to indicate to you that this is not inherent in the natures of the rest of his kind among the angels, such that it would become a miracle. Do you not see that the birds that fly—that is not a miracle from them, because they have kinds (أجناساً) from which the like of their flying occurs? But if a human flew like their flying, that would be a miracle. Thus, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made the matter easy for you and made him like you, such that His proof (حجّته) is established against you, while you propose the difficult action (عمل الصّعب) in which there is no proof." The narration continues, and a portion of it will come in Surat al-Furqan and another in Surat al-Zukhruf, if Allah wills.

footnote (١) In the *Hadith*: The Imam is like the abundant spring (*العين الغزيرة*). It is said: the water became abundant (*غزر الماء*) (with a *dammah*), *ghizāran* (*غزاراً*) and *ghizāratan* (*غزارة*), meaning it became plentiful, so it is *ghazīr* (*غزير*), meaning plentiful. The intended meaning is the intensity and universality of the benefit. And *al-midrār* (*المدرار*) means that which yields much milk (*الدّر*); it is a *mif'āl* (*مفعال*) form, which is the same for masculine and feminine.

footnote (٢) *Al-Khasb* (*الخصب*) (with a *kasrah*, like *himl* (*حِمل*)): growth and blessing. And *al-mar'ā al-khasb* (*المرعى الخصب*) is pasture with abundant herbage (*العشب*).

(10) وَلَقَدِ اسْتُهْزِئَ بِرُسُلٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (10) Certainly apostles have been ridiculed before you. This is a consolation (تسلية) for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم regarding what He sees from His people (قومِهِ). فَحاقَ بِالَّذِينَ سَخِرُوا مِنْهُمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** Then what they ridiculed encompassed those who scoffed at them. So that which they ridiculed—namely, the punishment (العذاب)—encompassed them.

(١١) قُلْ سِيرُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ ثُمَّ انْظُرُوا كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (11) Say, 'Travel through the earth and observe how was the fate of the deniers.' It was said: meaning, travel in it. ثُمَّ انْظُرُوا ** with your eyes and reflect with your hearts. And Al-Qummi (said): meaning انْظُرُوا ** in the Quran and the narratives (أخبار) of the Prophets. So look, and its parallel has already passed, narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in Surah Aal 'Imran. كَيْفَ كانَ عاقِبَةُ الْمُكَذِّبِينَ ** (meaning) those who mocked (المستهزئين) the Messengers from the previous nations (الأمم السالفة), where He eradicated them with the punishment (بالعذاب).

(١٢) قُلْ لِمَنْ ما فِي السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (12) Say, ‘To whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth?’ A question of rebuke (تبكيت) (1). Say: لِلَّهِ (To Allah). This is an affirmation for them, meaning: it belongs to Allah, there is no disagreement between me and you regarding that, and you are unable to attribute any part of it to other than Him. كَتَبَ عَلى نَفْسِهِ الرَّحْمَةَ He has prescribed mercy for Himself. He made it incumbent upon His essence (ذاته) in guiding you to His knowledge and the understanding of His Oneness (توحيده), by establishing proofs (الأدّلة), sending down the Books (الكتب), and granting respite (الإِمهال) despite disbelief (الكفر) and sins (الذنوب), so that what has been neglected may be rectified. لَيَجْمَعَنَّكُمْ He will surely gather you generation after generation إِلى يَوْمِ الْقِيامَةِ لا رَيْبَ فِيهِ on the Day of Resurrection, in which there is no doubt. It is said that this is a new sentence (استيناف) and a threat (وعيد) against their association (of partners with Allah) (اشراكهم) and their neglect of reflection (اغفالهم النظر). And it is said that it is a substitute (بدل) for الرَّحْمَةَ (mercy), for it is part of it. الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ Those who have ruined their souls by squandering their principal asset (رأس ما لهم), which is the original disposition (الفطرة الأصليّة). فَهُمْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ it is they who will not have faith. For the nullification (إبطال) of the disposition (الفطرة) led them to persistence (الإِصرار) in disbelief (الكفر).

(١٣) وَلَهُ وَللهِ ما سَكَنَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ (To Him belongs whatever dwells in the night and the day.) That which settled and resided (حَلَّ) by way of dwelling (السُّكْنَى). He mentioned in the first part (of the discussion) السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (the heavens and the earth), which encompass all places (الْأَمْكِنَة). And here (He mentioned) اللَّيْلِ وَالنَّهارِ ** (the night and the day), which encompass all times (الْأَزْمِنَة), so that it may encompass all existing things (الْمَوْجُودَات) which fall under the two circumstances (الظَّرْفَيْن). وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ (And He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing). Nothing is hidden from Him.

(١٤) قُلْ أَغَيْرَ اللهِ أَتَّخِذُ وَلِيًّا ** (14) Say, ‘Shall I take for a guardian other than Allah— This is a denial (إنكار) of taking other than Allah as a guardian (وليّاً), not a denial of taking a guardian [in general]. That is why (غير) was placed first and the interrogative hamza (الهمزة) was placed before it. فاطِرِ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ the Creator (منشؤهما) and Innovator (ومبدعهما) of them both, Who began [their creation] by His power and wisdom without following a prior example (من غير احتذاءِ مثال). وَهُوَ يُطْعِمُ وَلا يُطْعَمُ ** and He feeds, but is not fed?’ He provides sustenance (يرزق) and is not provided sustenance (ولا يرزق). This means that all benefits (المنافع) are from Him, and benefit (الانتفاعُ) is not permissible for Him [to receive]. قُلْ إِنِّي أُمِرْتُ meaning, my Lord commanded me— أَنْ أَكُونَ أَوَّلَ مَنْ أَسْلَمَ ** that I should be the first of those who submit [to Him].’ because the Prophet is the predecessor of his community (أمّته) in Islam. وَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ and [I have been commanded], ‘Do not be one of the polytheists.’ And it was said to me: وَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ (and ‘Do not be one of the polytheists’). It is permissible to connect (عطفه) it to [the command] قُلْ** (Say).

footnote (١) التّبكيت التقريع والتوبيخ كما يقال له يا فاسق أما استحييت أما خفت الله. (1) Al-Tabkit (التّبكيت) is censure (التقريع) and reprimand (التوبيخ), just as one says to him: "O wicked one (يا فاسق), have you no shame? Do you not fear Allah?"

(15) قُلْ إِنِّي أَخافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّي عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ ** Say, 'Indeed, I fear, if I disobey my Lord, the punishment of a tremendous day.' Another strong assertion in cutting off their ambitions and an allusion to them that they are disobedient ones deserving of the punishment. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not abandon [saying] إِنِّي أَخافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّي عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ until Surat al-Fath was revealed, and he did not return to that saying.

(١٦) مَنْ يُصْرَفْ عَنْهُ يَوْمَئِذٍ ** (He from whom it is averted on that day) It means the punishment (العذاب). And it was recited with the active voice (بالبناءِ للفاعل). then He has certainly had mercy upon him and bestowed favor upon him. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "By Him in Whose hand is my soul, none among mankind shall enter Paradise by virtue of his deeds." They said: "Not even you, O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Not even I, unless Allah encompasses me with mercy and favor from Him." وَذلِكَ الْفَوْزُ الْمُبِينُ.** (And that is the manifest triumph.)

(١٧) وَإِنْ يَمْسَسْكَ اللهُ بِضُرٍّ ** (17) If Allah visits you with a distress, with an affliction, such as illness (مرض) and poverty (فقر). فَلا كاشِفَ لَهُ ** there is none who may remove it except Him; then there is none capable of removing it except Him. وَإِنْ يَمْسَسْكَ بِخَيْرٍ ** and if He visits you with a good, with a blessing (نعمة), such as health (صحة) and wealth (غنىً). فَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** then He has power over all things. He is capable of maintaining it (إدامته) and removing it (إزالته).

(١٨) وَهُوَ الْقاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبادِهِ ** (18) He is the All-dominant over His servants. (It is) a depiction of His dominance (قهره) and His exaltedness (علّوه) through overwhelming power (الغلبة) and capability (القدرة), meaning that they are under His subjugation (تسخيره) and humbling (تذليله). وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ ** and He is the All-wise, in His command (أمره) and His governance (تدبيره). الْخَبِيرُ ** the All-aware. of the servants (العباد), the hidden aspects of their conditions (خفايا أحوالهم), and of everything (بكل شيء).

(19) قُلْ أَيُّ شَيْءٍ أَكْبَرُ شَهادَةً ** (Say, 'What thing is greater as a witness?') meaning: greater in testimony (شهادة) and more truthful. قُلِ اللهُ شَهِيدٌ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ** (Say, 'Allah is witness between me and you,') It was said that الله (Allah) is the answer, and شهيد (witness) is a new sentence (مستأنف) with the implied subject هو (He). And it was said that rather الله شهيد (Allah is witness) suffices in place of the answer (سادّ مسدّ الجواب).

I say: Perhaps what is intended is that it does not require an answer, and the meaning of the question is that it is not hidden that Allah is the greatest thing in testimony (شهادة), and you also know that. And the meaning of اللهُ شَهِيدٌ ** (Allah is witness) is that Allah, Who is the greatest thing in testimony (شهادة), is the One Who testifies to my prophethood. The application of the term الشيء (thing) to Allah, the Exalted, was permissible only to remove Him from the limit of negation (التعطيل). However, He is a thing (شيء) unlike [other] things (الأشياء). This is narrated in al-Kafi from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, the polytheists of Mecca said: "O Muhammad, Allah did not find a messenger to send other than you. We do not see anyone who confirms what you say." This was at the beginning of his call, when He was in Mecca. They said: "We have asked the Jews and the Christians about you, and they claim that there is no mention of you with them. So bring us a matter that testifies that you are the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: **اللهُ شَهِيدٌ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ وَأُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ هذَا الْقُرْآنُ لِأُنْذِرَكُمْ بِهِ وَمَنْ بَلَغَ ** (Allah is witness between me and you, and this Qur'an has been revealed to me that I may warn you thereby and whomever it may reach.) It was said: meaning, I warn you and I warn all others whom it reaches until the Day of Resurrection.

And in al-Majma', al-Kafi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, وَمَنْ بَلَغَ (and whomever it may reach): that it refers to an Imam from the Household of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them), for he warns by the Qur'an just as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم warned by it.

And al-Qummi narrated what is similar in meaning. أَإِنَّكُمْ لَتَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ مَعَ اللهِ آلِهَةً أُخْرى ** (Do you indeed bear witness that there are other gods besides Allah?) This is an affirmation (تقرير) directed at them, coupled with denial and astonishment (إنكار واستبعاد). قُلْ لا أَشْهَدُ (Say, 'I do not bear witness') to what you testify. قُلْ إِنَّما هُوَ إِلهٌ واحِدٌ ** (Say, 'Indeed He is the One God,') Rather, I testify that there is no god but Him. وَإِنَّنِي بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ (and I am absolved of what you associate [with Him]) of idols (الأوثان) and other things.

(20) الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ يَعْرِفُونَهُ (Those whom We have given the Book recognize him) They recognize the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم by his description (حِلْيَة) mentioned in the Torah and the Gospel كَما يَعْرِفُونَ أَبْناءَهُمُ (just as they recognize their own children.) by their descriptions (حِلاهم) (1).

Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning the Jews and the Christians, because Allah had sent down to them in the Torah, the Gospel, and the Psalms (الزبور) the description (صفة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, the description of his Companions, and his place of migration (مهاجره). And that is His Almighty saying: مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللهِ صلى الله عليه وآله until His saying: ذلِكَ مَثَلُهُمْ فِي التَّوْراةِ وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِي الْإِنْجِيلِ (That is their parable in the Torah, and their parable in the Gospel.) So this is the description (صفة) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله in the Torah and the Gospel, and the description of his Companions. When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent him, the People of the Book recognized him, just as He, majestic is His glory, said: فَلَمَّا جاءَهُمْ ما عَرَفُوا كَفَرُوا بِهِ (But when there came to them what they recognized, they disbelieved in it.)

الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ (Those who have ruined their souls) from the People of the Book and the polytheists (المشركين), they do not believe because of their squandering (تضييعهم) that by which faith (الإِيمان) is acquired.

(1) al-Hilyah (الحلية) with a kasrah means the description (الصّفة).

(21) وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً ** (Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah) Like their saying that the angels are the daughters of Allah, and that these are our intercessors with Allah. أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِآياتِهِ ** (or denies His signs?) Such as when they denied the Quran and the miracles and called them magic (سحراً). He only mentioned 'or' (أَوْ)—even though they had combined both matters—as an alert that each one of them alone reaches the utmost limit (غاية الافراط) in wrongdoing (الظّلم). **إِنَّهُ لا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** (Indeed, the wrongdoers will not be felicitous.) Let alone him than whom no one is a greater wrongdoer.

(٢٢) وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ (١) جَمِيعاً ** (22) And the day We shall muster them all It is in the accusative case (منصوب) due to an implied verb (بمضمر), for the purpose of magnifying the matter (تهويلاً للأمر). ثُمَّ نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا أَيْنَ شُرَكاؤُكُمُ ** then We shall say to those who associated partners [with Allah], 'Where are your partners It was said: meaning, your deities (آلهتكم) which you made partners (شركاء) with Allah, the Almighty (لله تعالى). And what has been narrated concerning this will come later, and that what is meant by them are their partners in authority (في الولاية). And it was read (وقرئ) as يَحْشُرُ and يَقُولُ with the yā (بالياءِ). الَّذِينَ كُنْتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ whom you used to claim?' Meaning: whom you used to claim them as partners (شركاء), [this is] a reprimand (توبيخ) to them due to their lack of benefit (بعدم انتفاعهم) from them.

(23) ثُمَّ لَمْ تَكُنْ فِتْنَتُهُمْ ** (Then their only plea was) In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means their excuse (معذرتهم).

I say: It means their excuse which they imagine will save them from the trial (فتنة) when it purifies them. And it was recited لَمْ تَكُنْ with the tā' (ت) and فِتْنَتُهُمْ in the nominative case (الرّفع), and with the yā' (ي) and the accusative case (النّصب): إِلَّا أَنْ قالُوا وَاللهِ رَبِّنا ما كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ ** (to say, 'By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists!'). They lie and swear by it, despite their knowledge that it will not benefit them due to the extremity of confusion (الحيرة) and astonishment (الدهشة). And رَبَّنَا was recited in the accusative case (النّصب).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him), and in Tafsir al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They mean the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (may the blessings and peace of Allah be upon him).

(24) انْظُرْ كَيْفَ كَذَبُوا عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** (24) Look how they lie against themselves, and what they used to fabricate has failed them. — referring to the partners (الشركاء).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in a hadith where he mentions the horrors of the Day of Resurrection: Then they will gather in another station and will be made to speak therein, and they will say: وَاللهِ رَبِّنا ما كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ ** (By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists.) These specifically are those who affirmed Monotheism (التّوحيد) in the worldly abode, but their faith in Allah, the Exalted, did not benefit them, along with their opposition to His Messengers, their doubt concerning what the Messengers brought from their Lord, their breaking of their covenants regarding their successors (أوصيائهم), and their exchanging الَّذِي هُوَ أَدْنى بِالَّذِي هُوَ خَيْرٌ ** (that which is inferior for that which is better). So Allah gave the lie to their claim of faith by His saying: انْظُرْ كَيْفَ كَذَبُوا عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ.

And al-Qummi, narrated in a truncated form (مقطوعاً), said: It refers to the Qadariyyah (قدريّة) (1) of this Ummah. Allah, the Exalted, will gather them on the Day of Resurrection along with the Sabeans (الصابئين), the Christians (النّصارى), and the Magians (المجوس). They will say: وَاللهِ رَبِّنا ما كُنَّا مُشْرِكِينَ (By Allah, our Lord, we were not polytheists.) Allah, the Exalted, will say: انْظُرْ كَيْفَ كَذَبُوا عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** (Look how they lie against themselves, and what they used to fabricate has failed them.) He said: And the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, every nation has Magians (مجوساً), and the Magians of this Ummah are those who say there is no Divine Decree (لا قَدَر) and claim that the Will (المشيئة) and the Power (القدرة) belong to them and are theirs."

(1) His saying وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ ** [and the day We shall gather them]—it is the object (مفعول به), and the implied meaning (التقدير) is: "And remember (واذكر*) يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ ** [the day We shall gather them]." And جَمِيعاً [all] is a circumstantial qualifier (حال) derived from the pronoun of the object. The two objects (مفعولا) of taz'umūn (تزعمون) [you claim] are omitted, meaning: you claim them to be partners (شركاء*), and what preceded indicates the omitted part.

(٢٥) وَمِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَسْتَمِعُ إِلَيْكَ (25) Among them are some who listen to you

**وَجَعَلْنا عَلى قُلُوبِهِمْ أَكِنَّةً ** (and We have put covers on their hearts)

covers (أغطية), the plural of kinān (كنان), which is that which conceals a thing.

**أَنْ يَفْقَهُوهُ ** (lest they understand it)

for fear that they might understand it (كراهة أن يفقهوه).

**وَفِي آذانِهِمْ وَقْراً ** (and a deafness in their ears)

which prevents them from listening to it, an allusion (كناية) to the aversion (نبوّ) of their hearts and ears from accepting it.

وَإِنْ يَرَوْا كُلَّ آيَةٍ لا يُؤْمِنُوا بِها (And even if they see every sign, they will not believe in it)

due to the extremity of their obstinacy (عنادهم) and the firmness of imitation (التقليد) within them.

حَتَّى إِذا جاؤُكَ يُجادِلُونَكَ (When they come to you to argue with you)

they contend with you (يخاصمونك).

يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هذا إِلَّا أَساطِيرُ الْأَوَّلِينَ (the faithless say, 'This is nothing but myths of the ancients.')

Al-Asātīr (الأساطير) means falsehoods (الأباطيل), and its origin is al-satr (السطر) meaning the line (الخطّ). The meaning is that their denial of the signs reached the point where they argue with you and dispute with you (ويناكرونك), and they make the Word of Allah, which is the most truthful discourse (أصدق الحديث), into the superstitions (خرافات) of the ancients, and this is the ultimate degree of denial.

**وَهُمْ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنْهُ وَيَنْأَوْنَ عَنْهُ ** (They forbid [others] from it, and keep away from it)

Al-Qummi said: Banu Hashim used to support the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and prevent Quraysh from harming him.

**وَيَنْأَوْنَ عَنْهُ ** (and keep away from it)

meaning, they distance themselves from it (يباعدونه) and do not believe in it.

**وَإِنْ يُهْلِكُونَ ** (and they destroy)

meaning, they do not destroy by that

**إِلَّا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَما يَشْعُرُونَ ** (except their own souls, and they are not aware)

that their harm does not extend beyond them to others.

(٢٧) وَلَوْ تَرى إِذْ وُقِفُوا عَلَى النَّارِ (27) Were you to see when they are made to stand before the Fire!

Its apodosis (جوابه) is omitted (محذوف), meaning: Were you to see them when they are made to stand before the Fire until they witness it, or when they look upon it by entering it, you would see a terrible matter (أمراً فظيعاً) (4). Al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning Banu Umayyah.

فَقالُوا يا لَيْتَنا نُرَدُّ Then they will say, 'Oh, would that we were sent back!

They wished that they would return to the world.

وَلا نُكَذِّبَ بِآياتِ رَبِّنا وَنَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ We would not deny the signs of our Lord and we would be among the faithful.'

It is conjoined (عطف) to نُرَدُّ (we are sent back), or it is the beginning of a new sentence (ابتداء كلام). And it was read with the nasb (accusative) case in both verbs, indicating a response (الجواب) by the omission (إضمار) of an (أن) after the waw (الواو), treating it like the fa' (الفاء), and also by the raf' (nominative) case for the first verb and the nasb (accusative) case for the second.

footnote (١) In the *Hadith*, *al-Qadariyyah* (*القدريّة*) are mentioned, and they are those attributed to *al-Qadar* (*القدر*). They claim that every servant is the creator of his own action, and they do not consider sins (*المعاصي*) and disbelief (*الكفر*) to be by the decree (*تقدير*) and will (*مشيّته*) of Allah. Thus, they were attributed to *al-Qadar* (*القدر*) because it is their innovation (*بدعتهم*) and misguidance (*ضلالتهم*). In *Sharh al-Mawāqif* (*شرح المواقف*), it is said that *al-Qadariyyah* (*القدريّة*) are *al-Mu'tazilah* (*المعتزلة*) because they attribute their actions to their own power (*قدرتهم*). In the *Hadith*: "A *Qadari* (*قدري*) will not enter Paradise," and he is the one who says: "What Allah wills does not occur, but what Iblis wills occurs."

footnote (٢) *Al-Waqr* (*الوقر*) with *fathah* (*بالفتح*) is heaviness in the ear (*الثّقل في الإذن*).

footnote (٣) *Nabā al-sayf* (*نبا السّيف*) (the sword turned back) from the category of *qatl* (*قتل*), *nabw* (*نبّوا*) on the pattern of *fu'ūl* (*فعول*): it turned back and returned without cutting.

footnote (٤) *Faẓu'a al-amr* (*فظع الأمر*) with *ḍammah* (*بالضمّ*), so it is *faẓā'ah* (*فظاعة*), and thus *faẓī'* (*فظيع*), meaning severe (*شديد*), heinous (*شنيع*), exceeding the measure (*جاوزا المقدار*).

(٢٨) **بَلْ بَدا لَهُمْ ما كانُوا يُخْفُونَ مِنْ قَبْلُ وَلَوْ رُدُّوا لَعادُوا لِما نُهُوا عَنْهُ وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ ** (28) Rather, what they used to conceal before has become manifest to them. Were they to be sent back, they would revert to what they were forbidden, and they are indeed liars.

(This refers to) their hypocrisy and the vices (قبايح) of their deeds. So they wished what they wished out of distress (ضجراً), not out of resolve (عزماً) that if they were returned, they would believe.

وَلَوْ رُدُّوا ** Were they to be sent back,** meaning: to the world after standing (for judgment) and the manifestation (of truth),

لَعادُوا لِما نُهُوا عَنْهُ ** they would revert to what they were forbidden, namely, disbelief (الكفر) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي).

وَإِنَّهُمْ لَكاذِبُونَ ** and they are indeed liars.** regarding what they promised themselves; they would not fulfill it.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that they are fundamentally (في الأصل) cursed.

(٢٩) وَقالُوا إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا حَياتُنَا الدُّنْيا وَما نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ (٢٩) (29) And they say, 'There is nothing but the life of this world, and we shall not be resurrected.'

وَقالُوا ** is an ʿaṭf (عطف) upon لَعادُوا ** or it is a new beginning (ابتداء). إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا حَياتُنَا الدُّنْيا ** The pronoun (الضّمير) refers to life (الحيوة). وَما نَحْنُ بِمَبْعُوثِينَ.**

(٣٠) وَلَوْ تَرى إِذْ وُقِفُوا عَلى رَبِّهِمْ قالَ أَلَيْسَ هذا بِالْحَقِّ قالُوا بَلى وَرَبِّنا قالَ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (٣٠) (30) Were you to see when they are made to stand before their Lord! He will say, 'Is this not the truth?' They will say, 'Yes, by our Lord!' He will say, 'Taste the punishment because of what you used to disbelieve.'

وَلَوْ تَرى إِذْ وُقِفُوا عَلى رَبِّهِمْ ** [They are made to stand] for rebuke (للتوبيخ) and questioning (والسّؤال), just as the offending slave (العبد الجاني) is made to stand before his master (مولاه). This is a metaphor (كناية) for their being informed (اطّلاعهم) of the Lord and His recompense (جزائه). And al-wuqūf (الوقوف) means being informed (الإِطّلاع). قالَ أَلَيْسَ هذا بِالْحَقِ ** This is a reproach (تعيير) from Allah to them for their denial (تكذيبهم) of the Resurrection (البعث). قالُوا بَلى وَرَبِّنا They affirmed (اقّروا) and confirmed (وأكدّوا) with an oath (باليمين) because the matter had become utterly clear (لانجلاءِ الأمر غاية الجلاءِ). قالَ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذابَ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ ** Due to (بسبب) your disbelief (كفركم).

(٣١) قَدْ خَسِرَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِلِقاءِ اللهِ ** (31) Certainly lost are those who deny the encounter with Allah. (It means) by reaching the Hereafter and what is connected to it of recompense (الجزاء), since the bliss (النّعيم) has escaped them and they have incurred the everlasting punishment (العذاب المقيم). حَتَّى إِذا جاءَتْهُمُ السَّاعَةُ ** This is a limit (غاية) for "they deny" (كَذَّبُوا), not for "they lost" (خَسِرَ), because their loss (خسرانهم) has no limit (غاية). بَغْتَةً ** abruptly (فجأة). قالُوا يا حَسْرَتَنا ** they will say, 'O, our regret, meaning: "Come forth, for this is your time (أوانُك)." عَلى ما فَرَّطْنا ** We fell short (قصّرنا) in it. It is said: meaning, in the world (الدّنيا), even though it was not mentioned, due to the knowledge of it; or in the Hour (السّاعة), meaning concerning its matter (شأنها) and belief (الإِيمان) in it; or in Paradise (الجنّة), meaning in seeking it and working for it, due to what was narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله concerning this Ayah: The people of the Fire will see their stations (منازلهم) in Paradise, and they will say: يا حَسْرَتَنا (O, our regret). وَهُمْ يَحْمِلُونَ أَوْزارَهُمْ عَلى ظُهُورِهِمْ ** This is a representation (تمثيل) of their deserving the burdens (آضار) (1) of sins (الآثام). أَلا ساءَ ما يَزِرُونَ ** Behold! Evil is what they bear! How evil is the thing they bear (يزرونه): their burden (وزرهم).

(1) الوَضَرُ (with vowelization) is dirt (الدّرن) and grease (الدّسم). It is said: وضرت القصعة (the bowl became greasy), meaning it became greasy (دسمت). And وضره وضراً (he made it dirty), so he is وضِر (dirty), similar in pattern (وزناً) and meaning (معنىً) to وسخ وسخاً فهو وسخ (he made it dirty, so he is dirty). Based on this, الآضار is the plural of وضر.

(٣٢) وَمَا الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا إِلَّا لَعِبٌ وَلَهْوٌ ** (32) The life of this world is nothing but play and diversion And its deeds are nothing but play and diversion which distracts people and preoccupies them from that which results in lasting benefit and true pleasure. And this is the answer to their saying: إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا حَياتُنَا الدُّنْيا وَلَلدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ** (It is nothing but the life of this world [that we live]. But the abode of the Hereafter is better for those who are Godwary) due to its permanence and the eternity of its pleasures and benefits. And it was read: ولدار الآخِرة. أَفَلا يَعْقِلُونَ ** (Do they not exercise their reason?) Which of the two matters is better? And it was read in the second person address (على الخطاب).

(٣٣) قَدْ نَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُ لَيَحْزُنُكَ الَّذِي يَقُولُونَ فَإِنَّهُمْ لا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ ** (33) We certainly know that what they say grieves you. Yet it is not you that they impugn**

in reality, وَلكِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ بِآياتِ اللهِ يَجْحَدُونَ (but the wrongdoers impugn the signs of Allah). But they deny (يجحدون) the signs of Allah and deny Him (يكذّبونه), and the bāʾ (الباء) is due to the denial (الجحود) containing the meaning of rejection (التكذيب). And it was read with takhfīf (بالتخفيف) (lightened form, يُكْذِبُونَكَ) from (أكذبه) if he found him to be a liar or attributed lying to him.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): A man recited before the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): فَإِنَّهُمْ لا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ ** (Yet it is not you that they impugn). He (peace be upon him) said to me: "By Allah, they have certainly denied Him (كذّبوه) with the severest denial (التّكذيب). But it is read with takhfīf (مخففة), meaning: they do not find you a liar (لا يكذبونك) and they do not bring forth falsehood (باطل) by which they deny (يكذبون) your truth (حقّك)."

And al-Qummi attributed it to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), except that he said: "They do not bring forth a truth (حقّ) by which they invalidate your truth (حقّك)." And this supports the establishment of the denial (التّكذيب).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Meaning: they are not able to invalidate your saying.

And in al-Majma, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): That He used to read (لا يكذّبونك) (with takhfīf) and say that the intended meaning is that they do not bring forth a truth (حقّ) more truthful than your truth (حقّك).

And in it (al-Majma), narrated from most of the exegetes (المفسّرين): يُكَذِّبُونَكَ ** (they impugn you) is with their hearts, as a belief (اعتقاداً). He said: And what supports this is what was narrated: That the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم met Abu Jahl and shook his hand. He was asked about that, and he said: "By Allah, I certainly know that he is truthful (صادق), but when were we ever followers (تبعاً) of Abd Manaf?" So Allah the Almighty revealed الْآيَةَ.** (the Ayah).

(٣٤) وَلَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (34) And certainly apostles were denied before you. (This is) a consolation for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. فَصَبَرُوا عَلى ما كُذِّبُوا وَأُوذُوا حَتَّى أَتاهُمْ نَصْرُنا ** So they were patient regarding what they were denied, and they were tormented until Our help came to them.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Indeed, whoever is patient, he is patient for a short time, and whoever is anxious (جَزِعَ), he is anxious for a short time." Then he said: "And upon you is patience in all your affairs, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and commanded him to have patience and gentleness (الرّفق)." He (peace be upon him) said: "So he was patient until they afflicted him (1) with great calamities (بالعظائم) and cast them upon him, and his breast became constricted. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ بِما يَقُولُونَ فَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ وَكُنْ مِنَ السَّاجِدِينَ ** And We certainly know that your breast is constrained by what they say. So celebrate the praise of your Lord, and be among those who prostrate. Then they denied him and cast accusations upon him, and he grieved because of that. So Allah revealed: قَدْ نَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُ لَيَحْزُنُكَ الَّذِي يَقُولُونَ فَإِنَّهُمْ لا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ وَلكِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ بِآياتِ اللهِ يَجْحَدُونَ وَلَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فَصَبَرُوا عَلى ما كُذِّبُوا وَأُوذُوا حَتَّى أَتاهُمْ نَصْرُنا We certainly know that what they say grieves you. Yet it is not you that they deny, but the wrongdoers impugn the signs of Allah. And certainly apostles were denied before you. So they were patient regarding what they were denied, and they were tormented until Our help came to them. So the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم committed himself to patience—the rest of the narration (الحديث).

And al-Qummi narrated from him (peace be upon him) what is close to it.

وَلا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِماتِ اللهِ ** And none can change the words of Allah. It was said: meaning His promises, from His saying: وَلَقَدْ سَبَقَتْ كَلِمَتُنا لِعِبادِنَا الْمُرْسَلِينَ إِنَّهُمْ لَهُمُ الْمَنْصُورُونَ And Our word has certainly gone forth for Our servants, the Messengers: they will indeed be assisted.**

وَلَقَدْ جاءَكَ مِنْ نَبَإِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** And certainly there has come to you some news of the apostles** —from their stories and what they endured (2) from their people.

(٣٥) وَإِنْ كانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ (And if their turning away is hard on you) — meaning, it became great (عظم) and difficult (وشقّ) [for you]. إِعْراضُهُمْ (their turning away) — from you and from the faith which you brought.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم loved the conversion to Islam of Al-Harith ibn Nawfal ibn Abd Manaf. He invited him and strove hard for him to embrace Islam, but wretchedness (الشّقاءِ) overcame him. This was difficult (فشقّ ذلك) for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, so Allah revealed this verse: فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَبْتَغِيَ نَفَقاً فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (then if you can seek a tunnel in the earth) — meaning, an opening (منفذاً) through which you penetrate (تنفذ فيه) into the depths of the earth. أَوْ سُلَّماً فِي السَّماءِ (or a ladder into the sky) — meaning, or an ascent (مصعداً) by which you ascend to the sky. فَتَأْتِيَهُمْ بِآيَةٍ ** (and bring them a sign) — so that you bring forth for them a sign from the earth or bring down a sign from the sky, by which they would believe. And its apodosis (وجوابه) is omitted, meaning: then do it (فافعل). And this sentence is the apodosis of the first condition (الشرط الأوّل). The intent is to show His extreme eagerness (حرصه البالغ) for the faith of His people, and that if He were able to do that, He would do it, but He is not able. Its parallel is: فَلَعَلَّكَ باخِعٌ (٣) نَفْسَكَ (Perhaps you will kill (3) yourself). وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَجَمَعَهُمْ عَلَى الْهُدى ** (Had Allah wished, He would have gathered them to guidance) — by bringing them a sign to which they would submit, but He does not do so because it deviates from wisdom (لخروجه عن الحكمة).

In al-Ikmal, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "O Ali, Allah has decreed separation (الفرقة) and disagreement (الإِختلاف) upon this nation. وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَجَمَعَهُمْ عَلَى الْهُدى ** (Had Allah wished, He would have gathered them to guidance) so that no two people from this nation would disagree, nor dispute concerning any matter of His command, nor would the inferior (المفضول) deny the merit (فضله) of the one possessing merit (لذي الفضل)." فَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْجاهِلِينَ.** (So do not be one of the ignorant.)

Al-Qummi [said]: The address is to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, but the intended meaning (والمعنيّ) is the people.

(١) His saying: they attained Him with great things (نالوه بالعظائم) means they attributed to Him lying, madness, sorcery, and other things, and they fabricated lies against Him. (٢) Al-Kabad (الكَبَد) with vowel movement (بالتحريك): hardship (الشدّة) and difficulty (المشقّة) derived from enduring (المكابدة) something, which is bearing hardships in a matter. (٣) Meaning: you kill yourself (قاتل نفسك) out of grief (بالغمّ) and sorrow (والوجد) for them.

(٣٦) إِنَّما يَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ يَسْمَعُونَ ** (Only those respond who listen) with understanding and reflection (بتفهم وتدبّر). Meaning that those whose faith you are keen upon are in the position of the dead who do not hear. وَالْمَوْتى يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ (And the dead, Allah will resurrect them) and He will judge concerning them. ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ ** (then they will be returned to Him). So at that time they will hear, but as for before that, there is no path to their hearing.

(٣٧) وَقالُوا لَوْ لا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ آيَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (37) And they say, 'Why has not a sign been sent down to him from his Lord?'**

From what they proposed, they abandoned considering what was revealed to him of Allah's signs and miracles, despite their abundance, as if nothing of the signs had been revealed to him, due to their obstinacy. قُلْ إِنَّ اللهَ قادِرٌ عَلى أَنْ يُنَزِّلَ آيَةً ** Say, 'Indeed Allah is able to send down a sign,' so that they submit to it. And it was read an yunzila with takhfif (التّخفيف). وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لا يَعْلَمُونَ** (but most of them do not know) that He is capable of it, and that His wisdom does not necessitate that.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: They do not know that if the sign comes and they do not believe in it, they would perish. And from al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding this verse: He will show you signs at the end of time, among them the Beast of the Earth (دابّة الأرض), the Dajjal (الدّجّال), the descent of Jesus son of Mary, and the rising of the sun from its setting place.

(٣٨) وَما مِنْ دَابَّةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** that creeps upon its surface, وَلا طائِرٍ يَطِيرُ بِجَناحَيْهِ ** in the air. It was said that He described it thus to definitively exclude the metaphor of speed and the like. إِلَّا أُمَمٌ أَمْثالُكُمْ ** whose conditions are preserved, whose provisions are decreed, whose appointed times are written, whose bodies are created, whose souls are sustained, just as you are likewise. (38) There is no animal on the earth nor a bird flying with its two wings, but [they are] communities like yourselves.

Al-Qummi said: It means created like you. He said: And He said that everything that He created is created like you.

It was said that the purpose of this is to indicate the perfection of His power, the comprehensiveness of His knowledge, and the vastness of His governance (تدبيره), and to serve as proof that He is capable of sending down a sign. ما فَرَّطْنا فِي الْكِتابِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** (meaning) anything of omission, because (فَرَّطَ) does not take an object directly (بِنَفْسِهِ), but here it is made transitive using (في) to refer to the Book (الكتاب). And it was read with the lightened form (بالتخفيف). (We have not omitted anything from the Book.)

And by the Book (الكتاب) He means the Quran (القرآن), as is understood from many narrations, such as the narration concerning the disagreement of scholars in issuing legal rulings (الفتيا) found in Nahj al-Balagha, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), where He said: "Did Allah send down an incomplete religion, so He sought their help in completing it? Or were they partners with Him, so it is for them to speak and for Him to be pleased? Or did Allah send down a complete religion, but the Messenger fell short in conveying and delivering it? Yet Allah, the Glorified, says: ما فَرَّطْنا فِي الْكِتابِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ and **تِبْياناً لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** (as a clarification of all things)."

And the narration describing the Imamate (الإِمامة) from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) in al-Uyun and other sources: The people were ignorant and deceived regarding their religions. Indeed, Allah did not take His Prophet until He had perfected the religion and sent down the Quran upon him, in which is the detailed explanation of everything. He clarified therein the lawful (الحلال) and the unlawful (الحرام), the prescribed punishments (الحدود), and the rulings (الأحكام), and gathered completely everything that is needed. So, He, the Mighty and Majestic, said: **ما فَرَّطْنا فِي الْكِتابِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ثُمَّ إِلى رَبِّهِمْ يُحْشَرُونَ ** (We have not omitted anything from the Book. Then they will be gathered toward their Lord.)

Meaning all the communities. In al-Faqih, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace and blessings be upon him): Any camel upon which the pilgrimage (حجّ) was performed for three years will be made from the livestock (نَعَمِ) of Paradise. He said: And it was narrated: seven years.

And therein (in al-Faqih): The Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) saw a she-camel tied up with its saddle (جهازها) upon it. He said: "Where is its owner? Command him to prepare himself for the dispute tomorrow."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from the Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) in the narration concerning the Day of Resurrection (القيامة), He said: "None shall ride on that Day except four: I, and Ali, and Fatima, and Salih, the Prophet of Allah. As for me, I shall be upon al-Buraq. As for my daughter Fatima, she shall be upon my she-camel al-Adhba. As for Salih, he shall be upon the She-Camel of Allah (ناقة الله) which was hamstrung. And as for Ali, he shall be upon a she-camel of light, whose reins are of ruby (ياقوت), and upon Him are two green garments (حلّتان خضراوان)."

(39) وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا صُمٌ ** (39) And those who deny Our signs are deaf concerning guidance, وَبُكْمٌ ** and dumb they do not speak with good, فِي الظُّلُماتِ ** in the darkness meaning the darknesses of disbelief (ظلمات الكفر). Thus did al-Qummi narrate it from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in the interpretation of the Ayah. مَنْ يَشَأِ اللهُ يُضْلِلْهُ ** Whomever Allah wills, He leads astray He forsakes him, so he goes astray, because he is not among the people of guidance. وَمَنْ يَشَأْ يَجْعَلْهُ عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** and whomever He wills, He sets him on a straight path. He guides him to guidance through His grace (لطفه), because he is among the people of guidance and grace (اللّطف).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning those who denied the Successors (الأوصياء). They are صُمٌّ وَبُكْمٌ ** (deaf and dumb), as Allah said, فِي الظُّلُماتِ ** (in the darkness). Whoever is from the progeny of Iblis, he will never affirm the Successors (الأوصياء) nor believe in them, and they are those whom Allah has led astray. And whoever is from the progeny of Adam believes in the Successors (الأوصياء), and they are عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ (on a straight path).

(٤٠) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتَكُمْ ** (40) Say, 'Tell me, The meaning of أرأيت أنفسكم is "inform me" (أخبروني). إِنْ أَتاكُمْ عَذابُ اللهِ ** if Allah's punishment comes upon you in this world (الدّنيا), أَوْ أَتَتْكُمُ السَّاعَةُ ** or the Hour overtakes you, meaning the Resurrection (القيامة). Whom do you call upon? أَغَيْرَ اللهِ تَدْعُونَ Do you call on other than Allah? (This is) a rebuke (تبكيت) for them. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** if you are truthful that the idols are deities (آلهة).

(٤١) بَلْ إِيَّاهُ تَدْعُونَ ** (41) Nay, Him you invoke) Rather, you single out Allah with the invocation (بالدعاء) without the deities (دون الآلهة). فَيَكْشِفُ ما تَدْعُونَ (and He removes what you invoke) إِلَيْهِ ** (Him) What you invoke for its removal (إلى كشفه). إِنْ شاءَ ** (if He wishes) That He bestow His favor upon you by removing it (بكشفه). وَتَنْسَوْنَ ما تُشْرِكُونَ ** (and you forget what you associate [with Him]) And you abandon your deities (آلهتكم) because of what is established (ركز) in the intellects (في العقول): that He is the One capable of removing the harm (كشف الضرّ) without anyone else (دون غيره). Or, you do not remember them (لا تذكرونها) at that time due to the severity of the matter (من شدّة الأمر), and He is the One to Whom it belongs (وهو له).

(٤٢) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا إِلى أُمَمٍ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ ** (We have certainly sent [apostles] to nations before you) meaning the Messengers (الرّسل), but they denied them. فَأَخَذْناهُمْ بِالْبَأْساءِ ** (then We seized them with distress) with hardship (بالشدّة) and poverty (والفقر). وَالضَّرَّاءِ ** (and affliction) and illness (والمرض), and the decrease of lives (الأنفس) and wealth (والأموال). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَضَرَّعُونَ ** (so that they might entreat [Us].) so that they might entreat (يتضرّعوا), submit (ويخضعُوا), and humble themselves (ويتذلّلوا), or repent (يتوبُوا) from their sins (ذنوبهم).

(٤٣) **فَلَوْ لا إِذْ جاءَهُمْ بَأْسُنا تَضَرَّعُوا وَلكِنْ قَسَتْ قُلُوبُهُمْ وَزَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الشَّيْطانُ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (43) So why did they not supplicate humbly when Our might overtook them? But their hearts hardened, and Satan made what they used to do seem good to them.

Its meaning is the negation of their humble supplication (تَضَرُّع) at that time. The particle لَوْ لَا (why not) was used to indicate that they had no excuse for abandoning humble supplication (التَّضَرُّع) except for their obstinacy (عِنَاد), the hardening of their hearts (قَسْوَة قُلُوب), and their admiration (إِعْجَاب) for their deeds which Satan (الشَّيْطَان) made fair-seeming to them.

In Nahj al-Balagha, from his (the Commander of the Faithful's) speech: "And if the people, when afflictions (النِّقَم) descend upon them and blessings (النِّعَم) depart from them, were to turn to their Lord with sincerity (صِدْق) in their intentions (نِيَّات) and yearning (وَلَه) in their hearts (قُلُوب), He would restore to them every stray thing (شَارِد) and rectify for them every corrupt thing (فَاسِد)."

(٤٤) فَلَمَّا نَسُوا ما ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ ** (44) So when they forgot what they were reminded of, (from hardship (الْبَأْسَاءِ) and distress (الضَّرَّاءِ)), meaning they abandoned taking heed of it, فَتَحْنا عَلَيْهِمْ أَبْوابَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ (We opened upon them the gates of everything) (from health and expansion in provision). And it was read فَتَّحْنا with تَشْدِيد wherever it occurred. حَتَّى إِذا فَرِحُوا بِما أُوتُوا ** (until, when they rejoiced in what they were given) (from goodness and blessings, and they became preoccupied with the blessings instead of the Benefactor), أَخَذْناهُمْ بَغْتَةً (We seized them suddenly)—a surprise from where they did not perceive—فَإِذا هُمْ مُبْلِسُونَ (then behold, they were utterly despairing)—despairing of salvation and mercy, regretful (مُتَحَسِّرُون).

(٤٥) فَقُطِعَ دابِرُ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا (So the last remnant of the wrongdoing lot was cut off) meaning, their last remnant (آخرهم). Not one of them was left, from his rear (دبره), when He pursued him. وَالْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (and all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.) [Praise belongs to Him] for the destruction of His enemies and the exaltation of His Word (كلمته). For, the deliverance of the people of the earth from the evil beliefs of the disbelievers and the ugly deeds of the disobedient and the wicked is a great favor (نقمة جليلة) for which it is right that He be praised.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "If you see Allah, the Exalted, giving to the disobedient (العاصي), then that is a gradual enticement (استدراج) from Him." Then He recited this Ayah.

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام: "O son of Adam, if you see your Lord following up His favors upon you, then beware of Him."

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام regarding: فَلَمَّا نَسُوا ما ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ (So when they forgot what they had been reminded of) He said: meaning, when they abandoned the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام, though they had been commanded concerning it. فَتَحْنا عَلَيْهِمْ أَبْوابَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ (We opened to them the gates of everything) [meaning] their dominion (دولتهم) in the world and what was extended to them therein. أَخَذْناهُمْ بَغْتَةً (We seized them suddenly). He means by this the rising (قيام) of the Qa'im صلوات الله عليه, until it is as if they never had any authority (سلطان) at all.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from him عليه السلام: When they abandoned the Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali صلوات الله عليه, though they had been commanded concerning it, regarding the Ayah أَخَذْناهُمْ بَغْتَةً (We seized them suddenly), he said: It was revealed concerning the children of al-Abbas (ولد العباس).

(٤٦) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَخَذَ اللهُ سَمْعَكُمْ وَأَبْصارَكُمْ ** (Say, 'Tell me, if Allah takes away your hearing and your sight) by making you deaf and blind, وَخَتَمَ عَلى قُلُوبِكُمْ by covering them with that which removes your intellect and strips away your discernment, مَنْ إِلهٌ غَيْرُ اللهِ يَأْتِيكُمْ بِهِ ** (and seals your hearts, what god other than Allah will bring it back to you?') with that (i.e., the hearing, sight, and intellect).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding إِنْ أَخَذَ اللهُ : (If Allah takes away) from you the guidance. انْظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآياتِ ثُمَّ هُمْ يَصْدِفُونَ ** (See how We elaborate the signs, yet they turn away.) He said: they turn away (يُعْرِضُونَ).

(٤٧) قُلْ أَرَأَيْتَكُمْ إِنْ أَتاكُمْ عَذابُ اللهِ بَغْتَةً ** (47) Say, 'Tell me, should Allah's punishment overtake you suddenly (without a prelude or the appearance of a sign) أَوْ جَهْرَةً بتقدم امارة قابل البغتة بالجهرة لما في البغتة من معنى الخفية هَلْ يُهْلَكُ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** or openly (جَهْرَةً), with the precedence of a sign (امارة). He contrasted البغتة (suddenness) with الجهرة (openness) because البغتة contains the meaning of hiddenness (الخفية). will anyone be destroyed except the wrongdoing lot?' No destruction (هلاك) of punishment (تعذيب) and wrath (سخط) occurs except for those who committed wrong (ظلموا) through their disbelief (كفرهم) and corruption (فسادهم).

Al-Qummi (narrates): It was revealed when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم migrated to Medina and hardship (الجهد), ailments (العلل), and sickness (المرض) afflicted his Companions. They complained of this to him. (He means) that nothing afflicts you except hardship (الجهد) and harm (الضّرّ) in this world (الدّنيا). As for the painful punishment (العذاب الأليم), which is destruction (الهلاك), it afflicts none except the wrongdoing lot (القوم الظّالمين).

Al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: The Banu Umayyah were seized بَغْتَةً ** (suddenly) and the Banu Abbas جَهْرَةً.** (openly).

(٤٨) وَما نُرْسِلُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرِينَ ** (48) (We do not send the apostles except as bearers of good news) to the believers (المؤمنين) with Paradise (الجنّة), وَمُنْذِرِينَ ** (and warners) to the disbelievers (الكافرينَ) with the Fire (النار). فَمَنْ آمَنَ وَأَصْلَحَ فَلا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ ** (So whoever believes and mends his ways, no fear shall befall them) from the punishment (العذاب), وَلا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ ** (nor shall they grieve) due to the loss of the reward (الثواب).

(٤٩) وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا يَمَسُّهُمُ الْعَذابُ ** (49) But those who deny Our signs, the punishment shall befall them He made the punishment (العذاب) touch them, as if it is the seeker (الطّالب) striving to reach them, doing with them what it wills. بِما كانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ ** (because of their committing transgression) due to their departure (خروجه) from affirmation (التّصديق) and obedience (الطّاعة). (٥٠) قُلْ لا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ عِنْدِي خَزائِنُ اللهِ ** (50) Say, ‘I do not tell you that I possess the treasuries of Allah, In al-Tawhid, al-Ma'ani, and al-Majalis, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Moses (upon our Prophet and his Progeny and upon him be peace) ascended Mount Tur and called upon his Lord, the Mighty and Majestic, he said: "O Lord, show me Your treasuries (خزائنك)." So, the Almighty said: "O Moses, My treasuries are only that when I desire a thing, I say to it: 'Be,' and it is (كُنْ فَيَكُونُ)." وَلا أَعْلَمُ الْغَيْبَ ** nor do I know the Unseen, which Allah has reserved exclusively for His knowledge, and I only know of it what Allah teaches me. وَلا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ إِنِّي مَلَكٌ ** nor do I say to you that I am an angel. of the genus of angels (الملائكة) who can do what they are capable of doing. إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا ما يُوحى إِلَيَ (I only follow what is revealed to me). I do not inform you of what was and what will be except through revelation (الوحي). He disavowed the claim of divinity (الألوهية) and angelhood (الملكيّة) and claimed prophethood (النّبوّة), which is among the perfections (كمالات) of humankind, in refutation of their deeming His claim improbable and their certainty regarding the falsehood of His assertion.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked one day, while a group of his companions had gathered around him, and they were disputing over two differing narrations (الحديثين المختلفين) from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم concerning a single matter. He (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden what is forbidden (حرّم حراماً), made lawful what is lawful (أحلّ حلالاً), and obligated duties (فرض فرائض). So, whatever comes regarding the making lawful of what Allah has forbidden, or the making forbidden of what Allah has made lawful, or the removal of an obligatory duty (فريضة) in the Book of Allah whose ruling (رسمها) is established without abrogation (نسخ), then that is something which is not permissible to adopt. This is because the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would not forbid what Allah has made lawful, nor make lawful what Allah has forbidden, nor change the obligatory duties (فرائض) and rulings (أحكامه) of Allah. In all of that, he was a follower (متّبعاً), one who submitted (مسلّماً), and one who conveyed (مؤدِّياً) from Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا ما يُوحى إِلَيَ ** (I only follow what is revealed to me). Thus, he was a follower of Allah, conveying from Allah what He commanded regarding the delivery of the message (تبليغ الرّسالة)." قُلْ هَلْ يَسْتَوِي الْأَعْمى وَالْبَصِيرُ ** Say, ‘Are the blind and the sighted equal? It is said: the misguided (الضّالّ) and the guided (المهتدي). And al-Qummi said: He who does not know (من لا يعلم) and he who knows (ومن يعلم). And in al-Majma', he attributed it to the People of the House (peace be upon them). أَفَلا تَتَفَكَّرُونَ ** Will you not then reflect?’ So that you do not become misguided (ضالّين), resembling the blind, and that you may be fair (تنصفوا) to yourselves.

(٥١) وَأَنْذِرْ بِهِ الَّذِينَ يَخافُونَ أَنْ يُحْشَرُوا إِلى رَبِّهِمْ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِهِ وَلِيٌّ وَلا شَفِيعٌ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ. (51) And warn with it those who fear that they will be mustered toward their Lord, having no protector or intercessor besides Him, so that they may be Godwary.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): وَأَنْذِرْ ** (And warn) means: by the Qur'an, those who hope for arrival (الوصول) to their Lord. You encourage them in what is with Him, for indeed the Qur'an is an intercessor whose intercession is accepted (شافع مشفّع).

(52) وَلا تَطْرُدِ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ بِالْغَداةِ وَالْعَشِيِ ** (52) And do not drive away those who supplicate their Lord morning and evening, meaning, they repeat it constantly (على الدّوام). يُرِيدُونَ وَجْهَهُ ** seeking His Face. meaning, they seek His pleasure (مرضاته), being sincere (مخلصين) to Him. And it was recited (وقرئ) as بالغَدْوة. ما عَلَيْكَ مِنْ حِسابِهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ وَما مِنْ حِسابِكَ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ فَتَطْرُدَهُمْ ** You are not accountable for them in any way, nor are they accountable for you in any way, that you should drive them away This is the answer (جواب) to the negation (النّفي). فَتَكُونَ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** and thus become one of the wrongdoers. This is the answer (جواب) to the prohibition (النّهي).

Al-Qummi said: The reason (سَبَب) for its revelation (نزولها) was that there was a group of poor believers in Medina called the People of the Bench (أصحاب الصّفّة). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم commanded them to be in a bench area (صفّة) where they would take shelter. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would personally look after them (يتعهدهم) and sometimes carry to them what they would eat. They would frequent the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and He would bring them close, sit with them, and comfort them (يؤنسهم). When the wealthy (الأغنياء) and the affluent (المترفون) among His Companions came, they would disapprove of that and say, "Drive them away from you." So, one day, a man from the Ansar came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while a man from the Companions of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, one of the People of the Bench (أصحاب الصّفّة), was with Him, clinging close to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the Messenger of Allah was speaking to him. The Ansari sat far away from them both. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "Come closer." But he did not do so. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: "Perhaps you feared that his poverty might cling to you." The Ansari said: "Drive these away from you." So Allah revealed: **وَلا تَطْرُدِ الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ رَبَّهُمْ ** the Ayah.

(٥٣) وَكَذلِكَ ** Like that (مثل ذلك)—the trials (الفتن), which is the variation in the conditions of people regarding worldly matters—فَتَنَّا ** We tested (ابتلينا) بَعْضَهُمْ بِبَعْضٍ ** some of them by means of others, regarding the matter of religion. So We preferred these weak ones over the nobles of Quraysh by their precedence in faith (الإِيمان), لِيَقُولُوا أَهؤُلاءِ مَنَّ اللهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنْ بَيْنِنا ** so that they might say: 'Are these the ones whom Allah has favored from among us?' Meaning: Are these the ones whom Allah has favored with guidance (الهداية) and success (التوفيق) in what makes him happy, to the exclusion of us, while we are the great ones (الأكابر) and the leaders (الرؤساء), and they are the poor (المساكين) and the weak (الضعفاء)? And this is a denial that He should single out these individuals from among them for attaining the truth (الحقّ) and preceding others to goodness (الخير), like their saying: لَوْ كانَ خَيْراً ما سَبَقُونا إِلَيْهِ ** (had it been any good, they would not have preceded us to it). And the lām (اللام) [in لِيَقُولُوا] is for the consequence (للعاقبة). أَلَيْسَ اللهُ بِأَعْلَمَ بِالشَّاكِرِينَ ** (Is not Allah the best knower of the grateful ones?) [He is the best knower] of those from whom faith (الإِيمان) and gratitude (الشكر) proceed, so He grants him success (يوفّقه), and of those from whom it does not proceed, so He abandons him (يخذله).

(٥٤) وَإِذا جاءَكَ الَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِآياتِنا فَقُلْ سَلامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ كَتَبَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلى نَفْسِهِ الرَّحْمَةَ (54) When those who have faith in Our signs come to you, say, 'Peace be to you! Your Lord has prescribed mercy for Himself.'

It was said that it was revealed concerning those whom Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, forbade His Prophet from driving away. And the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, whenever He saw them, would initiate the greeting of peace (السّلام) to them. And it was said that it was revealed concerning Hamzah, Ja'far, Ammar, Mus'ab ibn Umayr, and others.

And it was said that a group came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and said, "Indeed, we have committed many sins," so He remained silent regarding them, and then it was revealed.

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that it was revealed concerning the repentant ones (التائبين).

And the remainder of the Ayah supports this, and there is no contradiction between the narrations (الرّوايات). أَنَّهُ (with dammah on the hamzah) is a new clause (استيناف) that explains الرَّحْمَةَ. It was also read with fathah (أَنَّهُ) as a substitute (البدل) for it. مَنْ عَمِلَ مِنْكُمْ سُوءاً بِجَهالَةٍ ثُمَّ تابَ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَأَصْلَحَ (Whoever among you commits an evil out of ignorance, and then repents after that and reforms) by making amends (بالتدارك) فَأَنَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ (then He is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful.) And it was read with fathah (فَأَنَّهُ).

(٥٥) وَكَذلِكَ ** (And thus) And similar to that clear elaboration (التفصيل الواضح), نُفَصِّلُ الْآياتِ ** (We elaborate the signs) (We elaborate) the verses (آيات) of the Quran regarding the description (صفة) of the obedient (المطيعين) and the criminals (المجرمين), those who persist (in sin) (المصرّين) among them, and the repentant ones (الأوّابين), وَلِتَسْتَبِينَ سَبِيلُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (and so that the way of the guilty may become distinct.) It was recited with the tā' (التّاءِ) and the nasb (accusative) of al-Sabīl (السّبيل) based on the address (الخطاب), and with the yā' (الياءِ) and the raf' (nominative) of it.

(٥٦) قُلْ إِنِّي نُهِيتُ ** (56) Say, 'I have been forbidden I was turned away and warned by what was established for me of proofs and what was sent down to me of signs concerning the matter of Monotheism (التوحيد) أَنْ أَعْبُدَ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ ** to worship those whom you invoke (meaning) you worship مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ قُلْ لا أَتَّبِعُ أَهْواءَكُمْ ** besides Allah.' Say, 'I will not follow your desires. An affirmation to cut off their hopes and an indication of the reason (الموجب) for the prohibition and the cause (علّة) of refusing to follow them, and declaring them ignorant, and a clarification of the origin (مبدأ) of their error, and that what they are upon is mere desire (هوىً) and is not guidance (هدىً), and an alert for whoever seeks the truth (الحقّ) to follow the proof (الحجّة) and not to imitate (يقلّد). قَدْ ضَلَلْتُ إِذاً If I did, I would be astray Meaning: If I were to follow your desires, I would indeed be astray. وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُهْتَدِينَ and I would not be one of the guided.' Meaning: in any aspect of guidance (الهدى) so that I might be counted among them. And in this is an allusion that they are likewise.

(٥٧) قُلْ إِنِّي عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ upon a clear proof (حجة واضِحة) مِنْ رَبِّي from the knowledge of my Lord (معرفة ربّي), and that there is no deity worshipped besides Him, or [this phrase is] an attribute for the manifest proof (لبيّنة) وَكَذَّبْتُمْ بِهِ you [have denied it] since you associated others with Him ما عِنْدِي ما تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِ It is said that this means the punishment which they sought to hasten by their saying: فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ أَوِ ائْتِنا بِعَذابٍ أَلِيمٍ (Rain down upon us stones from the sky, or bring us a painful punishment.) إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ regarding the hastening of the punishment and its delay يَقْضِي بِالْحَقِ the decree of truth (قضاء الحقّ) in everything He decrees, whether it be delay or hastening وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْفاصِلِينَ the judges (القاضين). And it was read (وقرئ) يَقُصُّ الْحَقَّ (He follows the truth), meaning He follows it, like one who follows its trace (أثره).

**قُلْ إِنِّي عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّي وَكَذَّبْتُمْ بِهِ ما عِنْدِي ما تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِ إِنِ الْحُكْمُ إِلَّا لِلَّهِ يَقْضِي بِالْحَقِّ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْفاصِلِينَ ** (Say, 'Indeed I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, and you have denied it. I do not have what you seek to hasten. The judgement belongs only to Allah. He decrees with the truth, and He is the best of deciders.')

(٥٨) قُلْ لَوْ أَنَّ عِنْدِي ما تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِ ** (58) Say, ‘If what you seek to hasten were with me, the matter would have been decided between you and me.’ of the punishment لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ** I would have destroyed you immediately out of anger for my Lord, and what is between me and you would have been severed. وَاللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِالظَّالِمِينَ ** This is in the sense of a reservation (استدراك), as if He said: "But the matter belongs to Allah, and He is more knowledgeable of those who ought to be seized and those who ought to be granted respite." Thus it was said.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said to Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: قُلْ لَوْ أَنَّ عِنْدِي ما تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ بِهِ لَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ ** (58) Say, ‘If what you seek to hasten were with me, the matter would have been decided between you and me.’ He (peace be upon him) said: "If I were commanded to inform you of what you conceal in your hearts regarding your hastening of my death, so that you may oppress my Household (أهل بيتي) after me, then your likeness would be as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: كَمَثَلِ الَّذِي اسْتَوْقَدَ ناراً فَلَمَّا أَضاءَتْ ما حَوْلَهُ (like the person who kindled a fire, and when it illuminated all around him)." He says: "The earth was illuminated by the light of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, just as the sun illuminates." (The rest of) the hadith.

(٥٩) وَعِنْدَهُ مَفاتِحُ الْغَيْبِ ** (With Him are the keys of the Unseen) His treasures (خزائنه), if it is the plural of المفتح (with a fathah on the mīm) meaning the storehouse (المخزن); or His keys (مفاتيحه), if it is the plural of المِفتح (with a kasrah on the mīm) meaning the key (المفتاح), meaning that by which one attains the hidden things (المغيبات). And it was recited as مفاتيح. لا يَعْلَمُها إِلَّا هُوَ ** (no one knows them except Him.) Then He reveals them according to what His wisdom necessitated. وَيَعْلَمُ ما فِي الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ وَما تَسْقُطُ مِنْ وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا يَعْلَمُها وَلا حَبَّةٍ فِي ظُلُماتِ الْأَرْضِ وَلا رَطْبٍ وَلا يابِسٍ (He knows whatever is in the land and the sea. No leaf falls but He knows it, nor is there a grain in the darkness of the earth, nor anything wet or dry,) These are conjoined (معطوفات) to وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ ** (but it is in a manifest Book.) It is said that [the Book means] the knowledge of Allah, or the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ), or the Quran, as a substitute (بدل) for the first exception (الاستثناءِ الأوّل). And the conjoined words (المعطوفات) were recited with the rafʿ (nominative case), either as an ʿaṭf (conjunction) upon the place (محلّ) of مِنْ وَرَقَةٍ or upon the subject (الابتداءِ) with the predicate (الخبر) being إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ.

In al-Faqih, in a sermon by the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): **وَما تَسْقُطُ مِنْ وَرَقَةٍ ** (No leaf falls) means from a tree.
