In al-Kafi, al-Ma'ani, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) and al-Qummi: The leaf (الورقة) is the miscarriage (السّقط), and the grain (الحبّة) is the child (الولد), and ظُلُماتِ الْأَرْضِ ** (the darkness of the earth) is the wombs (الأرحام), and the wet (الرّطب) is that which lives (ما يحيى), and the dry (اليابس) is that which diminishes (ما يغيض). And all of that is فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ.** (in a manifest Book.)

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): The leaf (الوَرَقة) is the miscarriage (السقط) that falls from its mother's womb before the child cries out (يهلّ). And the grain (الحبّة) is the child (الولد) in its mother's womb when it cries out (هلّ) and falls before birth. And the wet (الرطب) is the lump of flesh (المضغة) when it settles in the womb before its creation is completed, before it moves. And the dry (اليابس) is the complete child (الولد التّام). And the manifest Book (الكتاب المبين) is the manifest Imam (الإِمام المبين).

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), in a hadith: And He said to your companion, the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): قُلْ كَفى بِاللهِ شَهِيداً بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَكُمْ وَمَنْ عِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ الْكِتابِ ** (Say: 'Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you, and he who possesses the knowledge of the Book.') And Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: وَلا رَطْبٍ وَلا يابِسٍ إِلَّا فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ ** (nor anything wet or dry, but it is in a manifest Book.) And the knowledge of this Book is with him.

I say: The meaning of the Book (الكتاب) from the perspective of esoteric interpretation (التّأويل) has already passed in the beginning of Surat al-Baqarah.

(60) وَهُوَ الَّذِي يَتَوَفَّاكُمْ بِاللَّيْلِ ** (60) It is He who takes your souls by night, He takes your souls (أرواحكم) away from activity (التصرّف) through sleep, just as He takes them away through death. وَيَعْلَمُ ما جَرَحْتُمْ ** and knows what you commit by day, meaning, what you earned (ما كسبتم) of deeds (الأعمال). بِالنَّهارِ ثُمَّ يَبْعَثُكُمْ فِيهِ ** then He raises you up in it, then He awakens you from your sleep during the day, لِيُقْضى أَجَلٌ مُسَمًّى so that a specified term may be fulfilled. so that you may complete your appointed terms (آجالكم).

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: لِيُقْضى أَجَلٌ مُسَمًّى ** (so that a specified term may be fulfilled). He (peace be upon him) said: "It is death." ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ ** Then to Him will be your return, through death. ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ ** whereat He will inform you concerning what you used to do. through recompense (بالمجازاة).

(٦١) وَهُوَ الْقاهِرُ فَوْقَ عِبادِهِ ** (61) He is the All-dominant over His servants, The All-powerful (المقتدر), the Exalted (المستعلي) over His servants. وَيُرْسِلُ عَلَيْكُمْ حَفَظَةً ** and He sends to you guardians who protect you, and preserve your deeds, and ward off from you the rebellious devils (مردة الشّياطين), the creeping things (هوامّ) of the earth, and all other afflictions (الآفات), and they record what you do. It was said that the wisdom in recording deeds is that when servants know that their deeds are being recorded against them and will be presented before the heads of witnesses (رؤوس الأشهاد), they are more deterred from committing vile acts (القبائح). And that when a servant trusts in the kindness (لطف) of his Master, and relies upon His compassion (عطفه) and His concealment (ستره), he does not feel the same shyness (احتشامه) toward Him as he does toward the servants who are watching him. And what is close to this meaning will come from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in Surat al-Infitar, if Allah wills (إنشاء الله). حَتَّى إِذا جاءَ أَحَدَكُمُ الْمَوْتُ تَوَفَّتْهُ رُسُلُنا until, when death approaches any of you, Our apostles take him away, (meaning) the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) and his assistants, as its explanation preceded in Surat al-Nisa. And it was recited (وقرء) as توفّاه with an alif (1) inclined (ممالة). وَهُمْ لا يُفَرِّطُونَ and they do not neglect [their duty]. They do not fall short (لا يقصّرون) through slackness (التّواني) or delay (التأخير).

(٦٢) ثُمَّ رُدُّوا إِلَى اللهِ مَوْلاهُمُ الْحَقِّ أَلا لَهُ الْحُكْمُ وَهُوَ أَسْرَعُ الْحاسِبِينَ (Then they will be returned to Allah, their true Master. Look! The judgment belongs to Him, and He is the swiftest of reckoners.)

ثُمَّ رُدُّوا إِلَى اللهِ ** to His judgment and recompense. مَوْلاهُمُ ** Who manages their affair, الْحَقِ the Just One (العدل) Who judges only with truth (بالحقّ). أَلا لَهُ الْحُكْمُ ** On that Day, judgment belongs to Him alone; there is no judgment for anyone other than Him. وَهُوَ أَسْرَعُ الْحاسِبِينَ ** He reckons the creatures in the measure of a blink of an eye, as previously mentioned in Surat al-Baqarah.

And in al-I'tiqadat (الإِعتقادات), [it is stated] that Allah, the Exalted, will address His servants, from the first and the last generations, on the Day of Resurrection, concerning the summary of the reckoning of their deeds, with a single address. Every single person will hear his own case from it, excluding others, and he will assume that he is the one being addressed, excluding others. Addressing one does not distract Him, the Mighty and Majestic, from addressing another. And He will complete the reckoning of the first and the last generations in the measure of half an hour of the hours of this world (الدّنيا).

footnote (١) By extending the *fathah* (*الفتحة*) until half an *alif* (*ألف*) results from it, and it inclines toward the *alif* (*الألف*).

(٦٣) قُلْ مَنْ يُنَجِّيكُمْ مِنْ ظُلُماتِ الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ ** (63) Say, 'Who delivers you from the darkness of the land and the sea, when you supplicate Him humbly and secretly: "If He delivers us from this, we will surely be among the grateful"?' from the severities (شدائدهما) of both. The darkness (الظلمة) is metaphorically borrowed for severity (الشدة) due to their shared characteristics of terror (الهول) and the nullification of sight (إبطال الإبصار). Thus, a severe day (اليوم الشديد) is called a dark day (يوم مظلم). تَدْعُونَهُ تَضَرُّعاً ** [Meaning] being humble (متضرعين) with your tongues. وَخُفْيَةً ** and concealing [it] (ومسرين) within yourselves. لَئِنْ أَنْجانا مِنْ هذِهِ ** based on the intention of speech (ارادة القول), meaning: saying, "If You deliver us from this darkness (الظلمة) and severity (الشدة)." لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ.

(٦٤) قُلِ اللهُ يُنَجِّيكُمْ مِنْها ** (64) Say: 'Allah delivers you from it' And it was read with takhfif (التّخفيف). وَمِنْ كُلِّ كَرْبٍ ** 'and from every distress' A grief (غمٍ) other than it. ثُمَّ أَنْتُمْ تُشْرِكُونَ ** 'yet you ascribe partners [to Him].' You return to polytheism (الشرك) and do not fulfill the covenant after the proof (الحجّة) has been established against you.

(٦٥) قُلْ هُوَ الْقادِرُ عَلى أَنْ يَبْعَثَ ** (65) Say: 'He is able to send' He sends. عَلَيْكُمْ عَذاباً مِنْ فَوْقِكُمْ ** 'upon you a punishment from above you' Just as He rained stones upon the people of Lot and upon the Companions of the Elephant (أصحاب الفيل). أَوْ مِنْ تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِكُمْ 'or from under your feet' Just as He drowned Pharaoh (فرعون) and caused the earth to swallow Qārūn (قارون). أَوْ يَلْبِسَكُمْ ** 'or confound you' He mixes you (يخلطكم) as factions (شِيَعاً). Groups (فرقاً) differing in desires (الأهواء), with every group among you supporting (مشايعة) the leader (الإِمام). And the meaning of confounding them is that they mix together or become entangled in the great battles (ملاحم القتال). وَيُذِيقَ بَعْضَكُمْ بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ ** 'and make you taste the violence of one another.' Some of you kill others. انْظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآياتِ 'Observe how We elaborate the signs,' By promise (الوعد) and threat (الوعيد). لَعَلَّهُمْ يَفْقَهُونَ 'so that they may understand.'

( A Narrative Discussion )

In al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): عَذاباً مِنْ فَوْقِكُمْ ** is the smoke (الدّخان) and the cry (الصّيحَة). أَوْ مِنْ تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِكُمْ ** is the swallowing by the earth (الخسف). أَوْ يَلْبِسَكُمْ شِيَعاً ** is the disagreement (الإِختلاف) in religion and some of you slandering others. وَيُذِيقَ بَعْضَكُمْ بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ ** is that some of you kill others. And all of this is concerning the People of the Qiblah (أهل القبلة). Allah says: **انْظُرْ كَيْفَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآياتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَفْقَهُونَ **

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): مِنْ فَوْقِكُمْ ** refers to the oppressive rulers (السّلاطين الظّلمة). أَوْ مِنْ تَحْتِ أَرْجُلِكُمْ ** refers to the evil slaves (العبيد السّوء) and those in whom there is no good. أَوْ يَلْبِسَكُمْ شِيَعاً ** means He strikes some of you against others through the enmity (العداوة) and partisanship (العصَبية) that He casts among you. وَيُذِيقَ بَعْضَكُمْ بَأْسَ بَعْضٍ ** is bad neighborliness (سوء الجوار).

And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "I asked my Lord that He not allow the followers of a religion other than theirs to prevail over my Ummah, and He granted me that. And I asked Him that He not destroy them by famine (جوعاً), and He granted me that. And I asked Him that He not gather them upon error (ضلال), and He granted me that. And I asked Him that He not confound them as factions, but He withheld that from me."

He said: And in the narration, it is stated that He said: "If the sword (السّيف) is placed among my Ummah, it will not be lifted from them until the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة)."

(٦٦) وَكَذَّبَ بِهِ قَوْمُكَ (٦٦) وَهُوَ الْحَقُّ قُلْ لَسْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ بِوَكِيلٍ (66) Your people have denied it, though it is the truth. Say, 'I am not a guardian over you.' It was said: meaning, the Quran. And it was said: meaning, the punishment (العذاب). وَهُوَ الْحَقُ: The truth (الصّدق), or that which is inevitable (الواقع) and must certainly descend. قُلْ لَسْتُ عَلَيْكُمْ بِوَكِيلٍ: Meaning, a guardian (بحفيظٍ). (٦٧) لِكُلِّ نَبَإٍ مُسْتَقَرٌّ (٦٧) وَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (67) For every prophecy there is a time of settlement, and soon you will know. لِكُلِّ نَبَإٍ: News (خبر). مُسْتَقَرٌّ: A time for its settlement (استقرار) and occurrence (وقوع). وَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ: When it occurs. (٦٨) وَإِذا رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ يَخُوضُونَ فِي آياتِنا (٦٨) فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ حَتَّى يَخُوضُوا فِي حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِ وَإِمَّا يُنْسِيَنَّكَ الشَّيْطانُ فَلا تَقْعُدْ بَعْدَ الذِّكْرَى مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ (68) When you see those who indulge in [blasphemous] talk about Our signs, avoid them until they engage in a different discourse. Should Satan make you forget, do not sit with the wrongdoing lot after the recollection. وَإِذا رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ يَخُوضُونَ فِي آياتِنا: By denying (بالتكذيب), mocking (والاستهزاءِ بها), and slandering (والطعن فيها) them. فَأَعْرِضْ عَنْهُمْ: So do not sit with them, and rise from their presence. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: It refers to speaking about Allah and arguing (الجدال) about the Quran. He said: The storytellers (القُصّاص) are among them. حَتَّى يَخُوضُوا فِي حَدِيثٍ غَيْرِهِ: Other than that [discourse]. وَإِمَّا يُنْسِيَنَّكَ الشَّيْطانُ: The prohibition (النّهي). And it was read (وقرئ) يُنسينك with takhfif (بالتخفيف). فَلا تَقْعُدْ بَعْدَ الذِّكْرى: After you remember. مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ: Meaning, with them. The explicit noun (الظاهر) was placed in the position of the pronoun (1) as an alert that they committed wrongdoing (ظلموا) by placing denial (التكذيب) and mockery (الاستهزاءِ) in the place of affirmation (التصديق) and veneration (الاستعظام). In al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): It is not permissible for you to sit with whomever you wish, because Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, says: وَإِذا رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ [up to] the end of the verse (الآية). And in al-Qummi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should not sit in an assembly where an Imam is reviled (يسبّ) or where a Muslim is backbitten (يغتاب). Indeed, Allah the Almighty says in His Book: وَإِذا رَأَيْتَ الَّذِينَ يَخُوضُونَ فِي آياتِنا [up to] the end of the verse (الآية).

(1) And to prevent the misconception that the pronoun refers specifically to these known deniers; rather, the prohibition is general for everyone who commits an act similar to theirs.

(٦٩) وَما عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ** (69) And there is nothing upon those who are Godwary And what is incumbent upon the Godwary who sit with them مِنْ حِسابِهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** (of their reckoning of anything)—from what they are reckoned for, concerning the abominations of their deeds and sayings—وَلكِنْ ذِكْرى ** (but a reminder). But upon them is a reminder, or upon them is that they remind them with a reminder, and prevent them from engaging [in vain talk] and other abominations, and show their dislike for it, لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ ** (that perhaps they may be Godwary), meaning, they avoid that, either out of love or dislike for their offense.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When فَلا تَقْعُدْ بَعْدَ الذِّكْرى مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (So do not sit with the wrongdoing lot after the reminder) was revealed, the Muslims said: "What should we do if every time the polytheists mock, we stand up and leave them? Then we would not enter the Sacred Mosque (المسجد الحرام) nor circumambulate the Sacred House (البيت الحرام)." So Allah revealed وَما عَلَى الَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ مِنْ حِسابِهِمْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ** (And there is nothing upon those who are Godwary of their reckoning of anything). He commanded them to remind them and enlighten them as much as they were able.

(٧٠) وَذَرِ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا دِينَهُمْ لَعِباً وَلَهْواً ** (70) And leave those who take their religion for a diversion and a play where they mocked it and ridiculed it, and based the matter of their religion upon caprice (التشهّي), or they made their festival (عيدهم), which was appointed as the time (ميقات) for their worship, a time of play and diversion (لعب ولهو). And the meaning is: turn away from them and do not care about their deeds and their sayings. وَغَرَّتْهُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا ** and the life of the world has deceived them so it distracted them from the Hereafter (العقبى). وَذَكِّرْ بِهِ ** and admonish with it meaning, with the Quran, أَنْ تُبْسَلَ نَفْسٌ بِما كَسَبَتْ ** lest any soul should be delivered to perdition for what it has earned for fear that a soul might be delivered to destruction (الهلاك) and be held hostage (ترتهن) by its evil deed. And the root meaning of البَسْل (المنع) is prevention/prohibition. لَيْسَ لَها مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ وَلِيٌّ وَلا شَفِيعٌ ** It shall have no guardian besides Allah, nor any intercessor who might ward off the punishment from it, وَإِنْ تَعْدِلْ كُلَّ عَدْلٍ (and though it offers every equivalent), meaning, though it offers every ransom (فداء). And العدل (the equivalent) is the ransom (الفدية) because it equals the ransomed object (المفدي). What is intended by it here is the ransom (الفداء). لا يُؤْخَذْ مِنْها أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ أُبْسِلُوا بِما كَسَبُوا ** it shall not be accepted from it. They are the ones who are delivered to perdition for what they have earned meaning, they were delivered to the punishment because of their ugly deeds and their false (الزّايفة) beliefs. لَهُمْ شَرابٌ مِنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ بِما كانُوا يَكْفُرُونَ ** For them is a drink of boiling water and a painful punishment because of what they used to disbelieve. This is a confirmation and detailing of that. And the meaning is: they are between boiling water (ماءٍ مغليّ) that gurgles (يتجرجر) in their bellies and a fire that blazes (تشتعل) upon their bodies because of their disbelief.

(٧١) قُلْ أَنَدْعُوا مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَنْفَعُنا وَلا يَضُرُّنا وَنُرَدُّ عَلى أَعْقابِنا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدانَا اللهُ كَالَّذِي اسْتَهْوَتْهُ الشَّياطِينُ فِي الْأَرْضِ حَيْرانَ لَهُ أَصْحابٌ يَدْعُونَهُ إِلَى الْهُدَى ائْتِنا قُلْ إِنَّ هُدَى اللهِ هُوَ الْهُدى وَأُمِرْنا لِنُسْلِمَ لِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (٧١) (Say, 'Shall we invoke, besides Allah, what neither benefits us nor harms us, and be turned back on our heels after Allah has guided us, like someone whom the devils have lured away in the land, bewildered, who has companions calling him to guidance: "Come to us!"?' Say, 'Indeed, the guidance of Allah is the [true] guidance, and we have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds.')

Say, 'Shall we invoke (قُلْ أَنَدْعُوا)?' meaning: we worship. besides Allah what neither benefits us nor harms us (مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ما لا يَنْفَعُنا وَلا يَضُرُّنا)?' meaning: [what] is not capable of benefiting us or harming us. and be turned back on our heels (وَنُرَدُّ عَلى أَعْقابِنا)?' meaning: and return from the religion of Islam to polytheism (الشّرك). after Allah has guided us, like someone whom the devils have lured away (بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدانَا اللهُ كَالَّذِي اسْتَهْوَتْهُ الشَّياطِينُ)?' meaning: like someone whom the rebellious ones (مردة) among the Jinn have taken away into the deserts (المهامة) (1) from a desire (هوى) when he departed. And it was read (استهواه) with an alif inclined (ممالة). in the land, bewildered (فِي الْأَرْضِ حَيْرانَ)?' meaning: confused (متحيراً), straying from the path. He has companions (لَهُ أَصْحابٌ)?' meaning: this lured person (المستهوى) has a company (رفقة). who call him to guidance (يَدْعُونَهُ إِلَى الْهُدَى)?' meaning: to the level path (الطريق المستوى) or to [the point] that they guide him to the straight path (الطريق المستقيم). 'Come to us!' (ائْتِنا)?' meaning: they say to him, 'Come to us!' while he has traversed the wilderness (التّيه) following the Jinn, not answering them nor coming to them. This is based on what the Arabs claim: that the Jinn lure away (يستهوي) the human being. Likewise, Say, 'Indeed, the guidance of Allah (قُلْ إِنَّ هُدَى اللهِ),' which is Islam, is the [true] guidance (هُوَ الْهُدى)' meaning: alone, and whatever is besides it is error (ضلال). and we have been commanded to submit to the Lord of all the worlds (وَأُمِرْنا لِنُسْلِمَ لِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ).' [This is] part of what is said.

footnote (١) *المهامة* (the deserts) is either derived from *الهومة*, meaning the wilderness (*الفلاة*), which is why the lion is nicknamed *الهوامّ* (the wanderer) due to its taking residence in the *الهومة*. Thus, *الهومة* and *المهامة* would have the same meaning. Or, it comes from *الهيماء*, meaning a waterless desert (*المفازة بلا ماء*).

(٧٢) وَأَنْ أَقِيمُوا الصَّلاةَ وَاتَّقُوهُ ** (72) and to maintain the prayer and to be wary of Him. Meaning, He commanded us to submit (لأن نسلم) and لأن أقيموا (that you maintain) means for Islam and for the maintenance of the prayer (لإِقامة الصّلوة). وَهُوَ الَّذِي إِلَيْهِ تُحْشَرُونَ ** (72) and it is He toward whom you will be mustered. So He will recompense every worker among you for his deed. (٧٣) وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ بِالْحَقِ ** (73) It is He who created the heavens and the earth with the truth. Existing by truth and wisdom. وَيَوْمَ يَقُولُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ (٧٣) (73) And the day He says, 'Be!' it is. (٧٤) قَوْلُهُ الْحَقُ ** (74) His word is the truth. It was said: Meaning, His word (قَوْلُهُ الْحَقُ) is the truth on the day He speaks, like your saying, "The fighting is on Friday," where اليوم (the day) means the time (الحين). The meaning is that He is the Creator of the heavens and the earth, and His word (قَوْلُهُ الْحَقُ) is effective in all existing things (الكائنات). Or, يَوْمَ (the day) is conjoined (معطوف) to السَّماواتِ , and قَوْلُهُ الْحَقُ ** is a subject and predicate (مبتدأ وخبر), or it is the agent (فاعل) of فَيَكُونُ (it is), meaning: and when He says, due to His word being the truth (لقوله الحق), meaning, due to His decree (لقضائه). كُنْ فَيَكُونُ ** (74) 'Be!' and it is. And what is meant is when He brings things into existence (يكوّن الأشياء) and originates them (يحدثها). وَلَهُ الْمُلْكُ يَوْمَ يُنْفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ ** (74) To Him belongs the sovereignty on the day the Trumpet is blown. Like His saying: لِمَنِ الْمُلْكُ الْيَوْمَ لِلَّهِ الْواحِدِ الْقَهَّارِ ** (Whose is the sovereignty today? Allah’s, the One, the All-subduing.) And الصُّورِ (the Trumpet) is a horn (قرن) made of light (نور) which Isrāfīl has taken into his mouth (التقمه), and he will blow into it. This is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And it is narrated that in it there is a hole (ثقبة) for every human being, containing his soul (روحه). It is described by wideness (السّعةِ) and narrowness (الضّيق). There is disagreement as to whether its top is narrow and its bottom wide, or the reverse, and there is a justification (وجه) for each view. A narration concerning its explanation and the description of the blowing into it will come in Sūrat al-Zumar, if Allah wills. عالِمُ الْغَيْبِ وَالشَّهادَةِ ** (74) Knower of the sensible and the Unseen. Meaning, He is the Knower of the sensible (الْغَيْبِ) and the Unseen (الشَّهادَةِ). وَهُوَ الْحَكِيمُ الْخَبِيرُ (٧٤) (74) and He is the All-wise, the All-aware. And this is like the summary (الفذلكة) (1) for the verse.

(٧٥) وَإِذْ قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ آزَرَ. (75) And when Abraham said to his father, Azar,

In al-Majma', he said, reporting from al-Zajjaj: There is no disagreement among the genealogists (النّسابين) that the name of Abraham's father was Tarikh (تارح) (1). He said: And this strengthens what our companions (أصحابنا) have said: that آزَرَ ** was Abraham's (peace be upon him) maternal grandfather or his paternal uncle (عمّه), since it is established with them that the fathers of the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) up to Adam (peace be upon him) were all monotheists (موحّدينَ), and the sect (الطائفة) is unanimous on this. And they narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny and grant them peace) that He said: "Allah Almighty continuously transferred Me from the loins of the pure (الطّاهرين) to the wombs of the purified (المطهّرات) until He brought Me forth into this world of yours. He did not defile Me with the defilement (دنس) of al-Jahiliyyah. Had there been a disbeliever (كافر) among His fathers, He would not have described all of them as pure (بالطّهارة), considering His saying: إِنَّمَا الْمُشْرِكُونَ نَجَسٌ. (28) Indeed the polytheists are unclean.**

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that آزَرَ , the father of Abraham (peace be upon him), was an astrologer (منجّماً) for Nimrod. And he continued the narration until he said: And Azar had intercourse (وقع) with his wife, and she conceived Abraham (the rest of the narration).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): that He was asked about the Almighty's saying: وَإِذْ قالَ إِبْراهِيمُ لِأَبِيهِ آزَرَ ** (75) And when Abraham said to his father, Azar, He said: The name of his father was Azar, and the knowledge is with Allah. أَتَتَّخِذُ أَصْناماً آلِهَةً إِنِّي أَراكَ وَقَوْمَكَ فِي ضَلالٍ ** (74) Will you take idols as gods? Indeed I see you and your people in manifest error. [Meaning:] away from the truth (الحقّ). مُبِينٍ ** (74) manifest error. [Meaning:] apparent error (ظاهر الضلالة).

(1) That is its calculation (حسابه). Its inventor completed and finished it from his saying: If he summarizes its calculation, that is such and such.

(٧٦) وَكَذلِكَ نُرِي إِبْراهِيمَ (76) And thus did We show Abraham We made him see, like this illumination (التبصير), and it is a narration of his past state, مَلَكُوتَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ [meaning] Their Lordship (ربوبيّتها) and their sovereignty (ملكها). And al-Malakūt (الملكوت) is the greatest sovereignty (الملك), and the tā’ (التاء) in it is for emphasis (للمبالغة). وَلِيَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُوقِنِينَ the dominion of the heavens and the earth, that he might be one of the convinced. Meaning, so that he might see it and be [convinced], or: We did that so that he might be [convinced].

In al-Majma’, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah removed (كشط) the veil from the earths until he saw them and what was beneath them, and from the heavens until he saw them and what was in them of the angels and the bearers of the Throne (حملة العرش).

And in al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The veil was removed for him from the earth and those upon it, and from the heaven and those within it, and the Angel (المَلَك) who carries it, and the Throne (العرش) and those upon it.

And al-Qummi added: And He did that for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), and in one narration: and the Imams (peace be upon them).

And in a narration by al-Ayyashi from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): And He did for Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم what He did for Abraham (peace be upon him), and indeed, I see that your companion has had the like done to him.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: His sight was given such strength that it penetrated the heavens, so he saw what was in them, and He saw the Throne (العرش) and what was above it, and He saw what was in the earth and what was beneath it. And in al-Manaqib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Jābir ibn Yazīd asked him about this verse, so he raised his hand and said: "Raise your head." He said: So I raised it and found the ceiling separated, and my gaze (ناظري) peered (رمق) through a gap (ثلم) until I saw a light from which my sight was bewildered. Then he said: "Thus did Abraham (peace be upon him) see مَلَكُوتَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (the dominion of the heavens and the earth)." "And look at the earth, then raise your head." When I raised it, I saw the ceiling as it was. Then he took my hand and led me out of the house, and dressed me in a garment, and said: "Close your eyes for an hour." Then he said: "You are in the darknesses (الظلمات) that Dhul-Qarnayn saw." So I opened my eyes but saw nothing. Then he took a few steps (أخطأ خُطاً) and said: "You are at the source (رأس عين) of the Water of Life (الحيوة) for al-Khidr (peace be upon him)." Then we left that world until we passed five regions (أقاليم). He said: "This is the dominion (ملكوت) of the earth." Then he said: "Close your eyes," and he took my hand, and behold, we were in the house where we had been, and he removed the garment he had dressed me in. I said: "May I be made your ransom (جعلت فداك), how much of the day has passed?" He said: "Three hours."

And in al-Kafi, al-Majma’, al-Qummi, and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Abraham (peace be upon him) saw مَلَكُوتَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ (the dominion of the heavens and the earth), he saw a man committing fornication (يزني), so he prayed against him and he died. Then he saw another and prayed against him and he died. Then he saw three and prayed against them and they died. So Allah revealed to Him: "O Abraham, your prayer is answered, so do not pray against My servants. For had I wished to cause them to die due to your prayer, I would not have created them. Indeed, I created My creation in three categories (أصناف): a category that worships Me and associates nothing with Me, so I reward them; a category that worships other than Me, but they will not escape Me; and a category that worships other than Me, but I will bring forth from their loins those who will worship Me."

(٧٦) فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اللَّيْلُ (76) When the night covered him It darkened upon him and covered him with its darkness. رَأى كَوْكَباً قالَ هذا رَبِّي he saw a star and said, 'This is my Lord.' [He said this] by way of denial (الإِنكار) and inquiry (الاستخبار), because his people used to worship the stars; or by way of observation (النّظر) and inference (الاستدلال), because he was seeking [the truth] in his youth. فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ But when it set, It disappeared. قالَ لا أُحِبُّ الْآفِلِينَ he said, 'I do not love those that set.' Let alone worshipping them, for movement (الانتقال), veiling (الاحتجاب), and concealment (الاستتار) are evidence of createdness (الحدوث) and need (الفقر).

(٧٧) فَلَمَّا رَأَى الْقَمَرَ بازِغاً ** (Then when he saw the moon rising) beginning in its ascent. قالَ هذا رَبِّي فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ قالَ لَئِنْ لَمْ يَهْدِنِي رَبِّي لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ ** (He said, 'This is my Lord!') But when it set, he said, 'If my Lord does not guide me, I will surely be one of the astray people.'

He deemed himself incapable and sought assistance from his Lord in grasping the truth, for one is not guided to it except through His divine success (توفيق). This was an instruction to his people and an alert to them that the moon, too, due to the change in its state, is not fit for divinity (الألوهية), and that whoever takes it as a god is astray (ضال).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from them عليهما السّلام: لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ ** (I will surely be one of the astray people) meaning, forgetting the covenant (الميثاق).

(٧٨) فَلَمَّا رَأَى الشَّمْسَ بازِغَةً قالَ هذا رَبِّي ** (78) When he saw the sun rising, he said, 'This is my Lord. It was said that the demonstrative pronoun (اسم الإِشارة) was mentioned to masculinize the predicate (الخبر) and to protect the Lord (الربّ) from the suspicion of feminization (التّأنيث). هذا أَكْبَرُ ** This is bigger.' He declared it great (كبّره) to manifest the specious argument (شبهة الخصم) of the opponent, or as a means of inference (استدلالاً). فَلَمَّا أَفَلَتْ قالَ يا قَوْمِ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ ** So when it set, he said, 'O my people! I am indeed quit of that which you ascribe as partners [to Allah].' [I am quit] of the originated bodies (الأجرام المحدثة) which are in need of an originator (محدث) who originates them, and who specifies their states with what they have been specified with. Then, when he disassociated himself from them, he turned towards their Originator (موجدها) and Innovator (مبدعها), Whom they themselves indicated, and he said:

(79) إِنِّي وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ حَنِيفاً وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (Indeed, I have turned my face toward Him who originated the heavens and the earth, being a ḥanīf, and I am not one of the polytheists.)

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Al-Ma’mun asked him, saying: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, is it not your saying that the Prophets are infallible (معصومون)?" He replied: "Yes." Al-Ma’mun said: "Then inform me about the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: فَلَمَّا جَنَّ عَلَيْهِ اللَّيْلُ رَأى كَوْكَباً قالَ هذا رَبِّي ** (When the night covered him, he saw a star and said, 'This is my Lord.')" Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, Abraham (peace be upon him) encountered three groups (أصناف): a group that worshipped Venus (الزّهرة), a group that worshipped the Moon, and a group that worshipped the Sun. That was when he emerged from the cave (السَّرَب) (1) where he had been hidden. When the night covered him, he saw Venus and said: قالَ هذا رَبِّي ** ('He said, "This is my Lord."')—this was said in denial (الإِنكار) and inquiry (الاستخبار). فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ ** (When it set) the star, he said: لا أُحِبُّ الْآفِلِينَ ** ('I do not love those that set.') because setting (الأفول) is among the attributes of the created (المحدث), not among the attributes of the Eternal (القديم). فَلَمَّا رَأَى الْقَمَرَ بازِغاً قالَ هذا رَبِّي ** (When he saw the moon rising, he said, 'This is my Lord.')—this was said in denial and inquiry. فَلَمَّا أَفَلَ ** (When it set) He (peace be upon him) said: لَئِنْ لَمْ يَهْدِنِي رَبِّي لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ ** ('If my Lord does not guide me, I will surely be among the astray people.') When morning came and he saw the sun rising, he said: قالَ هذا رَبِّي هذا أَكْبَرُ ** ('He said, "This is my Lord. This is greater"') than Venus and the Moon—this was said in denial and inquiry, not as a statement (الإِخبار) or affirmation (الإِقرار). فَلَمَّا أَفَلَتْ قالَ ** (When it set, he said) to the three groups who worshipped Venus, the Moon, and the Sun: يا قَوْمِ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ إِنِّي وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ حَنِيفاً وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** ('O my people! I am indeed absolved of what you associate [with Allah]. Indeed, I have turned my face toward Him who originated the heavens and the earth, being a ḥanīf, and I am not one of the polytheists.') Abraham (peace be upon him) only intended by what he said to clarify to them the falsehood of their religion and to establish for them that worship belongs to their Creator and the Creator of the heavens and the earth. What He used to argue against his people was what Allah inspired Him with and granted Him, as the Almighty said: وَتِلْكَ حُجَّتُنا آتَيْناها إِبْراهِيمَ عَلى قَوْمِهِ نَرْفَعُ دَرَجاتٍ مَنْ نَشاءُ ** ('That is Our argument, which We gave to Abraham against his people. We raise in rank whomever We wish.') Al-Ma’mun then said: "May Allah reward you well (لِلَّهِ درّك), O son of the Messenger of Allah."

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): آزَرَ ** (Azar), the father of Abraham (peace be upon him), was an astrologer (منجّماً) for Nimrod ibn Canaan. He said to Nimrod: "I see in the calculation of the stars that a man will appear in this time who will abrogate this religion and call to another religion." Nimrod asked him: "In which land will he be?" He replied: "In this land," and Nimrod's dwelling was in Kūthāriyā. Nimrod asked him: "Has he already come into the world?" Azar said: "No." Nimrod said: "Then it is necessary to separate men from women." The mother of Abraham became pregnant with Abraham (peace be upon him), but her pregnancy was not apparent. When her time of delivery approached, she said: "O Azar, I have become ill and wish to withdraw from you." It was customary in that time for a woman, when she became ill, to withdraw from her husband. So she went out and withdrew into a cave (غار), where she gave birth to Abraham (peace be upon him), prepared him, swaddled him, and returned to her home, sealing the entrance of the cave with stones. Allah caused milk to flow for Abraham (peace be upon him) from his thumb. His mother used to visit him. Nimrod had appointed guards over every pregnant woman and used to slaughter every male child. Thus, Abraham's mother fled with Abraham (peace be upon him) from the slaughter. Abraham grew in the cave in one day as others grew in a month, until he reached thirteen years of age in the cave. After that, his mother visited him. When she wanted to leave him, he clung to her and said: "O Mother, take me out." She said to him: "O my son, if the king learns that you were born in this time, he will kill you." When his mother left, he emerged from the cave. The sun had already set. He looked at Venus in the sky and said: قالَ هذا رَبِّي فَلَمَّا ** ('He said, "This is my Lord."') When Venus set, he said: "If He were my Lord, He would not move or depart." Then قالَ لا أُحِبُّ الْآفِلِينَ ** ('He said, "I do not love those that set."')—and the āfil (الآفل) is the one who disappears (الغائب). فَلَمَّا رَأَى الْقَمَرَ بازِغاً قالَ هذا رَبِّي ** ('When he saw the moon rising, he said, "This is my Lord."')—this is bigger and better. When it moved and departed, قالَ لَئِنْ لَمْ يَهْدِنِي رَبِّي لَأَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الضَّالِّينَ ** ('He said, "If my Lord does not guide me, I will surely be among the astray people."') When morning came and the sun rose, and he saw its light illuminating the world due to its rising, he said: قالَ هذا رَبِّي هذا أَكْبَرُ ** ('He said, "This is my Lord. This is greater"') and better. When it moved and departed, Allah removed the veil (كشط) for him from the heavens until he saw the Throne (العرش) and those upon it, and Allah showed him the dominion (مَلَكُوتَ) of the heavens and the earth. It was then that قالَ يا قَوْمِ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ إِنِّي وَجَّهْتُ وَجْهِيَ لِلَّذِي فَطَرَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ حَنِيفاً وَما أَنَا مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** ('He said, "O my people! I am indeed absolved of what you associate [with Allah]. Indeed, I have turned my face toward Him who originated the heavens and the earth, being a ḥanīf, and I am not one of the polytheists."') So he came to his mother, and she brought him into her house and placed him among her children. He (the narrator) said: Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) was asked about Abraham’s (peace be upon him) saying: هذا رَبِّي ** ('This is my Lord.') Did he commit polytheism (أشرك) in his saying هذا رَبِّي ? He replied: "Whoever says this today is a polytheist (مشرك), but there was no polytheism (شرك) from Abraham (peace be upon him). Rather, he was seeking his Lord, whereas it is polytheism (شرك) from anyone else." And al-Ayyashi narrated similarly, adding from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them): "He was only seeking his Lord and did not reach disbelief (كفراً). And whoever among people reflects upon something similar, he is in the same position as him."

(1) السَّرَب (al-Sarab), with the vowel movement (بالتحريك), means the burrow (جحر) of a wild animal or a humble dwelling beneath the earth, or the channel through which water enters a wall. What is meant here is the cave (الغار) where he was born, to which his mother fled out of fear of النمرودية. She gave birth to him there and raised him with the assistance of Gabriel (جبرئيل) until years passed, whereupon he emerged from the cave, appeared, and began the call (الدعوة).

(٨٠) وَحاجَّهُ قَوْمُهُ ** (80) And his people argued with him. And they disputed with him concerning Monotheism (التوحيد). قالَ أَتُحاجُّونِّي فِي اللهِ ** He said, 'Do you argue with me concerning Allah, concerning His Oneness (وحدانيته). And it was read (وقرئ) with the nūn (النون) being lightened (بتخفيف). وَقَدْ هَدانِ ** while He has guided me? to His Monotheism (توحيده). وَلا أَخافُ ما تُشْرِكُونَ بِهِ ** I do not fear what you ascribe as partners to Him, meaning, I do not fear your deities (معبوداتكم) at all, because they have no power (قدرة) over harm (ضرّ) or benefit (نفع). إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ رَبِّي شَيْئاً ** unless my Lord wills something. that He afflict me with something disliked (مكروه). And it is as if this is a response to their attempt to frighten him concerning their deities (آلهتهم). وَسِعَ رَبِّي كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْماً ** My Lord comprehends all things in [His] knowledge. So, it is not unlikely (فلا يستبعد) that the sending down (إنزال) of something fearful (مخوف) upon me is within His knowledge (علمه). أَفَلا تَتَذَكَّرُونَ (Will you not then take admonition?) so that you distinguish (فتميّزوا) between the Capable (القادر) and the incapable (العاجز).

(٨١) وَكَيْفَ أَخافُ ما أَشْرَكْتُمْ ** (And how should I fear what you have associated [with Him]) —when no harm is attached to it— وَلا تَخافُونَ أَنَّكُمْ أَشْرَكْتُمْ بِاللهِ ** (while you do not fear that you have associated [others] with Allah) —and He is worthy that all fear should be directed towards Him, because it is associating the created (المصنوع) with the Creator (الصانع), and equating the incapable, powerless (المقدور العاجز) with the Capable (القادر), the Harmful (الضّارّ), the Beneficial (النّافع)— ما لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ عَلَيْكُمْ سُلْطاناً ** (for which He has not sent down to you any authority) —meaning, a proof (حجّة). The meaning is: Why do you object to my security in a place of security, yet you do not object to your own security in a place of fear?— فَأَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ أَحَقُّ بِالْأَمْنِ ** (Which of the two parties is worthier of security) —the monotheists (الموحدون) or the polytheists (المشركون)— إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ. (if you know.)

(82) الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا ** (and did not mix) إِيمانَهُمْ بِظُلْمٍ أُولئِكَ لَهُمُ الْأَمْنُ وَهُمْ مُهْتَدُونَ ** (Those who have faith and do not taint their faith with wrongdoing—for them is safety, and they are the guided ones.)

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (prayers of Allah be upon him): that it is part of the completion of the saying of Ibrahim (peace be upon him).

And narrated from Ibn Mas'ud: When this verse was revealed, it was difficult for the people, and they said: "O Messenger of Allah, which of us has not wronged himself?" He said: "It is not what you mean. Have you not heard what the righteous servant (peace be upon him) said: يا بُنَيَّ لا تُشْرِكْ بِاللهِ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ (O my son! Do not ascribe any partners to Allah. Indeed, polytheism is a great wrong!)"

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: "The wrongdoing (الظلم) is misguidance (الضّلال) and what is greater than it."

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked about الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا إِيمانَهُمْ بِظُلْمٍ ** (Those who have faith and do not taint their faith with wrongdoing)—is fornication (الزّنا) part of it? He said: "I seek refuge in Allah from those! No, but it is a sin (ذنب) that if he repents, Allah accepts his repentance." And He said: "The one who persists in fornication (الزّناءِ), theft (السّرقة), and drinking wine (شارب الخمر) is like an idol worshipper (عابد الوثن)." And in a narration, He said: "Those are the Khawarij (الخوارج) and their followers (أصحابهم)."

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): that the wrongdoing (الظلم) here is doubt (الشّكّ).

And narrated from Him (peace be upon him) regarding وَلَمْ يَلْبِسُوا إِيمانَهُمْ بِظُلْمٍ : He said: "They believed in what Muhammad (prayers of Allah be upon him and his family) brought concerning the Guardianship (الولاية) and did not mix it with the guardianship (ولاية) of so-and-so and so-and-so (فلان وفلان)."

(٨٣) وَتِلْكَ حُجَّتُنا آتَيْناها إِبْراهِيمَ ** (83) That was Our argument, which We gave to Abraham We guided him to it and taught it to him عَلى قَوْمِهِ نَرْفَعُ دَرَجاتٍ مَنْ نَشاءُ ** over his people. We raise in rank whomever We wish. in knowledge (العلم) and wisdom (الحكمة). And it was read (وقرئ) with tanwin (التّنوين). إِنَّ رَبَّكَ حَكِيمٌ ** Indeed your Lord is all-wise, in His raising and His lowering. عَلِيمٌ ** all-knowing. of the condition of the one He raises and his preparedness for it.

(٨٤) وَوَهَبْنا لَهُ إِسْحاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ كُلًّا هَدَيْنا ** (And We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and We guided each of them) meaning, each of the two of them. وَنُوحاً هَدَيْنا مِنْ قَبْلُ ** (and Noah We guided before) meaning, We guided them so that We might place the trusteeship (الوصيّة) in their Household (أهل بيتهم). This is narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). He narrated it in al-Kafi and al-Ikmal in the narrative concerning the continuous connection of the trusteeship (الوصيّة) from the time of Adam. وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ داوُدَ وَسُلَيْمانَ وَأَيُّوبَ وَيُوسُفَ وَمُوسى وَهارُونَ وَكَذلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُحْسِنِينَ. (and from his descendants, David and Solomon, Job and Joseph, Moses and Aaron. Thus do We reward the virtuous.)

(٨٥) وَزَكَرِيَّا وَيَحْيى وَعِيسى ** (And Zechariah, John, and Jesus) In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "By Allah, Allah has indeed attributed Jesus son of Mary in the Quran to Abraham (peace be upon him) through the women (من قبل النّساءِ)." Then He recited this Ayah. And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): "Jesus (peace be upon him) was only joined to the progeny (بذراري) of the Prophets through the path of Mary. Likewise, we have been joined to the progeny (بذراري) of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم through our mother Fatima (peace be upon her)." (This was said in response to Harun regarding this issue.) وَإِلْياسَ كُلٌّ مِنَ الصَّالِحِينَ.** (and Elijah. Each of them was one of the righteous.)

(٨٦) وَإِسْماعِيلَ وَالْيَسَعَ وَيُونُسَ وَلُوطاً وَكلًّا فَضَّلْنا عَلَى الْعالَمِينَ. (86) And Ishmael, Elisha, Jonah, and Lot. We graced each [of them] over all the nations. (٨٧) وَمِنْ آبائِهِمْ وَذُرِّيَّاتِهِمْ وَإِخْوانِهِمْ وَاجْتَبَيْناهُمْ وَهَدَيْناهُمْ إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ. (87) And [some] of their fathers, their descendants, and their brethren. We chose them and guided them to a straight path. (٨٨) ذلِكَ هُدَى اللهِ يَهْدِي بِهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ مِنْ عِبادِهِ وَلَوْ أَشْرَكُوا مع فضلهم وعلوّ شأنهم لَحَبِطَ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ فكانوا كغيرهم. (88) That is Allah’s guidance, by which He guides whomever He wishes of His servants. But had they ascribed partners [to Allah], despite their merit and high status, what they used to do would have been rendered void for them, and they would have been like others. (٨٩) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ يريد به الجنس وَالْحُكْمَ والحكمة أو الحكم بين النّاس وَالنُّبُوَّةَ فَإِنْ يَكْفُرْ بِها أي بالنّبوة أو الثلاثة هؤُلاءِ يعني قريشاً فَقَدْ وَكَّلْنا بِها قَوْماً لَيْسُوا بِها بِكافِرِينَ. (89) They are the ones whom We gave the Book, He means by it the genus (الجنس), the judgment, and wisdom (الحكمة) or judgment (الحكم) among the people, and the prophethood. So if these [people] disbelieve in it, meaning, the prophethood (النّبوة) or the three [things], these meaning Quraysh, We have entrusted it to a people who will not disbelieve in it.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding قَوْماً (a people): "They establish the prayer (الصلوة), give the Zakat (الزّكوة), and remember Allah frequently."

(90) The Almighty's saying: أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ هَدَى اللهُ ** (They are those whom Allah has guided) He means the Prophets whose mention preceded. فَبِهُداهُمُ اقْتَدِهْ ** (follow their guidance) So follow their way (طريقتهم) by emulation (بالاقتداءِ), and the hā' (الهاء) is for pausing (للوقف).

In Misbah al-Shari'ah, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): There is no path for the intelligent (الأكياس) among the believers safer than emulation (الاقتداءِ), because it is the clearest methodology (المنهج الأوضح) and the soundest objective (المقصد الأصحّ). Allah said to His most honored creation, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ هَدَى اللهُ فَبِهُداهُمُ اقْتَدِهْ ** (They are those whom Allah has guided, so follow their guidance) For if there were a straighter course (مسلك أقوم) for the religion of Allah than emulation (الاقتداءِ), He would have urged His Prophets and His saints (أولياءه) toward it.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: And the best guidance (الهدى) is the guidance of the Prophets (هدى الأنبياءِ).

And in Nahj al-Balaghah: Emulate the guidance of your Prophet, for it is the best guidance (أفضل الهدى).

قُلْ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** (Say, 'I do not ask you any reward for it') —meaning, for the conveyance (على التبليغ)—أَجْراً ** (reward) —meaning, a recompense (جعلاً) from your side, just as those Prophets who were before me did not ask for it. And this is part of what He commanded to emulate them in (الاقتداءِ بهم).

إِنْ هُوَ ** (It is nothing but) —meaning, the conveyance (التبليغ)—إِلَّا ذِكْرى لِلْعالَمِينَ ** (a reminder for all the nations) —meaning, a reminder (تذكيراً) and an admonition (وعظةً) for them.

(٩١) وَما قَدَرُوا اللهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ ** (91) They did not measure Allah with His true measure. And they did not know Him with His true knowledge, nor did they glorify Him with His true glorification, nor did they describe Him with that which is worthy of Him to be described, such as mercy upon His servants and kindness toward them. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah is not described. And how can He be described when He has said in His Book: وَما قَدَرُوا اللهَ حَقَّ قَدْرِهِ (They did not measure Allah with His true measure)? Thus, He is not described by any measure (قَدْر) except that He is greater than that. And another narration concerning this will come in Surat al-Zumar, if Allah the Almighty wills. إِذْ قالُوا ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ عَلى بَشَرٍ مِنْ شَيْءٍ ** when they said, 'Allah has not sent down anything to any human.' When they denied the revelation (الوحي) and the sending of the Messengers, and that is among His greatest mercies and most magnificent kindnesses. Al-Qummi said: They are Quraysh and the Jews. قُلْ مَنْ أَنْزَلَ الْكِتابَ الَّذِي جاءَ بِهِ مُوسى نُوراً وَهُدىً لِلنَّاسِ تَجْعَلُونَهُ قَراطِيسَ تُبْدُونَها وَتُخْفُونَ كَثِيراً ** Say, 'Who sent down the Book which Moses brought, a light and a guidance for the people, which you turn into sheets of paper, disclosing some of it and concealing much?' He obligated them with that which they must acknowledge, along with rebuking them for their distortion by disclosing some and concealing some, and making it scattered sheets (وَرَقَات) so that they could achieve what they intended. In al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse. He said: "They used to conceal what they willed and disclose what they willed." And in another narration: "They used to write it in sheets (القراطيس), then disclose what they willed and conceal what they willed." And al-Qummi said: يُخْفُونَ (they conceal) means from the reports (أخبار) of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. And it was read with the yāʾ (بالياءِ). وَعُلِّمْتُمْ ما لَمْ تَعْلَمُوا أَنْتُمْ وَلا آباؤُكُمْ قُلِ اللهُ ** And you were taught what neither you nor your fathers knew. Say, 'Allah.' Meaning: Allah sent it down. It is said that He commanded him (the Prophet) to answer on their behalf, indicating that the answer is definite (مُتَعَيَّن) and no other is possible, and alerting (them) that they were confounded (بُهِتُوا) such that they could not provide the answer. ثُمَّ ذَرْهُمْ فِي خَوْضِهِمْ يَلْعَبُونَ ** Then leave them to play in their rambling. Al-Qummi said: Meaning what they plunged into (خاضوا فيه) of denial (التكذيب).

(٩٢) وَهذا كِتابٌ أَنْزَلْناهُ مُبارَكٌ ** (92) This is a blessed Book which We have sent down, Abundant in benefit (النّفع) and advantage (الفائِدة) مُصَدِّقُ الَّذِي بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ** confirming that which was before it, The Books which preceded it وَلِتُنْذِرَ ** and that you may warn And it was read with the yā’ (بالياءِ), meaning the Book (warns) أُمَّ الْقُرى ** the Mother of Cities Meaning Mecca (مكّة). It was named by this because the earth was spread out (دُحِيَت) from beneath it, so it is as if it was born from it.

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: It was named أُمَّ الْقُرى ** (the Mother of Cities) because it is the first spot (بقعة) on earth that Allah created وَمَنْ حَوْلَها ** and those around it. The people of the East and the West وَالَّذِينَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ وَهُمْ عَلى صَلاتِهِمْ يُحافِظُونَ ** Those who believe in the Hereafter believe in it, and they are watchful of their prayers. For indeed, whoever confirms the Hereafter fears the consequence (العاقبة). And the fear continuously carries him to contemplation (النظر) and reflection (التدبّر) until he believes in it (the Book) and maintains obedience (الطّاعة). And the specification of the prayer (الصلوة) is because it is the pillar (عماد) of the religion and the sign (علم) of faith (الإِيمان).

(93) **وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً أَوْ قالَ أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ وَلَمْ يُوحَ إِلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَمَنْ قالَ سَأُنْزِلُ مِثْلَ ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ ** (And who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or says, 'It has been revealed to me,' though nothing has been revealed to him, and he who says, 'I will send down the like of what Allah has sent down'?)

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both): It was revealed concerning Ibn Abi Sarh, whom Uthman had appointed over Mudar. He was one of those whose blood the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم declared forfeit on the Day of the Conquest of Mecca. He used to write for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: إِنَّ اللهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ ** (Indeed, Allah is All-mighty, All-wise), he would write: إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ (Indeed, Allah is All-knowing, All-wise). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would say to him: "Leave it [as it is]: إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيمٌ ** (Indeed, Allah is All-knowing, All-wise)." Ibn Abi Sarh used to say to the hypocrites (المنافقين): "I certainly say from my own self the like of what He brings, and nothing is changed concerning me." So Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, revealed concerning him what He revealed.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: Abdullah ibn Sa'd ibn Abi Sarh, the foster brother (أخو من الرّضاعة) of Uthman ibn Affan, embraced Islam and came to Medina. He had a good appearance (حسن). When the revelation (الوحي) descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, he would call him, and he would write what the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم dictated. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to him: سَمِيعٌ بَصِيرٌ ** (All-hearing, All-seeing), he would write سميع عليم (All-hearing, All-knowing). And when He said: وَاللهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ ** (And Allah is well aware of what you do), he would write بصير (All-seeing), and he would differentiate between the تاء (ت) and the ياء (ي). The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم would say: "It is one [meaning]." Then he apostatized (ارتدّ) as a disbeliever (كافراً) and returned to Mecca, saying to Quraysh: "By Allah, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم does not know what he is saying. I say the like of what he says, and that is not denied to me, so I send down the like of what He sends down." So Allah revealed to His Prophet concerning that: وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً أَوْ قالَ أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ وَلَمْ يُوحَ إِلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ وَمَنْ قالَ سَأُنْزِلُ مِثْلَ ما أَنْزَلَ اللهُ* (And who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or says, 'It has been revealed to me,' though nothing has been revealed to him, and he who says, 'I will send down the like of what Allah has sent down'?).

When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم conquered Mecca, He ordered his execution. Uthman came with him, holding his hand, while the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was in the Mosque (المسجد). He said: "O Messenger of Allah, pardon him." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم remained silent. Then he repeated [the request], and He remained silent. Then he repeated it again, and He said: "He is yours." When he had passed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to His companions: "Did I not say that whoever sees him should kill him?" A man said: "My eye was upon you, O Messenger of Allah, [waiting] for you to signal to me so that I might kill him." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, the Prophets do not kill by signaling (بالاشارة)." Thus, he became one of the freed captives (الطّلقاءِ).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding its esoteric interpretation (تأويله), [it refers to] whoever claims the Imamate (الإمامة) besides the Imam (peace be upon him).

**وَلَوْ تَرى إِذِ الظَّالِمُونَ فِي غَمَراتِ الْمَوْتِ ** (If you could see when the wrongdoers are in the throes of death)

— meaning its hardships (شدائده), derived from the submerging of water (غمره الماء) when it covers something — وَالْمَلائِكَةُ باسِطُوا أَيْدِيهِمْ ** (and the angels stretch out their hands) — to seize their souls (لقبض أرواحهم), like the demanding, dominant creditor (كَالمتقاضي المتسلّط) — أَخْرِجُوا أَنْفُسَكُمُ ** (Bring out your souls!) — they say to them in severity (تغليظاً) and harshness (تعنيفاً) (1) الْيَوْمَ تُجْزَوْنَ عَذابَ الْهُونِ (Today you shall be requited with the punishment of humiliation) — meaning disgrace (الهوان). Al-Qummi said it means thirst (العطش). And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Thirst (العطش) on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). بِما كُنْتُمْ تَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللهِ غَيْرَ الْحَقِّ وَكُنْتُمْ عَنْ آياتِهِ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ ** (because of what you used to say against Allah falsely, and because you used to disdain His signs) — meaning you did not believe in them (لا تؤمنون بها).

(٩٤) وَلَقَدْ جِئْتُمُونا فُرادى ** (And certainly you have come to Us individually) separated from your wealth, your children, and your idols. كَما خَلَقْناكُمْ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ (just as We created you the first time) upon the form (الهيئة) upon which you were born. In al-Khara'ij, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: He recited this verse to Fatimah bint Asad, and she asked: "What is فرادى (furādā)?" He said: "Naked (عراة)." She said: "Oh, my shame (وا سوأتاه)!" So, he asked Allah that He not expose her nakedness (عورتها) and that He resurrect her in her shrouds (أكفانها). A حديث with the same meaning is found in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام. And narrated from Him عليه السلام: Be meticulous (تنوقوا) (2) regarding the shrouds (الأكفان), for you will be resurrected in them. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from Him عليه السلام: He was asked about the people: "Will they be resurrected naked (عراة)?" He said: "Rather, they will be resurrected in their shrouds (أكفانهم)." It was said: "How can they have shrouds when they have decayed?" He said: "Indeed, the One Who gave life to their bodies will renew their shrouds." It was asked: "What about the one who died without a shroud?" He said: "Allah will cover his nakedness (عورته) with whatever He wills from Himself." وَتَرَكْتُمْ ما خَوَّلْناكُمْ (and you have left behind your backs what We had bestowed on you) that which We made you possess in the world, and with which you were preoccupied instead of the Hereafter, وَراءَ ظُهُورِكُمْ (behind your backs) you did not carry anything of it. وَما نَرى مَعَكُمْ شُفَعاءَكُمُ الَّذِينَ زَعَمْتُمْ أَنَّهُمْ فِيكُمْ شُرَكاءُ (and We do not see your intercessors with you, whom you claimed to be partners in your [affairs]) meaning: partners of Allah in your Lordship (ربوبيّتكم) and your deserving of worship (واستحقاق عبادتكم). لَقَدْ تَقَطَّعَ بَيْنَكُمْ (Certainly, the bond between you has been severed) meaning, your connection has been severed and your gathering has been scattered. The word البين (al-bayn) is one of the contraries (الأضداد); it is used for connection (الوصل) and separation (الفصل). It was recited with the نصب (naṣb) (accusative case) based on the omission of the agent (إضمار الفاعل), meaning: what was between you (ما بينكم). وَضَلَّ عَنْكُمْ (and has failed you) meaning, it was lost and became void (ضاع وبطل). ما كُنْتُمْ تَزْعُمُونَ (what you used to claim.) Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: This verse was revealed concerning Mu'awiyah and Banu Umayyah, and their partners (شركاؤهم) are their Imams (أئمّتهم). لَقَدْ تَقَطَّعَ بَيْنَكُمْ (Certainly, the bond between you has been severed) means the affection (المودّة).

(95) إِنَّ اللهَ فالِقُ الْحَبِّ وَالنَّوى ** (95) Indeed Allah is the splitter of the grain and the date-stone, by means of vegetation (النّبات) and trees (الشجر). يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ ** He brings forth the living from the dead, that which grows, from animals (الحيوان) and plants (النبات), from that which does not grow, such as the sperm-drop (النّطفة) and the grain (الحبّ). **وَمُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتِ مِنَ الْحَيِ ** and He is the bringer forth of the dead from the living. that which does not grow, from that which grows.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: The clay (الطّينة). الْحَبِ ** (the grain) is the clay (طينة) of the believers (المؤمنين), upon which Allah cast love (المحبّة). وَالنَّوى ** (and the date-stone) is the clay (طينة) of the disbelievers (الكافرين) who kept far (نأوا) from every good. And the nawa (النّوى) was only named thus because it kept far (نأى) from every good and distanced itself from it. So Allah said: يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَمُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتِ مِنَ الْحَيِ ** (He brings forth the living from the dead, and He is the bringer forth of the dead from the living.) So the living (الحيّ) is the believer (المؤمن) whose clay is brought forth from the clay of the disbeliever (الكافر), and the dead (الميت) who is brought forth from the living (الحيّ) is the disbeliever (الكافر) who is brought forth from the clay of the believer (المؤمن).

And al-Qummi said: الْحَبِ ** (the grain) is what He loved (أحبّه), and وَالنَّوى ** (and the date-stone) is what kept far (نأى) (1) from the truth (الحق). He also said: فالِقُ الْحَبِ (the splitter of the grain) means He splits the knowledge (العلم) from the Imams (الأئمّة), and وَالنَّوى (and the date-stone) is what is far (بعد) from Him (2).

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْحَبِ ** (the grain) is the believer (المؤمن), and that is His saying: وَأَلْقَيْتُ عَلَيْكَ مَحَبَّةً مِنِّي ** (and I cast upon you a love from Me). وَالنَّوى (and the date-stone) is the disbeliever (الكافر) who kept far (نأى) from the truth (الحق) and did not accept it.

ذلِكُمُ اللهُ ** Such is Allah! meaning: He is the One for Whom worship (العبادة) is rightfully due. فَأَنَّى تُؤْفَكُونَ** Then where are you being led away? meaning: you are turned away from Him to other than Him.

(٩٦) فالِقُ الْإِصْباحِ ** (96) (He is) the splitter of the dawn, Splitting the pillar (عمود) of the morning from the darkness of the night, and جَعَلَ اللَّيْلَ سَكَناً ** (and He made the night a rest), wherein the creation rests, just as He said: لِتَسْكُنُوا فِيهِ ** (that you may rest in it). In Nahj al-Balagha: "And do not travel at the beginning of the night, for Allah made it a rest (سكناً) and decreed it a place of residence (مقاماً), not a journey (ظعناً). So, rest your body in it and relieve your back." And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Marry during the night, for Allah made it a rest (سكناً)." And al-Ayyashi narrated the like of it. And in a narration: "And do not seek needs (الحوائج) during the night, for it is dark (مظلم)." And in al-Kafi: ‘Ali ibn al-Husayn (peace be upon them both) used to command his servants not to slaughter until the dawn broke, saying: "Indeed, Allah جَعَلَ اللَّيْلَ سَكَناً ** (made the night a rest) for everything." And it was recited (وقرئ) as wa jā‘il al-layl (وَجَاعِلَ اللَّيْلِ) and وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ حُسْباناً (and the sun and the moon for reckoning), based on different cycles (أدوار مختلفة) by which the times (الأوقات) are calculated. ذلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ الْعَزِيزِ (That is the determination of the All-mighty), Who subdued them both and made them travel in that specific manner, الْعَلِيمِ ** (the All-knowing) of their management (بتدبيرهما).

(٩٧) وَهُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ النُّجُومَ لِتَهْتَدُوا بِها فِي ظُلُماتِ الْبَرِّ وَالْبَحْرِ ** (And He is the One who set up the stars for you, that you may be guided by them in the darkness of the land and the sea.) [This refers to] in the darkness of the night in the land and the sea, and their attribution to the two [land and sea] is due to association (لِلْمُلَابَسَة), or [it refers to guidance] in the ambiguous paths or matters, which He named darknesses (ظُلُمَات) metaphorically (عَلَى الِاسْتِعَارَة). Al-Qummi said: النُّجُومَ ** [the stars] are the Family of Muhammad (peace be upon them). قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآياتِ ** (We have certainly elaborated the signs) We have clarified them section by section (فَصْلًا فَصْلًا). لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ ** (for a people who know) for they are the ones who benefit from it.

(98) وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنْشَأَكُمْ مِنْ نَفْسٍ واحِدَةٍ ** (It is He who produced you from a single soul) And that is Adam (peace be upon him). فَمُسْتَقَرٌّ (where there is a lodging) And it was recited with a kasrah on the qāf (بكسر القاف), meaning: settled (قارّ). وَمُسْتَوْدَعٌ ** (and a repository.) And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said to Abu Basir when he asked Him about this verse: "What do the people of your town, where you are, say?" He replied: "They say فَمُسْتَقَرٌّ ** (where there is a lodging) is in the womb (الرّحم), and وَمُسْتَوْدَعٌ ** (and a repository) is in the loins (الصّلب)." He (peace be upon him) said: "They lied. The mustaqarr (المستقرّ) is he in whose heart faith (الإِيمان) has settled, so it is never removed from him. And the mustawdaʿ (المستودع) is he who is entrusted with faith (الإِيمان) for a time, and then it is stripped from him. And indeed, al-Zubayr was one of them."

And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about it, and He said: فَمُسْتَقَرٌّ ** (where there is a lodging) is in the womb (الرَّحِم), and وَمُسْتَوْدَعٌ ** (and a repository) is in the loins (الصلب). And the mustawdaʿ (المستودع) may be faith (الإيمان) which is then stripped away from him. Indeed, al-Zubayr walked in the light and illumination of faith (الإِيمان) when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم passed away, until he walked with the sword, saying: "We shall not pledge allegiance except to Ali." And in one narration, He said: The mustaqarr (المستقرّ) is the permanent (الثابت), and the mustawdaʿ (المستودع) is the borrowed (المعار).

And narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: Whatever faith (الإِيمان) was settled (المستقر) is فَمُسْتَقَرٌّ (lodged) forever until the Day of Resurrection, and whatever was deposited (مستودعاً), Allah strips it away before death.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Him (peace be upon him): Allah created the Prophets (النّبيينّ) upon Prophethood (النبوّة), so they are nothing but Prophets. And He created the believers (المؤمنين) upon faith (الإِيمان), so they are nothing but believers. And He lent (أعار) faith (إيماناً) to some people; if He wills, He completes it for them, and if He wills, He strips it away from them. He said: "And concerning them, the verse فَمُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمُسْتَوْدَعٌ ** (where there is a lodging and a repository) applies." And He said: "Indeed, so-and-so (فلاناً) was entrusted with his faith (إيمانه), but when he lied against us, that faith was stripped from him."

I say: The reference to "so-and-so" (فلان) is a euphemism for Abu al-Khattab Muhammad ibn Miqlas al-Ghali (أبي الخطاب محمّد بن مقلاص الغالي), as is understood from another narration. قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآياتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَفْقَهُونَ (We have certainly elaborated the signs for a people who understand.) It is said: The mention of the stars is followed by "they know" (يعلمون) because their matter is apparent, but the mention of the creation of the Children of Adam is followed by "they understand" (يفقهون) because their production from a single soul (نفس واحدة) and their transformation through different states is subtle (دقيق) and obscure (غامض), requiring the use of insight (فطنة) and careful consideration (تدقيق نظر).

footnote (١) Because some of the stars are before man, some are behind him, some are on his right, and some are on his left, and he is guided by them during travels (*الأسفار*), in lands (*البلاد*), regarding the *Qiblah* (*القبلة*), during the hours of the night, and to the path in the routes of the deserts (*البراري*) and the seas (*البحار*). Al-Balkhi (*البلخيّ*) said: There is nothing in His saying **لِتَهْتَدُوا** (that you may be guided) that indicates He did not create them for anything else; rather, He, glory be to Him, created them for great and magnificent matters. And whoever reflects upon the smallness of the smallest among them and the greatness of the largest, and the variation in their positions, their courses, their conjunctions, and their movement, and the manifestation of the benefits of the sun and the moon in the growth of animals and plants, knows that the matter is thus.

(99) وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ مِنَ السَّماءِ مَاءً فَأَخْرَجْنَا (٩٩) ** (99) He is the One who sends down water from the sky, and with it We bring forth [Commentary]: Due to the shift (تلوين) (1) in discourse, بِهِ ** (with it)—meaning, with the water (بالماء)—نَبَاتَ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** (the vegetation of every thing), meaning, the vegetation of every kind of plant (نبت كل شيء من أصناف النبات). The meaning is the manifestation of the Power in causing the different types to grow with a single water, just as He said: يُسْقَى بِمَاءٍ وَاحِدٍ وَنُفَضِّلُ بَعْضَهَا عَلَى بَعْضٍ فِي الْأُكُلِ ** (They are irrigated with a single water, and We give an advantage to some of them over others in taste.)

فَأَخْرَجْنَا مِنْهُ خَضِرًا ** (and We bring forth from it green growth)—meaning, tender, green vegetation (نبتاً غضاً أخضر), which is that which emerges from the seed, branching out. We bring forth from it (from the green growth) حَبًّا مُتَرَاكِبًا ** (grains piled up), where some parts are piled upon others, which is the ear of grain (السّنبل).

وَمِنَ النَّخْلِ مِنْ طَلْعِهَا قِنْوَانٌ ** (and from the date-palm, from its spathe, clusters)—clusters (أعذاق) (2), the plural of قنو (cluster), like صنوان (twin) is the plural of صنو (twin)—دَانِيَةٌ ** (hanging low), meaning, close to being picked (قريبة من التناول). وَجَنَّاتٍ مِنْ أَعْنَابٍ وَالزَّيْتُونَ وَالرُّمَّانَ مُشْتَبِهًا وَغَيْرَ مُتَشَابِهٍ ** (and gardens of grapes, and the olive and the pomegranate, similar and dissimilar). Some of them are similar in form (الهيئة), size (المقدار), color (اللّون), and taste (الطّعم), and some of them are dissimilar.

انْظُرُوا إِلَى ثَمَرِهِ ** (Look at its fruit)—at the fruit of every single one of those things. It was recited with a ḍammah on the thā’ (ثُمرِه), indicating the plural—إِذَا أَثْمَرَ ** (when it bears fruit), meaning, when it produces its fruit, how it is small and insignificant, scarcely benefiting anyone. وَيَنْعِهِ ** (and its ripening)—and at the state of its ripening, or at its ripeness, how it returns large, possessing benefit and pleasure. It is the verbal noun (مصدر) of ينعت (3) (the fruit) when it matures, or it is the plural of يانع (ripe). إِنَّ فِي ذَلِكُمْ لَآيَاتٍ ** (There are indeed signs in that), indicating the existence of an All-Knowing, Wise, Powerful Maker Who determines it, manages it, and transfers it from one state to another, **لِقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ ** (for a people who have faith), for they are the ones who benefit.

footnote (١) The shift (*تلوين*) in discourse (*الخطاب*) is the transition from one style (*أسلوب*) to another, and this is a form of eloquence (*البلاغة*).

footnote (٢) The *عَذق* (with *fatḥah*) is the date palm tree with its load (fruit), and with *kasrah* (*عِذق*) it is the cluster (*القنو*) from it, and the bunch (*العنقود*) of grapes. When what is on it is eaten, they are called *أعذاق* (clusters).

footnote (٣) The fruit *ينع* (ripened) (like *منع* and *ضرب*) *ينعاً* (*yan‘an*), *يُنعاً* (*yun‘an*), and *ينوعاً* (*yunū‘an*) (with *ḍammah* on both) means its harvest time has come, like *أينع*. The *يانع* (ripe) is the red color of everything, and the ripe fruit is like *الينع*, which is the plural of *ينع*.

(١٠٠) وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ شُرَكاءَ الْجِنَ ** (100) Yet they make the jinn partners of Allah, (They are) the angels; they made them rivals (أنداداً) to Allah, so they worshipped them and said that they are the daughters of Allah. He named them jinn (جنّاً) because of their concealment (لاجتنانهم) (1) and to belittle their status, and similar reasons. And they established a kinship (نسبا) between Him and the jinn (الجنّة). And it was said that rather, what is meant by the jinn (الجنّ) are the devils (الشياطين), because they obeyed them just as Allah is obeyed, or they worshipped the idols due to their incitement (بتسويلهِم), and they said that Allah is the Creator of good and Iblis is the creator of evil. وَخَلَقَهُمْ ** though He has created them. And He has created them (2). Meaning: And they knew that Allah is their Creator, not the jinn (الجنّ), and the one who creates is not like the one who does not create. وَخَرَقُوا (٣) لَهُ ** And they falsely attribute to Him And they fabricated for Allah بَنِينَ وَبَناتٍ ** (sons and daughters). For the polytheists said the angels are the daughters of Allah, and the People of the Two Books (أهل الكتابين) said ‘Uzayr (عُزَيْرٌ) is the son of Allah and the Messiah (المسيح) is the son of Allah. And it was read (وقرء) wa kharraqū (وخرّقوا) (with tashdid on the rāʾ) for intensification (للتكثير). بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ (without knowledge), meaning: without knowing the reality of what they said, but rather out of ignorance (جهلاً) of the greatness of Allah. سُبْحانَهُ وَتَعالى عَمَّا يَصِفُونَ ** Exalted is He and far above what they attribute to Him!** Which is that He has a partner or a son.

(١٠١) بَدِيعُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (101) The originator of the heavens and the earth! Meaning, He is their originator (مبدعهما) and their creator (منشؤهما) by His knowledge, initially, not from anything, nor based on a previous example. Thus it is in al-Majma' narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). أَنَّى يَكُونُ لَهُ وَلَدٌ ** (How can He have a child) From where and how can He have a child وَلَمْ تَكُنْ لَهُ صاحِبَةٌ ** (when He has had no consort) from whom the child would come? وَخَلَقَ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ وَهُوَ بِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ عَلِيمٌ ** (and He has created everything, and He has knowledge of all things?) And whoever possesses these attributes is independent (غنيّ) of everything.

(102) ذلِكُمُ ** (That is) the One described by these attributes, اللهُ رَبُّكُمْ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ خالِقُ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ.** (102) That is Allah, your Lord! There is no god except Him, the creator of all things.

In al-Khisal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him).

And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): The actions of the servants are created by a creation of estimation (خلق تقدير), not a creation of formation (خلق تكوين). And Allah is the Creator of all things, and we do not affirm (الجبر) (compulsion) nor (التفويض) (delegation).

فَاعْبُدُوهُ ** (So worship Him), for whoever combines these attributes deserves worship. وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَكِيلٌ ** (and He is the guardian over all things) (He is) a Protector (حفيظ), a Disposer (of affairs) (مدبّر). And it was said: He, along with those attributes, is the Guardian (متوليّ), so entrust them (your affairs) to Him (4), and seek means through His worship to achieve success in your aims, and He is a Watcher (رقيب) over your deeds, so He will recompense you for them.

footnote (١) Meaning: because of their concealment (*إِستتارهم*) by the darkness (*جُنَّة*) of the night.

footnote (٢) (*خلق الإِفك*) (He created the lie) means He fabricated it (*افتراه*), like (*اختلقه*) and (*تخلّقه*).

footnote (٣) (*خرق الرجل*) (The man tore/pierced) means he lied (*كذب*).

footnote (٤) (*وَكَلَ بِاللهِ يَكِلُ*) (He relied upon Allah, He relies) and (*تَوَكَّلَ عَلَيْهِ*) (he relied upon Him), so (*أَوْكَلَ*) and (*اِتَّكَلَ*) (he entrusted and relied) means he submitted to Him (*اِسْتَسْلَمَ إِلَيْهِ*). And (*وَكَلَ إِلَيْهِ الْأَمْرَ وَكْلًا وَوُكُولًا*) (He entrusted the matter to him, an entrusting and a reliance) means he handed it over to him (*سَلَّمَهُ*) and left it (*تَرَكَهُ*).

(١٠٣) لا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصارُ وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ الْأَبْصارَ. (103) The sights do not apprehend Him, but He apprehends the sights.

In al-Kafi and al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "It means the comprehension (إحاطة) of the imagination (الوهم). Do you not see His saying: قَدْ جاءَكُمْ بَصائِرُ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ (There have come to you insights from your Lord)? It does not mean the sight (بصر) of the eyes. فَمَنْ أَبْصَرَ فَلِنَفْسِهِ (So whoever sees, it is to his own benefit). It does not mean seeing with his eye. وَمَنْ عَمِيَ فَعَلَيْها (and whoever is blind, it is to his own detriment). He did not mean the blindness (عمي) of the eyes; rather, He meant the comprehension (إحاطة) of the imagination (الوهم), just as it is said: 'So-and-so is insightful (بصير) regarding poetry,' and 'So-and-so is insightful regarding jurisprudence (الفقه),' and 'So-and-so is insightful regarding dirhams (الدّراهم),' and 'So-and-so is insightful regarding clothes (الثياب).' Allah is greater than to be seen by the eye."

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "The imaginations (أوهام) of the hearts are more subtle (أدق) than the sights (أبصار) of the eyes. You may apprehend with your imagination (وهم) Sind (السند) and Hind (الهند) and the lands which you have not entered and have not apprehended with your sight (بصر). Yet the imaginations (أوهام) of the hearts do not apprehend Him, so how can the sights (أبصار) of the eyes [apprehend Him]?"

In al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), when a man asked him about the Ayahs that were confusing to him: "As for His saying: لا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصارُ وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ الْأَبْصارَ (The sights do not apprehend Him, but He apprehends the sights), it is as He said: لا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصارُ (The sights do not apprehend Him) means that imaginations (الأوهام) do not encompass Him, and وَهُوَ يُدْرِكُ الْأَبْصارَ (but He apprehends the sights) means He encompasses them."

In al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), that He was asked about the disagreement among people concerning the vision (الرؤية). He said: "Whoever describes Allah, the Glorified, contrary to how He described Himself, has committed a great fabrication (الفرية) against Allah. لا تُدْرِكُهُ الْأَبْصارُ (The sights do not apprehend Him). And these sights (الأبصار) are not these eyes (الأعين); rather, they are the sights (الأبصار) that are in the hearts (القلوب). Imaginations (الأوهام) do not fall upon Him. وَهُوَ اللَّطِيفُ (1) الْخَبِيرُ. (and He is the All-subtle, the All-aware.)"

In al-Kafi, al-Tawhid, and al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "As for اللَّطِيفُ (the All-subtle), it is not based on smallness (قلة), thinness (قصافة), or minuteness (صغر). Rather, that is based on penetrating (النفاذ) into things and being impossible to apprehend, like the saying of a man: 'This matter was subtle (لطف) for me,' and 'So-and-so was subtle (لطف) in his doctrine (مذهب) and his saying,' which informs you that the intellect (العقل) was obscured (غمّض) by it, and the pursuit (الطلب) was missed, and it became profound (متعمقاً), subtle (متلطفاً), and the imagination (الوهم) does not apprehend it. Thus, the subtlety (لطف) of Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, is such that He cannot be apprehended by a limit (حدّ) or defined by a description (وصف). Whereas subtlety (اللطافة) from us is minuteness (الصِّغر) and smallness (القلّة). Thus, the name (الاسم) is shared between us, but the meaning (المعنى) differs."

He said: "As for الْخَبِيرُ (the All-aware), He is the One from Whom nothing is absent (لا يغرب عنه) and Whom nothing escapes (لا يفوته). It is not due to experience (التجربة) or reflection (الاعتبار) upon things, such that experience and reflection would grant Him knowledge (علماً), for had it not been for them, He would not have known. This is because whoever is like that is ignorant (جاهلاً). And Allah has always been All-aware (خبيراً) of what He creates. Whereas the الخبير (expert/aware) among people is the one who seeks information (المستخبر) due to the ignorance (جهل) of the learner (المتعلم). Thus, the name (الاسم) is shared between us, but the meaning (المعنى) differs."

(1) In the narration (الحديث): "Indeed, Allah is لطيف (All-subtle), not based on smallness (قلة) or thinness (قصافة)." القصافة with a ḍamma (بالضمّ) and القضف with a ḥarakah (محركة) means thinness (النحافة). القضف means fineness (الدّقة). And قضف with a ḍamma (بالضّم) قصافة means he is قضيف (thin), meaning نحيف (lean), and the plural is قضاف.

(١٠٤) قَدْ جاءَكُمْ بَصائِرُ (104) Certainly, insights have come to you *(١) مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** from your Lord.
