The insight (البصيرة) is to the heart like sight (البصر*) is to the body. فَمَنْ أَبْصَرَ the truth and believes in it, فَلِنَفْسِهِ he sees clearly because its benefit is for his own soul (نفسه). وَمَنْ عَمِيَ to the truth and goes astray, then the burden (وباله) is upon it (the soul). وَما أَنَا عَلَيْكُمْ بِحَفِيظٍ I am only a warner (منذر), and Allah is the Guardian (الحفيظ) over you. He preserves your deeds and rewards you for them. And this is speech that came upon the tongue of the Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(١٠٥) وَكَذلِكَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآياتِ (Thus do We elaborate the signs) We elaborate like that elaboration, and it is the application of the circulating meaning across successive meanings, derived from الصرف (elaboration), which is the transfer of a thing from one state to another. وَلِيَقُولُوا دَرَسْتَ (and so that they may say, 'You have studied'), meaning: We elaborated so that they may say, "You have studied." The ل (lām) is for the consequence (لِلْعَاقِبَة). And الدرس (studying) means reading and learning. And it was recited as دارست (you studied with others), meaning: you studied with the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) and reviewed with them. And دَرَسْتَ ** is from الدروس (becoming worn out), meaning: these verses have become old and effaced, like their saying: "tales of the ancients (أساطير الأوّلين)." Al-Qummi said: Quraysh used to say to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, the news you inform us of, you learn it from the scholars of the Jews and you study it." وَلِنُبَيِّنَهُ (and that We may clarify it). The ل (lām) here is according to its original purpose, because clarification (التّبيين) is the intended goal of the elaboration (التصريف). The pronoun (الضمير) refers to the verses (الآيات) based on the meaning. لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ ** (for a people who know.) For they are the ones who benefit from it. **وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (And disregard the polytheists.) And do not concern yourself (1) with their sayings, nor pay attention to their opinions.

footnote (١) His saying: **بَصائِرُ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** (insights from your Lord), meaning: clear proofs (*حجج بيّنة*), the singular of which is *بصيرة* (insight), which is the indication by which a thing is clearly perceived as it truly is, and it is the light of the heart.

(١٠٧) وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ ما أَشْرَكُوا. (Had Allah willed, they would not have ascribed partners [to Him].)

In al-Majma', in the exegesis narrated from the Household of the Prophet (peace be upon them): وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ (Had Allah willed) that He make all of them believers (مؤمنين), infallible (معصومين), so that no one would disobey Him, then He would not have needed Paradise (جنة) or the Fire (نار). But He commanded them and forbade them and tested them, and He gave them that by which He has the proof (الحجة) against them, namely the means (الألة) and the capacity (الاستطاعة), so that they may deserve the reward (الثواب) and the punishment (العقاب).

Al-Qummi narrated what is close to this: وَما جَعَلْناكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظاً (We have not made you a guardian over them) meaning a watcher (رقيباً). وَما أَنْتَ عَلَيْهِمْ بِوَكِيلٍ (nor are you a trustee over them) meaning one who manages their affairs (تقوم بأمورهم).

(١٠٨) وَلا تَسُبُّوا الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ (108) Do not revile those whom they invoke besides Allah, And do not mention their deities which they worship with the ugly things (القبائح) that are in them, فَيَسُبُّوا اللهَ عَدْواً lest they revile Allah out of spite, transgressing from the truth (الحق) to falsehood (الباطل), بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ without any knowledge. due to ignorance (جهالة) of Allah and of what He loves to be mentioned by.

In al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the saying of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): "Indeed, al-shirk (الشّرك) (polytheism) is more hidden than the creeping (دبيب) (2) of an ant upon a black smooth stone (صفا) (3) on a dark night." So he (peace be upon him) said: "The believers used to revile what the polytheists (المشركون) worshipped besides Allah, and then the polytheists would revile what the believers worshipped. So Allah forbade the believers from reviling their deities, lest the disbelievers (الكفّار) revile the God of the believers, and thus the believers would have committed shirk (أشركوا) with Allah without knowing it."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "Beware of reviling the enemies of Allah where they can hear you, lest they revile Allah out of spite, without any knowledge."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, so he said: "Have you seen anyone reviling Allah?" It was said: "No, and how could that be?" He said: "Whoever reviles the friend (وليّ) of Allah has reviled Allah." And in al-I'tiqadat, narrated from him (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "We see a man in the mosque openly declaring the reviling of your enemies and reviling them." So he said: "What is wrong with him? May Allah curse him! He exposes us." Allah said: وَلا تَسُبُّوا الَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ (Do not revile those whom they invoke) [the rest of] the verse.

He said: And Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said regarding the interpretation (تفسير) of this verse: "Do not revile them, for they will revile you in return." And he said: "Whoever reviles the friend (وليّ) of Allah has reviled Allah." And the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said to Ali (may the blessings of Allah be upon him): "Whoever reviles you has reviled me, and whoever reviles me has reviled Allah, and whoever reviles Allah, Allah will cast him (1) upon his nostrils into the Fire of Hell (نار جهنم)." كَذلِكَ زَيَّنَّا لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ عَمَلَهُمْ Thus did We make the deeds of every nation fair-seeming to them, whether in good or evil.

And al-Qummi [said]: meaning after testing them and their entering into it, so Allah attributed it to Himself. The proof for this, due to their preceding action, is His saying: بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ثُمَّ إِلى رَبِّهِمْ مَرْجِعُهُمْ فَيُنَبِّئُهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ in what they used to do. Then to their Lord will be their return, and He will inform them about what they used to do, through accountability (المحاسبة) and recompense (المجازاة).

** (١٠٩) وَأَقْسَمُوا بِاللهِ جَهْدَ أَيْمانِهِمْ ** (109) They swore by Allah with their most solemn oaths They swore by Him, striving and exerting themselves (in their oaths). Al-Qummi says: meaning Quraysh. ** لَئِنْ جاءَتْهُمْ آيَةٌ ** that if a sign came to them, from their proposed signs. ** لَيُؤْمِنُنَّ بِها قُلْ إِنَّمَا الْآياتُ عِنْدَ اللهِ ** they would surely believe in it. Say: 'The signs are only with Allah.' He is capable of them (the signs); He manifests from them whatever He wills according to the requirement of wisdom. Nothing of them is by my power and will. ** وَما يُشْعِرُكُمْ ** And what makes you know And what makes you know? (This is) an interrogative of denial (استفهام إنكار). ** أَنَّها إِذا جاءَتْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** that when it comes, they will not believe? that the proposed sign (if it comes) they will not believe in it. Meaning: I know ** أَنَّها إِذا جاءَتْ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ ** in it, but you do not know that. It was said: This is because the believers were hopeful for their belief upon the arrival of the sign, wishing for its coming. So Allah, the Glorified, informed them that they do not know what His prior knowledge (علمه) has decreed concerning them, which is that they will not believe. Do you not see His saying: ** كَما لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** (as they did not believe in it the first time)? And it was said that the (لا) is extra (مزيدة). And it was said that (أنّ) means (لعلّ) (perhaps), and this is supported by Ubayy's reading (قراءة أبيّ) of (لعلّها). And it was read (إنّها) with a kasrah (بالكسر), based on the premise that the speech was completed before it, and then He informed them of His knowledge concerning them, and this is clearer. And (لا تؤمنون) was read with a tā’ (بالتاءِ), meaning the address is directed to the polytheists (للمشركين). ** وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ وَأَبْصارَهُمْ ** We will turn their hearts and their sights, This is a conjunction (عطف) upon ** لا يُؤْمِنُونَ . Meaning: And what makes you know that We, at that time, ** نُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ ** (turn their hearts) away from the truth, so they do not comprehend it, ** وَأَبْصارَهُمْ ** (and their eyes), so they do not perceive it, and thus they will not believe in it. ** كَما لَمْ يُؤْمِنُوا بِهِ أَوَّلَ مَرَّةٍ ** just as they did not believe in it the first time, meaning, in what Allah sent down of signs. And Al-Qummi says: meaning, during the time of the dharr (الذّرِّ) and the covenant (الميثاق). ** وَنَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ ** and leave them to wander in their transgression. And We leave them bewildered (متحيرّين), and We do not guide them with the guidance of the believers.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding ** وَنُقَلِّبُ أَفْئِدَتَهُمْ , He says: We invert their hearts (ننكّس قلوبهم), so the bottom of their hearts becomes the top, and their eyes become blind, so they do not perceive the guidance. And Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon him) said: The first thing you are turned away from (تقلّبون عليه) regarding Jihad is Jihad with your hands, then Jihad with your tongues, then Jihad with your hearts. So whoever's heart does not recognize a good deed (معروفاً) and does not reject an evil deed (منكراً), his heart is inverted (انكس قلبه), and its top is made its bottom, and he will never accept good, ever.

footnote (1) *Kababtu fulānan kabban* (*كببت فلاناً كباً*): I threw him upon his face (*ألقيته على وجهه*). Then *akabba huwa* (*فاكبّ هو*) with the *alif* (i.e., the fourth form verb), and this is one of the rare instances where the triliteral root (*ثلاثيها*) is transitive (*يعدّى*) but the quadriliteral form (*رباعيها*) is not.

(١١١) وَلَوْ أَنَّنا نَزَّلْنا إِلَيْهِمُ الْمَلائِكَةَ وَكَلَّمَهُمُ الْمَوْتى وَحَشَرْنا عَلَيْهِمْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ قُبُلاً ** (111) Had We sent down to them the angels, and the dead had spoken to them, and We had gathered before them everything face to face, as they proposed when they said: لَوْ لا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْنَا الْمَلائِكَةُ فَأْتُوا بِآبائِنا أَوْ تَأْتِيَ بِاللهِ وَالْمَلائِكَةِ قَبِيلاً. (Why have angels not been sent down to us? So bring us our fathers, or you bring Allah and the angels as guarantors.) Al-Qummi said regarding قُبُلاً : meaning visibly (عياناً). And it was interpreted with other meanings (معان أخر). And it was recited (وقرئ) as قِبَلاً with the kasrah on the qāf and the fatḥah on the bāʾ, and it carries the same meaning mentioned. ما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَجْهَلُونَ ** they would not have believed, unless Allah had wished. But most of them are ignorant. that if they were given every sign (آية), they would not believe, so they swear by Allah with the utmost of their oaths (جهد ايمانهم) concerning that of which they are unaware (لا يشعرون). For this reason, ignorance (الجهل) was attributed to most of them, even though absolute ignorance (مطلق الجهل) encompasses them all. However, most Muslims are ignorant that they (the disbelievers) will not believe, so they wish for the descent of the sign (الآية) out of hope for their belief. Thus it was said.

(١١٢) وَكَذلِكَ جَعَلْنا لِكُلِّ نَبِيٍّ عَدُوًّا ** (112) And thus did We appoint for every prophet an enemy Meaning, just as We appointed an enemy for you, We appointed an enemy for every prophet who preceded you, in the sense of leaving them and their enemies alone for the purpose of trial (للامتحان). Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah did not send any prophet except that there were two devils (شيطانان) in his nation who harmed him and misled the people after him. As for the two companions of Noah, they were فيطيقوس and حزام. As for the two companions of Abraham, they were مكمّل and رزام. As for the two companions of Moses, they were السّامريّ and مرعقيبا. As for the two companions of Jesus, they were بوليس and مرينون. And as for the two companions of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, they were حبتر and زريق—with the زاي preceding the راء, being the diminutive (مصغّر) of زرق—and الحبتر (with an undotted حاء, then a single-dotted باء, then a two-dotted تاء above, then a راء, on the pattern of جعفر) is the fox (الثعلب). He only referred to them by these names because one of them had a blue eye (لزرق عين أحدهما) and the other was likened to the fox (الثعلب) in his cunning. شَياطِينَ الْإِنْسِ وَالْجِنِ—their rebellious ones (مردتهما)—يُوحِي بَعْضُهُمْ إِلى بَعْضٍ زُخْرُفَ الْقَوْلِ غُرُوراً ** —satans from among humans and jinn—who inspire one another with specious talk, deceitfully. The embellished falsehoods (الأباطيل المموهة) (1), derived from زخرفة (embellishment) when He adorns it. Al-Qummi says regarding بَعْضُهُمْ إِلى بَعْضٍ ** (one another): "Do not believe in the embellished speech (زخرف القول), for this inspiration (الوحي) is a lie." In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a حديث (narration): "Whoever Allah does not make among the people of the description of Truth (صفة الحق), those are the شَياطِينَ الْإِنْسِ وَالْجِنِ (satans from among humans and jinn)." And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Mankind (الإنس) is divided into three parts: one part is under the shade of the Throne on the Day when there is no shade except His shade; one part is subject to accountability (الحساب) and punishment (العذاب); and one part—their faces are the faces of human beings (الآدميين) but their hearts are the hearts of devils (الشياطين)." وَلَوْ شاءَ رَبُّكَ ما فَعَلُوهُ فَذَرْهُمْ وَما يَفْتَرُونَ And had your Lord wished, they would not have done it. So leave them with what they fabricate.**

footnote (١) Meaning, group after group (*قبيلاً قبيلا*). And it is said: openly (*عياناً*) and face-to-face (*قبلاً*), meaning, types (*أصنافاً*), the plural of *قبيل* (type), meaning, kind.

(١١٣) وَلِتَصْغى إِلَيْهِ أَفْئِدَةُ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ وَلِيَرْضَوْهُ وَلِيَقْتَرِفُوا ما هُمْ مُقْتَرِفُونَ ** (113) And so that the hearts of those who do not believe in the Hereafter may incline towards it, and so that they may approve of it, and so that they may commit what they are committing. incline (تميل) أَفْئِدَةُ الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ وَلِيَرْضَوْهُ ** for themselves (لأنفسهم) وَلِيَقْتَرِفُوا and so that they may earn/acquire (وليكتسبُوا) ما هُمْ مُقْتَرِفُونَ ** of sins (من الآثام). (١١٤) أَفَغَيْرَ اللهِ أَبْتَغِي حَكَماً ** (114) Shall I seek a judge other than Allah? Meaning, say to them: Shall I seek anyone other than Allah to judge between me and you, and to distinguish the truthful (المحق) among us from the false one (المبطل)? وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنْزَلَ إِلَيْكُمُ الْكِتابَ ** (when it is He who has sent down to you the Book) the Quran (القرآن), detailed (مُفَصَّلاً), clarifying in it the truth (الحقّ) and the falsehood (الباطل), in a manner that negates confusion (التخليط) and ambiguity (الالتباس). وَالَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ ** (And those whom We have given the Book) the Torah (التوراة) and the Gospel (الإِنجيل) يَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهُ مُنَزَّلٌ مِنْ رَبِّكَ بِالْحَقِ (know that it is sent down by your Lord with the truth) due to its confirmation of what they possess, and due to its confirmation of what they possess, despite the fact that he صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم did not practice their books nor mix with their scholars (علماءهم). فَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُمْتَرِينَ ** (So do not be among the doubters) regarding their knowledge of that, or regarding its being sent down, due to the denial (جحود) of most of them. Thus, it is of the nature of incitement (التهييج), like His saying: وَلا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** (And do not be among the polytheists) which is of the category: I mean you, so listen, O neighbor (إيّاك أعني واسمعي يا جارة).

footnote (١) You say *مَوَّهْتُ الشَّيْءَ* with *بالتّشديد* when you plate something with silver or gold, and beneath that is copper or iron. From this comes *التمويه* (deception/camouflage), which is *التلبيس* (confusion/disguise). And a saying that is *مُمَوَّه* means embellished (*مزخرف*) or mixed (*ممزوج*) with truth (*الحقّ*) and falsehood (*الباطل*).

(١١٥) وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ ** (115) The word of your Lord has been fulfilled That which He spoke concerning the proof (الحجة). And it was read (قُرِئَتْ) as kalimātu Rabbika (the words of your Lord), meaning, His reports, His rulings, and His promises have reached the ultimate limit/perfection. صِدْقاً ** (in truth) in the reports and the promises, وَعَدْلاً ** (and justice) in the decrees (الأقضية) and the rulings (الأحكام). لا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِماتِهِ ** (There is none who can change His words). No one can change anything of them with that which is truer or more just. وَهُوَ السَّمِيعُ ** (and He is the All-hearing) of what they say, the All-knowing (الْعَلِيمُ) of what they conceal (يضمرون).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The Imam hears while in his mother's womb. When he is born, a line is drawn between his shoulders, and in one narration: between his eyes, and in another: on his right upper arm: وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ صِدْقاً وَعَدْلاً ** (The word of your Lord has been fulfilled in truth and justice) (the rest of the Ayah). When the authority (الأمر) devolves upon him, Allah grants Him a pillar of light (عموداً من نور) by which He sees what the people of every town are doing.

And in a narration: It is with this (بهذا) that Allah establishes proof (يحتج) against His creation.

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated that which is close to it.

(١١٦) وَإِنْ تُطِعْ أَكْثَرَ مَنْ فِي الْأَرْضِ يُضِلُّوكَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** (116) If you obey most of those on the earth, they will lead you astray from the way of Allah. Because the majority, generally, follow desires (الأهواء). إِنْ يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَ ** They follow nothing but assumptions, And that is their assumption that their forefathers were rightful and they imitate them, or their ignorances and their corrupt opinions. **وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ ** and they do nothing but surmise. They speak based on conjecture.

(١١٧) إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ مَنْ يَضِلُّ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ ** (117) Your Lord is indeed the best knower of those who stray from His way, meaning, of those who stray (بِمَنْ يَضِلُّ), or it is an interrogative (استفهام). وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُهْتَدِينَ and He is the best knower of the guided. meaning, He is the best knower of both groups (الفريقين).

(١١٨) فَكُلُوا مِمَّا ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ ** (118) So eat of that over which the Name of Allah has been mentioned, This is a consequence (مسبب) of rejecting the following of the misguided (المضلين) who forbid the lawful (الحلال) and permit the unlawful (الحرام). This is because they said to the Muslims: "Do you eat what you have killed, yet you do not eat what your Lord has killed?" So it was said: فَكُلُوا مِمَّا ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللهِ ** (So eat of that over which the Name of Allah has been mentioned) specifically upon its slaughter (ذبحه), excluding what the name of other than Him is mentioned upon, or what died naturally (مات حتف أنفه). إِنْ كُنْتُمْ بِآياتِهِ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** if you are believers in His signs. For belief in them necessitates deeming lawful what Allah has made lawful (استباحة ما أحلّه الله) and avoiding what He has forbidden.

(١١٩) وَما لَكُمْ أَلَّا تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ ** (119) 'Why should you not eat of that over which Allah's Name has been mentioned?' And what purpose do you have in being constrained (تتحرّجوا) from eating it, and what prevents you from it? وَقَدْ فَصَّلَ لَكُمْ ما حَرَّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ ** 'seeing that He has elaborated for you what He has forbidden you,' [This refers to what] He has not forbidden, by His saying: حُرِّمَتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَيْتَةُ ** (Forbidden to you is carrion). And it was read (فُصّل) (with a ḍamma on the fāʾ) based on the passive structure (البناءِ للمفعول), and (حرّم) (with a fatḥa on the ḥāʾ) based on the active structure (البناءِ للفاعل). إِلَّا مَا اضْطُرِرْتُمْ إِلَيْهِ ** 'barring that to which you may be compelled.' [This refers to] that which is forbidden to you, for it is also lawful (حلال) in a state of necessity (حال الضّرورة). وَإِنَّ كَثِيراً لَيُضِلُّونَ ** 'Indeed, many lead astray' by making the forbidden lawful (تحليل الحرام) and making the lawful forbidden (تحريم الحلال). And it was read (لَيُضِلُّونَ) (with a ḍamma on the yāʾ). بِأَهْوائِهِمْ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِالْمُعْتَدِينَ ** 'by their desires, without any knowledge. Indeed your Lord knows best the transgressors.' those who exceed the bounds (المتجاوزين) from the truth (الحق) to falsehood (الباطل) and from the lawful (الحلال) to the forbidden (الحرام).

(١٢٠) وَذَرُوا ظاهِرَ الْإِثْمِ وَباطِنَهُ ** (120) Abandon the manifest sin and the hidden one. That which is declared and that which is concealed. Al-Qummi said: The manifest (الظاهر) of the sin (الإِثم) is disobedience (المعاصي), and the hidden (الباطن) is polytheism (الشِّرك) and doubt (الشكّ) in the heart. إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَكْسِبُونَ الْإِثْمَ سَيُجْزَوْنَ بِما كانُوا يَقْتَرِفُونَ ** Indeed those who commit sin shall be requited for what they used to perpetrate. They commit/do (يعملون).

(١٢١) وَلا تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ (121) And do not eat from that over which Allah’s Name has not been mentioned.

In al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about a Magian (مجوسّي) who said بسم الله (In the Name of Allah) and slaughtered, so he said: "Eat." Then it was said: "A Muslim slaughtered but did not mention the Name (يسمّ)," so he said: "Do not eat. Indeed, Allah says: فَكُلُوا مِمَّا ذُكِرَ اسْمُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ وَلا تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ (So eat of that over which Allah’s Name has been mentioned, and do not eat of that over which Allah’s Name has not been mentioned)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about the slaughtered animals of the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), so he said: "There is no harm if Allah’s Name has been mentioned over it, but I mean those among them who follow the way of Moses and Jesus (peace be upon them both)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the slaughtered animals of the Jews and the Christians, so he said: "The slaughter (الذبيحة) is a name (اسم), and no one is trusted with the Name (الاسم) except a Muslim."

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding the slaughtered animal of the Nasibi (الناصب), the Jew, and the Christian, he said: "Do not eat his slaughtered animal until you hear him mention the Name of Allah over it. Have you not heard the saying of Allah: وَلا تَأْكُلُوا مِمَّا لَمْ يُذْكَرِ اسْمُ اللهِ عَلَيْهِ (And do not eat from that over which Allah’s Name has not been mentioned)?"

I say: This narration clarifies the preceding one, rules upon both of them, and details their general meaning, just as the first one rules upon it, and the three reconcile all that has been reported concerning this meaning, despite its abundance and variation.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who slaughtered but did not mention the Name (يسمّ), so he said: "If he was forgetful (ناسياً), let him mention the Name when he remembers and say: بسم الله على أوّله وآخره (In the Name of Allah, upon its beginning and its end)."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): If a Muslim slaughters but does not mention the Name and forgets, then eat from his slaughtered animal and mention the Name of Allah over what you eat.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man who slaughtered and then glorified Allah (سبّح) or magnified Him (كبّر) or declared His oneness (هلّل) or praised Him (حمد الله). He said: "All of this is from the Names of Allah, the Exalted, and there is no harm in it." وَإِنَّهُ لَفِسْقٌ (And indeed, that is transgression.)

And indeed, the transgression (الفسق) is that over which the name of other than Allah has been invoked, due to His Almighty saying: أَوْ فِسْقاً أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللهِ بِهِ وَإِنَّ الشَّياطِينَ (1) لَيُوحُونَ (or transgression over which the name of other than Allah has been invoked). And indeed, the devils (1) inspire (ليوسوسون - whisper) their allies (from the disbelievers) to argue with you (by their saying: You eat what you and your hunting animals have killed, but you abandon what Allah has killed). وَإِنْ أَطَعْتُمُوهُمْ (And if you obey them) (in deeming lawful what He has forbidden), إِنَّكُمْ لَمُشْرِكُونَ (you will indeed be polytheists). For whoever abandons the obedience of Allah for the obedience of another and follows him in his religion has indeed associated partners with Allah.

(١٢٢) وَمَنْ كانَ مَيْتاً ** (122) Is he who was dead, and it was read with tashdid (doubling the ya' in mayyitan) فَأَحْيَيْناهُ وَجَعَلْنا لَهُ نُوراً يَمْشِي بِهِ فِي النَّاسِ كَمَنْ مَثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُماتِ لَيْسَ بِخارِجٍ مِنْها ** (122) Is he who was dead, then We revived him and made for him a light by which he walks among the people, like someone whose likeness is in the darkness, and he cannot leave it? meaning, the likeness of one whom Allah guided and saved from error (الضلالة) and made for him a proof (حجة) by the light of which he guides, is like the likeness of one whose characteristic is remaining in error (الضلالة), never leaving it under any circumstance, ever. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): مَيْتاً ** (dead) means one who knows nothing, and نُوراً يَمْشِي بِهِ فِي النَّاسِ ** (a light by which he walks among the people) means an Imam (إماماً) who is followed, كَمَنْ مَثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُماتِ ** (like someone whose likeness is in the darkness) means one who does not know the Imam (الإمام). And al-Ayyashi narrated the same. And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The dead one (الميت) is he who does not know this matter (الشأن), meaning this affair (الأمر). وَجَعَلْنا لَهُ نُوراً ** (and made for him a light) means an Imam (إماماً) whom he follows, meaning Ali ibn Abi Talib (may the blessings of Allah be upon him). كَمَنْ مَثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُماتِ ** (like someone whose likeness is in the darkness). He said, gesturing with his hand like this: this is the creation (الخلق) who know nothing. And in al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was dead concerning us, فَأَحْيَيْناهُ ** (then We revived him) through us. And al-Qummi said: He was ignorant of the Truth (الحق) and the Guardianship (الولاية), so We guided him to it. He said the Light (النور) is the Guardianship (الولاية). In the darkness (الظلمات) means the guardianship (ولاية) of others besides the Imams (peace be upon them). In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: Allah the Almighty يُخْرِجُ الْحَيَّ مِنَ الْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ الْمَيِّتَ مِنَ الْحَيِّ ** (He brings forth the living from the dead, and brings forth the dead from the living). The living (الحيّ) is the believer (المؤمن) whose clay (طينته) comes forth from the clay (طينة) of the disbeliever (الكافر). And the dead (الميّت) who comes forth from the living (الحيّ) is the disbeliever (الكافر) who comes forth from the clay (طينة) of the believer (المؤمن*). Thus, the living is the believer and the dead is the disbeliever. And that is His saying, exalted is His glory: أَوَمَنْ كانَ مَيْتاً فَأَحْيَيْناهُ ** (Is he who was dead, then We revived him?). His death (موته) was the mixing of his clay with the clay of the disbeliever, and his life (حيوته) was when Allah separated them both by His word (بكلمته). Likewise, Allah, exalted is His glory, brings forth the believer at birth (في الميلاد) from the darkness (الظلمة) to the Light (النور) after his entry into it, and He brings forth the disbeliever from the Light to the darkness after his entry into the Light. And that is His saying, exalted is His glory: لِيُنْذِرَ مَنْ كانَ حَيًّا وَيَحِقَّ الْقَوْلُ عَلَى الْكافِرِينَ (That he may warn whosoever is living, and that the word may be due against the faithless). كَذلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِلْكافِرِينَ ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ. (122) Thus what they have been doing is made to seem decorous to the faithless. In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The Ayah was revealed concerning Ammar ibn Yasir and Abu Jahl.

(١٢٣) وَكَذلِكَ جَعَلْنا فِي كُلِّ قَرْيَةٍ أَكابِرَ مُجْرِمِيها لِيَمْكُرُوا فِيها ** (And thus We appointed in every town its major criminals that they may scheme in it) That is, just as We appointed in Mecca (مكّة). And the meaning is that We left them to their own devices (وشأنهم) so that they might scheme, and We did not restrain them from scheming. He specified the major criminals (الأكابر) only because they are more capable of leading people astray (استتباع الناس) and scheming against them. وَما يَمْكُرُونَ إِلَّا بِأَنْفُسِهِمْ ** (and they scheme only against themselves) Because its evil consequence (وباله) encompasses (١) them. **وَما يَشْعُرُونَ ** (but they are not aware) of that.

(١٢٤) وَإِذا جاءَتْهُمْ آيَةٌ قالُوا (When a sign comes to them, they say) Al-Qummi said: The elders said: "We will not believe until we are given the like of what the messengers of Allah were given." It is narrated that Abu Jahl said: "Banu 'Abd Manaf competed with us (2) in honor until we became like two horses in a race. Then they said: 'A prophet from among us receives revelation.' By Allah, we will never be pleased with him nor follow him unless revelation comes to us just as it comes to him." So, [this verse] was revealed. And similar to this is His mighty saying: بَلْ يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امْرِئٍ مِنْهُمْ أَنْ يُؤْتى صُحُفاً مُنَشَّرَةً (Rather, every one of them desires to be given open scrolls) اللهُ أَعْلَمُ حَيْثُ يَجْعَلُ رِسالَتَهُ (Allah knows best where to place His apostleship.) This is a new statement (استيناف) in refutation of them, [stating] that prophethood is not based on lineage (النّسب) or wealth (المال), but rather it is based on spiritual virtues (فضائل نفسانيّة) with which Allah singles out whom He wills among His servants. Thus, He chooses for His apostleship the one He knows is fit for it, and He is best aware of the place where He should put it. And it was read (وقرئ) as رسالاته (His apostleships/messages). سَيُصِيبُ الَّذِينَ أَجْرَمُوا صَغارٌ (Soon there will befall those who committed sins, abasement) - meaning humiliation (ذل) and contempt (حقارة) after their arrogance - عِنْدَ اللهِ (in the sight of Allah) on the Day of Resurrection. And it was said: [meaning] from Allah. وَعَذابٌ شَدِيدٌ بِما كانُوا يَمْكُرُونَ (and a severe punishment because of the plotting they used to do.) Al-Qummi said: Meaning, they disobey Allah in secret (في السِّرّ).

footnote (١) **The Almighty's saying:** **وَحاقَ بِهِمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ** (and what they used to ridicule encompassed them) means: it surrounded them (*أحاط بهم*) and descended (*وحلّ*). It is said: *حاق بهم العذاب حيقاً* (the punishment encompassed them *ḥayqan*) when it descended (*إذا نزل*), and *الْحَيْق* (*al-ḥayq*) is the descent of affliction (*نزول البلاء*).

footnote (٢) His saying: *تزاحمنا* (competed with us) means: the matter became difficult (*ضايقنا الأمر*) for them in every respect, and we did not fall short of them in honor (*في شرف*) until we became like two competing horses (*كالفرسين المتسابقين*) in the field of racing (*ميدان الاستباق*), each concerned with surpassing the other. Therefore, we will never concede to them an honor (*شرفاً*) for which we do not have the like, so we will not believe in the signs (*الآيات*) revealed concerning them unless the like of them is revealed concerning us, so that we do not fall short of them.

(١٢٥) فَمَنْ يُرِدِ اللهُ أَنْ يَهْدِيَهُ ** (Then whomever Allah desires to guide) He makes him know the truth and grants him success (يُوَفِّقُهُ) for faith يَشْرَحْ صَدْرَهُ لِلْإِسْلامِ ** (He expands his breast for Islam), so that it becomes wide for it and its scope is broadened within it. This is a metaphor (كِنَايَة) for making the heart receptive (قَابِلًا) to the truth, prepared for its dwelling within it, and purified from what prevents it or contradicts it.

In al-Majma', the authentic narration (الرِّوَايَة الصَّحِيحَة) has been reported: When this verse was revealed, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was asked about the expansion of the breast (شَرْح الصَّدْر) and what it is. He said: "It is a light that Allah the Almighty casts into the heart of the believer, so his breast expands and widens." They asked: "Is there a sign (أَمَارَة) by which it is known?" He said: "Yes, turning in repentance (الإِنَابَة) toward the Abode of Eternity (دَار الْخُلُود), distancing oneself (التَّجَافِي) from the Abode of Delusion (دَار الْغُرُور), and preparing for death before its arrival."

وَمَنْ يُرِدْ أَنْ يُضِلَّهُ (1) (But whomever He desires to lead astray) He makes his breast narrow (ضَيِّقًا) and constricted (حَرَجًا), such that it recoils from accepting the truth, and faith does not enter it.

It was read (قُرِئَ) as ضَيْقًا (with takhfif—lightening the yā’) and حَرِجًا (with kasr—the vowel i on the rā’), meaning extremely narrow (شَدِيد الضِّيق).

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, he said: "It may be ضَيِّقاً ** (narrow), yet it has an opening (مَنْفَذ) through which one hears and sees. But the حَرَج (al-ḥaraj) is that which is completely sealed (الْمُلْتَئِم) and has no opening (مَنْفَذ) through which one hears or sees."

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He said to Musa ibn Asmar: "Do you know what الْحَرَج is?" I said: "No." So He gestured with His hand and closed His fingers, like a solid object (الشَّيْء الْمُصْمَت) (2) into which nothing enters and from which nothing exits.

كَأَنَّما يَصَّعَّدُ فِي السَّماءِ ** (as if he were climbing up to the sky). It is يَتَصَعَّد (yataṣa‘‘ad). And it was read (وَقُرِئَ) with takhfif (lightening) and يُصَاعِد (yuṣā‘id), meaning يَتَصَاعَد (yataṣā‘ad), as an exaggeration of the narrowness of his breast, by likening him to one who attempts what he is unable to do, for ascending the sky is an example of what is far from one's ability (الِاسْتِطَاعَة) and difficult (يَضِيق) to achieve. كَذلِكَ يَجْعَلُ اللهُ الرِّجْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ.** (Thus does Allah lay defilement upon those who do not have faith.)

In al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It (الرِّجْس) is doubt (الشَّك).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The heart certainly moves (لَيَتَخَلْخَل) (1) within the chest, seeking the truth. When it finds it, it becomes tranquil (اِطْمَأَنَّ) and settles by it. Then He recited **فَمَنْ يُرِدِ اللهُ أَنْ يَهْدِيَهُ ** (Then whomever Allah desires to guide) [the rest of] the verse.

And al-Ayyashi narrates the like of it. And in one narration, He said: "The heart turns over (يَنْقَلِب) from its place up to the throat as long as it has not found the truth. When it finds the truth, it settles." Then He recited this verse.

And in al-Majma', narrated from him (peace be upon him): The like of it.

I say: يَتَخَلْخَل (yataḥalḥal) is with two dotted khā’s (الْخَاءَيْن الْمُعْجَمَتَيْن) or two jīm’s (الْجِيمَيْن), meaning it moves (يَتَحَرَّك).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, desires good for a servant, He marks (نَكَتَ) (2) a spot (نُكْتَة) of light in his heart, and his hearing and heart become illuminated by it, until he is more eager for what is in your hands than you are. And when He desires evil for a servant, He marks a black spot (نُكْتَة سَوْدَاء) in his heart, and his hearing and heart become darkened by it. Then He recited **فَمَنْ يُرِدِ اللهُ أَنْ يَهْدِيَهُ ** (Then whomever Allah desires to guide) [the rest of] the verse.

And in it (al-Kafi), and in al-Tawhid and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, desires good for a servant, He marks a spot of light in his heart, opens the passages of his heart (مَسَامِع قَلْبِهِ), and assigns an angel (مَلَكًا) to guide him rightly (يُسَدِّدُهُ). And when He desires evil for a servant, He marks a black spot in his heart, closes the passages of his heart, and assigns a devil (شَيْطَانًا) to lead him astray. Then He recited this verse.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a ḥadīth: "And know that when Allah desires good for a servant, Allah expands his breast for Islam شَرَحَ اللهُ صَدْرَهُ لِلْإِسْلَامِ . When He grants him that, his tongue speaks the truth, and his heart is bound to it, so he acts upon it. When Allah combines this for him, his Islam is perfected, and if he dies upon that state, he is truly among the Muslims in the sight of Allah. But if Allah does not desire good for a servant, He entrusts him to himself, and his breast becomes صَدْرَهُ ضَيِّقاً حَرَجاً ** (narrow and constricted). If truth flows upon his tongue, his heart is not bound to it. And if his heart is not bound to it, Allah does not grant him the ability to act upon it. If this state persists until he dies, he is among the hypocrites (الْمُنَافِقِين) in the sight of Allah. And the truth that flowed upon his tongue, which Allah did not grant him to bind his heart to or grant him the ability to act upon, becomes a proof (حُجَّة) (1) against him. Therefore, fear Allah and ask Him to expand your breasts for Islam and to make your tongues speak with wisdom (الْحِكْمَة) until He takes your souls while you are upon that state."

And in al-Tawhid, al-Ma'ani, and al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: فَمَنْ يُرِدِ اللهُ أَنْ يَهْدِيَهُ ** (Then whomever Allah desires to guide) through his faith in this world and to His Paradise and the Abode of His Generosity (دَار كَرَامَتِهِ) in the Hereafter, يَشْرَحْ صَدْرَهُ ** (He expands his breast) for submission (التَّسْلِيم) to Allah, trust in Him, and tranquility regarding the reward He has promised, until he finds peace (يَطْمَئِنّ) with Him through it. وَمَنْ يُرِدْ أَنْ يُضِلَّهُ ** (But whomever He desires to lead astray) from His Paradise and the Abode of His Generosity in the Hereafter, due to his disbelief in Him and his disobedience to Him in this world, يَجْعَلْ صَدْرَهُ ضَيِّقاً حَرَجاً ** (He makes his breast narrow and constricted), until he doubts his disbelief (يَشُكّ فِي كُفْرِهِ) and is disturbed by the conviction of his heart, until he becomes كَأَنَّما يَصَّعَّدُ فِي السَّماءِ كَذلِكَ يَجْعَلُ اللهُ الرِّجْسَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ. (as if he were climbing up to the sky. Thus does Allah lay defilement upon those who do not have faith.)

(1) It is not permissible that the meaning of leading astray (الإِضْلَال) in the verse is calling to misguidance (الضَّلَال), or commanding it, or compelling (إِجْبَار) it, due to the consensus (إِجْمَاع) of the Ummah that Allah the Almighty does not command misguidance nor call to it, so how could He compel it, when calling to it is easier than compelling it? Allah has indeed condemned Pharaoh and the Samiri for their leading astray from the religion of guidance in His saying: وَأَضَلَّ فِرْعَوْنُ قَوْمَهُ وَما هَدى ** (Pharaoh led his people astray, and did not guide [them]) and His saying: وَأَضَلَّهُمُ السَّامِرِيُ ** (and the Samiri led them astray). And there is no disagreement that their leading astray was a leading astray involving command, compulsion, and calling, and Allah the Almighty condemned them absolutely for it. So how about what He condemned others for?

(2) الْمُصْمَت (al-muṣmat) is that which has no cavity (جَوْف).

(1) لَيَتَخَلْخَل (layatakhalḥal) means it moves (يَتَحَرَّك) and is agitated (يَضْطَرِب), like a worn ankle bracelet (الْخَلْخَال).

(2) The نُكْتَة (nuktah) in a thing is like a dot (النُّقْطَة), and the plural is نُقَط (nuqaṭ).

(١٢٦) **وَهذا صِراطُ رَبِّكَ مُسْتَقِيماً قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآياتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ ** (126) This is the straight path of your Lord. We have certainly elaborated the signs for a people who take admonition.

The Almighty's saying: وَهذا صِراطُ ** رَبِّكَ ** It is said that it means His way (طريقته) and His custom (وعادته) regarding granting success (التوفيق) and abandonment (الخذلان). مُسْتَقِيماً ** Just (عادلاً), consistent (مطّرداً), with no crookedness (اعوجاج) in it. Al-Qummi said: It means the clear path (الطريق الواضح). قَدْ فَصَّلْنَا الْآياتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَذَّكَّرُونَ ** So they know that the All-Powerful (القادر) is Allah, and that everything that occurs, whether good or evil (خير أو شرٍّ), is by His decree (بقضائه), and that He is All-Knowing (عليم) of the conditions of the servants (أحوال العباد), Wise (حكيم), and Just (عدل) in what He does with them.

(١٢٧) لَهُمْ ** (For them) refers to those who remembered (تذكروا) and recognized the truth (وعرفوا الحق). دارُ السَّلامِ ** (is the abode of peace) means the Abode of Allah (دار الله) or the Abode of Safety (دار السّلامَة) from every affliction (آفة) and calamity (بليّة). Al-Qummi (said): It means in Paradise (الجنّة), and al-Salām (السَّلامِ) means security (الأمان), well-being (العافية), and joy (السّرور). A narration concerning the first meaning will come in Surah Yunus. عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ ** (with their Lord) means under His guarantee (في ضمانه); He will certainly deliver them to it (يوصلهم إليها لا مجالة). وَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُمْ ** (and He is their guardian). It was said: He is their Master (مولاهم) and their Lover (ومحبّهم). Al-Qummi (said): Meaning, He is more worthy of them (أولى بهم). بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (because of what they used to do) means due to their deeds (بسَبَب أعمالهم).

لَهُمْ دارُ السَّلامِ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ وَهُوَ وَلِيُّهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (١٢٧) (127) For them is the abode of peace with their Lord, and He is their guardian because of what they used to do.

footnote (١) For indeed, knowledge, if it is not accompanied by action (*العمل*), is an adversary (*مخاصِم*) to its possessor.

footnote (٢) The Straight Path (*الصراط*), which consists of the proofs of truth (*أدلة الحق*), is described as straightness (*الاستقامة*) despite the variation in the aspects of the proofs (*وجوه الأدلة*), because even with their variation, they lead to the truth (*الحق*). Thus, it is as if it is a single path, due to the soundness of all of them from contradiction (*التناقض*) and corruption (*الفساد*).

(١٢٨) وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعاً ** (128) The day He will muster them all And remember the Day We muster them, or the Day He will muster them (it was read with the yāʾ (الياء)), then We will say: يا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِ ** (O company of jinn)—meaning the devils (الشياطين)—**قَدِ اسْتَكْثَرْتُمْ مِنَ الْإِنْسِ ** (You have certainly sought abundance from mankind), meaning: you led many of them astray.

Al-Qummi said: Everyone who allies himself with a people belongs to them, even if he is not of their kind (جنس). وَقالَ أَوْلِياؤُهُمْ مِنَ الْإِنْسِ ** (and their friends from among the humans will say)—those who followed them and obeyed them—رَبَّنَا اسْتَمْتَعَ بَعْضُنا بِبَعْضٍ ** (Our Lord, we have enjoyed one another,) Meaning: mankind benefited from the devils (الشياطين) when they guided them toward desires (الشهوات) and what leads to them, and the devils benefited from mankind when they obeyed them and achieved their goal (مرادهم). وَبَلَغْنا أَجَلَنَا الَّذِي أَجَّلْتَ لَنا. and we have reached the term that You set for us.' Al-Qummi said: Meaning the Day of Resurrection (القيامة). قالَ ** (He will say)—Allah said to them—النَّارُ مَثْواكُمْ ** (The Fire is your abode), meaning your station (مقامكم), خالِدِينَ فِيها ** (abiding therein), meaning eternally (مؤبّدين), إِلَّا ما شاءَ اللهُ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ حَكِيمٌ ** (except what Allah wishes. Indeed your Lord is all-wise,) in His actions, عَلِيمٌ ** (all-knowing.) of the deeds of the two weighty ones (الثقلين) (1) and their states.

(١٢٩) وَكَذلِكَ نُوَلِّي بَعْضَ الظَّالِمِينَ بَعْضاً بِما كانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ ** (And thus do We make some wrongdoers allies of others because of what they used to earn.) We entrust some of them to others. Al-Qummi said regarding نُوَلِّي : Everyone who appoints their allies (أولياءهم), they will be with them. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Allah did not take vengeance upon a wrongdoer except through a wrongdoer, and that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَكَذلِكَ نُوَلِّي بَعْضَ الظَّالِمِينَ بَعْضاً.**

يا مَعْشَرَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنْسِ أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِنْكُمْ يَقُصُّونَ عَلَيْكُمْ آياتِي وَيُنْذِرُونَكُمْ لِقاءَ يَوْمِكُمْ هذا ** (130) 'O company of jinn and humans! Did there not come to you apostles from among yourselves, relating My signs to you, and warning you of the encounter of this day of yours?' meaning the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). In al-Uyun, in the narration of the man from Sham (الشّامي), that he asked the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Did Allah send a prophet to the jinn (الجنّ)?" He replied: "Yes, Allah sent a prophet called Yusuf, and he called them to Allah, but they killed him."

And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) in a hadith: "Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم to the jinn (الجنّ) and the humans (الإنس)."

I say: And the universality of His message to the two weighty ones (الثقلين) is widespread (مستفيض). قالُوا شَهِدْنا عَلى أَنْفُسِنا ** They will say, 'We bear witness against ourselves.' with the crime (الجرم) and disobedience (العصيان), and this is an admission from them of disbelief (الكفر) and deserving the punishment (العذاب). وَغَرَّتْهُمُ الْحَياةُ الدُّنْيا وَشَهِدُوا The life of the world deceived them, and they bore witness عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ كانُوا كافِرِينَ ** against themselves that they had been faithless. This is a condemnation of them for their poor insight and erroneous opinion, for they were deceived by the worldly life (الحيوة الدّنيا) and the fleeting pleasures (اللذات المخدجة) (1), and they turned away entirely from the Hereafter (الآخرة), until the consequence of their affair was that they were compelled to bear witness against themselves regarding disbelief (الكفر) and submission to the eternal punishment (العذاب المخلّد), serving as a warning to the listeners against a state similar to theirs.

footnote (١) *Al-Thiql* (*الثقل*), with a *fatḥah* on the *qāf* (*محركة*), refers to the traveler's belongings and his retinue (*حشمه*), and everything precious and protected. From this is the *hadith*: "Indeed, I am leaving among you the two weighty ones (*الثقلين*): the Book of Allah and my progeny (*عترتي*)." And the two weighty ones (*الثقلان*) are the humans (*الإنس*) and the jinn (*الجنّ*).

(١٣١) ذلِكَ أَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ رَبُّكَ مُهْلِكَ الْقُرى بِظُلْمٍ وَأَهْلُها غافِلُونَ (131) That is because your Lord would not destroy the towns unjustly while their inhabitants were unaware.

ذلِكَ meaning, the sending of the Messengers, أَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ because your Lord would not be the destroyer of the towns unjustly (بِظُلْمٍ), (meaning) being unjust (ظالماً) or due to an injustice (بسبب ظلم) they committed, وَأَهْلُها غافِلُونَ while their inhabitants were unaware (غافِلُونَ), (meaning) they had not been warned (لم ينبّهوا) by a Messenger (برسول).

(١٣٢) وَلِكُلٍ دَرَجاتٌ مِمَّا عَمِلُوا وَما رَبُّكَ بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ (132) For everyone there are ranks according to what they have done, and your Lord is not oblivious of what they do.

وَلِكُلٍ among those held responsible (المكلفين), دَرَجاتٌ ranks (مراتب), مِمَّا عَمِلُوا وَما رَبُّكَ بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ so that an action or the measure of what one deserves of reward (ثواب) or punishment (عقاب) would be hidden from Him. And it was recited with the second-person address (بالخطاب).

(١٣٣) وَرَبُّكَ الْغَنِيُّ ذُو الرَّحْمَةِ إِنْ يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَسْتَخْلِفْ مِنْ بَعْدِكُمْ ما يَشاءُ كَما أَنْشَأَكُمْ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ قَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ (133) Your Lord is the All-sufficient, the possessor of mercy. If He wishes, He will take you away and replace you with whomever He wishes after you, just as He produced you from the progeny of another people.

وَرَبُّكَ الْغَنِيُّ independent of His servants and their worship, ذُو الرَّحْمَةِ the possessor of mercy. He shows mercy to them through the imposition of duties (بالتكليف) so that He may expose them (2) to the great benefits (المنافع العظيمة) which it is not proper to deliver to them except through deservingness (بالاستحقاق). إِنْ يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ O disobedient ones (أيها العُصاة), وَيَسْتَخْلِفْ مِنْ بَعْدِكُمْ ما يَشاءُ and He will bring forth, after your destruction (هلاككم) and removal (اذهابكم), a creation other than you who will obey Him, and they will be successors (خلفاً) to you, كَما أَنْشَأَكُمْ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ قَوْمٍ آخَرِينَ generation after generation (قرناً بعد قرن).

(١٣٤) إِنَّ ما تُوعَدُونَ ** (What you are promised will surely come, and you cannot frustrate [Him].) [What you are promised] refers to the Gathering (الحشر), the reward (الثواب), and the punishment (العقاب). لَآتٍ ** (will surely come) means it is certainly going to happen (لكائن لا محالة). وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ ** (and you cannot frustrate [Him]) means you are not escaping His dominion (بخارجين من ملكه). It is said: (أعجزني كذا) meaning "such-and-such escaped me (فاتني) and preceded me (وسبقني)." (١٣٥) قُلْ يا قَوْمِ اعْمَلُوا عَلى مَكانَتِكُمْ ** (Say, 'O my people! Act according to your ability; I too am acting. Soon you will know for whom the ultimate outcome of the abode will be. Indeed, the wrongdoers will not be felicitous.') It was said: [It means] according to the utmost limit of your capability (تمكّنكم) and power (استطاعتكم), or according to the state (حالكم) upon which you are. And the reading (وقرئ) is (مكاناتكم) wherever it occurs. إِنِّي عامِلٌ ** (I too am acting) according to my ability (مكانتي) upon which I am. This is a threat (تهديد). The meaning is: persist in your disbelief (كفركم) and enmity (عداوتكم), for I am persistent in Islam and in enduring you (مصابرتكم). فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ تَكُونُ لَهُ عاقِبَةُ الدَّارِ ** (Soon you will know for whom the ultimate outcome of the abode will be) meaning: which of us will have the good outcome (العاقبة الحسنى) for which Allah created this abode (الدار). And the word (يكون) is read with a yā (بالياءِ). The threat is presented in the imperative form (بصيغة الأمر) as an exaggeration in the warning (الوعيد) and a confirmation against the one commanded (المأمور) that nothing but evil will come from him. This is like His saying, the Almighty: اعْمَلُوا ما شِئْتُمْ إِنَّهُ لا يُفْلِحُ الظَّالِمُونَ ** (Act as you wish. Indeed, the wrongdoers will not be felicitous). He placed the wrongdoers (الظالمين) in the position of the disbelievers (الكافرين) because it is more general (أَعَمّ) and more beneficial (أكثر فائِدة).

footnote (١) In the narration (*الخبر*): "Every prayer (*صلاة*) in which *Fātiḥat al-Kitāb* is not recited is *khidāj* (*خداج*)," meaning deficiency (*نقصان*). It is described using the verbal noun (*المصدر*) for emphasis (*للمبالغة*). It is said: (*خدجت النّاقة فهي خادج*) meaning the she-camel aborted her young before the completion of the days, even if the creation was complete. And in the *ḥadīth* of Ali (peace be upon him): Regarding *Dhū al-Thudayyah* (*ذي الثدية*), he was *makhdaj al-yad* (*مخدج اليد*), meaning deficient in the hand (*ناقص اليد*).

footnote (٢) The Almighty's saying: **وَعَرَضْنا جَهَنَّمَ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلْكافِرِينَ عَرْضاً ** (and We shall expose hell to the faithless that day, exposed) means We made it manifest (*أظهرناها*) so that the disbelievers (*الكفّار*) may see it. It is said: (*عرضت الشيء فأعرض*) meaning I made the thing manifest (*أظهرته*) and so it became manifest (*فَظهَرَ*).

(١٣٦) وَجَعَلُوا لِلَّهِ (136) And they set aside for Allah meaning the polytheists of the Arabs مِمَّا ذَرَأَ ** out of what Allah created مِنَ الْحَرْثِ وَالْأَنْعامِ نَصِيباً فَقالُوا هذا لِلَّهِ بِزَعْمِهِمْ ** without being commanded to do so وَهذا لِشُرَكائِنا —meaning their idols which they associated with Him in their wealth. And it was recited with the ḍammah on the zāy (زُعْمِهِمْ), and likewise in what follows. فَما كانَ لِشُرَكائِهِمْ فَلا يَصِلُ إِلَى اللهِ وَما كانَ لِلَّهِ فَهُوَ يَصِلُ إِلَى شُرَكائِهِمْ ساءَ ما يَحْكُمُونَ ** (136) out of what He created of the crops and the cattle a share. And they say, 'This is for Allah'—so they claim—'and this is for our partners.' As for what is for their partners, it does not reach Allah, but as for what is for Allah, it reaches their partners. Evil is the judgement that they make! This is their judgment. It is narrated that they used to designate a portion of the crops and produce for Allah, and they would spend it on guests and the needy, and a portion of both for their deities, spending on their custodians (سَدَنتها) (1) and sacrificing beside them. Then, if they saw that what they had designated for Allah was purer (أزكى), they would exchange it for what was designated for their deities. But if they saw that what was designated for their deities was purer (أزكى), they would leave it for them, out of love for their deities, and they excused this by saying that Allah is Self-sufficient (غنيّ).

And in al-Majma', narrated from our Imams (peace be upon them): If what was designated for the idols became mixed with what was designated for Allah, they would return it (to the idols' share). But if what was designated for Allah became mixed with what they designated for the idols, they would leave it, saying, "Allah is Self-sufficient (غنيّ)." And if the water broke through from the share designated for Allah into the share designated for the idols, they would not block it. But if it broke through (2) from the share designated for the idols into the share designated for Allah, they would block it, saying, "Indeed, Allah is Self-sufficient (غنيّ)."

It is said that in His saying مِمَّا ذَرَأَ ** (out of what He created) there is an alert to the extremity of their ignorance, for they associated with the Creator in His creation an inanimate object (جماداً) that has no power over anything, and then they preferred it over Him by making the purer portion (الزّاكي) for it.

(١٣٧) وَكَذلِكَ ** (137) AND THUS And similar to that beautification (التّزيين), زَيَّنَ لِكَثِيرٍ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ قَتْلَ أَوْلادِهِمْ ** HAVE THEIR PARTNERS MADE THE KILLING OF THEIR CHILDREN SEEM DECOROUS TO MANY OF THE POLYTHEISTS, by way of burying alive (الوأد) (3) for fear of poverty (العيلة) or shame (العار), or by slaughtering (النحر) them for their deities (آلهتهم). شُرَكاؤُهُمْ ** THEIR PARTNERS from the devils (الشياطين) or the custodians (السدنة). لِيُرْدُوهُمْ ** THAT THEY MAY RUIN THEM, so that they may destroy them (يهلكوهم) through misguidance (الإغواء). وَلِيَلْبِسُوا عَلَيْهِمْ دِينَهُمْ ** AND CONFUSE THEIR RELIGION FOR THEM. and so that they may mix up (يخلطوا) for them what they were upon. وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ ما فَعَلُوهُ فَذَرْهُمْ وَما يَفْتَرُونَ. HAD ALLAH WISHED, THEY WOULD NOT HAVE DONE IT. SO LEAVE THEM AND WHAT THEY FABRICATE.**

(١٣٨) وَقالُوا هذِهِ أَنْعامٌ وَحَرْثٌ حِجْرٌ لا يَطْعَمُها إِلَّا مَنْ نَشاءُ بِزَعْمِهِمْ ** (And they say: these cattle and tillage are forbidden, no one shall eat of them except those whom we wish, so they claim) This refers to what they designated for their deities. حِجْرٌ ** means unlawful (حرام). بِزَعْمِهِمْ ** means without any proof. Al-Qummi said: They used to forbid them to a group of people. وَأَنْعامٌ حُرِّمَتْ ظُهُورُها ** (and cattle whose backs are forbidden) (حُرِّمَتْ) means unlawful (حرام). ظُهُورُها ** He said: meaning al-Bahirah (البحيرة), al-Sa'ibah (السائبة), al-Wasilah (الوصيلة), and al-Ham (الحام). وَأَنْعامٌ لا يَذْكُرُونَ اسْمَ اللهِ عَلَيْهَا ** (and cattle over which they do not mention Allah’s Name) during slaughtering (الذبح) and sacrificing (النحر). And it was said: they do not perform Hajj upon them, nor do they pronounce the Talbiyah upon their backs. The meaning is that they divided their cattle, saying: 'These are forbidden cattle (أَنْعامٌ حِجْرٌ),' and 'These are cattle whose backs are forbidden (أَنْعامٌ مُحَرَّمَةُ الظُّهُورِ),' and 'These are cattle over which the Name of Allah is not mentioned.' So they made them into categories (أَجْنَاسًا) through their false claim, and they attributed that division to Allah, افْتِراءً عَلَيْهِ ** (falsely attributing it to Him) meaning, they did all of that by way of fabrication (الافْتِرَاءِ). سَيَجْزِيهِمْ بِما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ.** (He will soon requite them for what they used to fabricate.)

(١٣٩) وَقالُوا ما فِي بُطُونِ هذِهِ الْأَنْعامِ خالِصَةٌ لِذُكُورِنا وَمُحَرَّمٌ عَلى أَزْواجِنا وَإِنْ يَكُنْ مَيْتَةً فَهُمْ فِيهِ شُرَكاءُ. (139) And they say, 'What is in the bellies of these cattle is exclusively for our males, and forbidden to our wives.' But if it be dead, they are partners in it.

In al-Qummi (التفسير), [it is stated that] they used to forbid the fetus (الجنين) which they took out of the bellies of the livestock to the women, but if it was dead, the men and the women would eat it. It was said that خالصة (khāliṣah) was feminized because ما (what) is in the meaning of fetuses (الأجنّة), and the تَاء (tā’) in it is for emphasis (المبالغة), as in the narration of al-shi'r (الشعر), or it is a verbal noun (مصدر) like al-ʿāfiyah (العافية). And تكن (takun) was read with the تَاء (tā’) and ميتة (maytatan) with the نصب (naṣb) in other ways. سَيَجْزِيهِمْ وَصْفَهُمْ ** Meaning: the recompense for their description (وصفهم) of falsehood (الكذب) upon Allah regarding prohibition (التحريم) and permission (التحليل), [which is understood] from His saying: تَصِفُ أَلْسِنَتُهُمُ الْكَذِبَ هذا حَلالٌ وَهذا حَرامٌ إِنَّهُ حَكِيمٌ عَلِيمٌ.** (which their tongues falsely describe: 'This is lawful, and this is unlawful.' He is indeed All-wise, All-knowing.)

(١٤٠) قَدْ خَسِرَ الَّذِينَ قَتَلُوا أَوْلادَهُمْ ** (Certainly, those have lost who killed their children) They used to kill their daughters for fear of captivity (السّبي) and poverty (الفقر). And it was read (قُتِّلُوا) with tashdid (doubling the letter), meaning multiplication (التكثير). سَفَهاً بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ ** (foolishly, without any knowledge) due to the lightness of their intellect and their ignorance that Allah is the Provider (رازق) of their children, not them. وَحَرَّمُوا ما رَزَقَهُمُ اللهُ ** (and forbade what Allah has provided them) such as the Bahā’ir (البحائر) and the like, افْتِراءً عَلَى اللهِ قَدْ ضَلُّوا وَما كانُوا مُهْتَدِينَ ** (falsely attributing it to Allah. They have certainly gone astray, and they were not guided) to the truth (الحق) and the correctness (الصواب).

(141) وَهُوَ الَّذِي أَنْشَأَ جَنَّاتٍ ** (It is He who produced gardens) [gardens] of grapevines مَعْرُوشاتٍ ** (trellised), raised upon that which supports them, وَغَيْرَ مَعْرُوشاتٍ ** (and untrellised), cast upon the surface of the earth, وَالنَّخْلَ وَالزَّرْعَ مُخْتَلِفاً أُكُلُهُ ** (and the date-palm and the crops, with diverse produce). The produce (أُكُلُهُ) of that—meaning its fruit which is eaten—[is diverse] in color, taste, size, and scent, وَالزَّيْتُونَ وَالرُّمَّانَ مُتَشابِهاً (and the olive and the pomegranate, similar) وَغَيْرَ مُتَشابِهٍ ** (and dissimilar). Some of their individual parts resemble each other in taste, color, and size, and some do not resemble each other. كُلُوا مِنْ ثَمَرِهِ ** (Eat of its fruit) from the fruit of every single one of those [plants] إِذا أَثْمَرَ ** (when it yields fruit), even if it has not ripened or matured yet. And it was said that its benefit is the permission granted to the owner to eat from it before rendering the right (حقّ) of Allah. I say: And that is only valid if he estimates (خرص) what he eats. وَآتُوا حَقَّهُ يَوْمَ حَصادِهِ ** (and render its due on the day of harvest). And it was read with a kasrah on the Ḥāʾ (الحاءِ). In Qurb al-Isnad: It was read before al-Rida (peace be upon him). He said to the reciter: "Did those before you read it this way?" He said: "Yes." He said: "Open the mouth with the Ḥāʾ (الحاءِ)." It is as if he (the reciter) was reading it with a kasrah. And it is as if al-Qummi also alluded to this when he said: "Thus it was revealed." It was said that by the right (الحقّ) is meant what is given in charity on the day of harvest, not the fixed Zakāh (الزكاة المقدرة), because Zakāh was mandated in Medina, and the Ayah is Meccan. And it was said, rather, it is the Zakāh (الزّكوة), meaning: do not delay it past the first time when giving it is possible, and the Ayah is Medinan. And what is narrated from the People of the House (peace be upon them) is that it is other than the Zakāh (الزّكوة). In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding crops, there are two rights (حقان): a right by which you are taken [to account], and a right which you give. As for that by which you are taken [to account], it is the tenth (العشر) and half a tenth (نصف العشر). And as for that which you give, it is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَآتُوا حَقَّهُ يَوْمَ حَصادِهِ ** (and render its due on the day of harvest). So, we give the handful (الضغث) (1), then the handful (الضغث), until you finish. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): This is from the charity (الصدقة). You give the destitute person a handful (القبضة) after a handful, and from the date-picking (الجذاذ) (2), a double handful (الحفنة) after a double handful (3). And al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: He said, the handful (الضغث) of the ears of grain, and the palmful (الكف) of dates, when it is estimated (خرص). And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding it: He said, give from your green produce (خضرك), to a polytheist (مشرك) or otherwise. And the narrations (الأخبار) concerning this meaning are numerous. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Do not harvest dates (تصرم) (1) at night, nor harvest crops at night, nor sacrifice (تضح) at night, nor sow seeds (تبذر) at night, up to His saying: "And if you harvest at night, the beggars (السّؤّال) will not come to you." And that is the saying of Allah: وَآتُوا حَقَّهُ يَوْمَ حَصادِهِ (and render its due on the day of harvest), meaning the handful (القبضة) after the handful when you harvest it. Then, when it is threshed (خرج), [you give] the double handful (الحفنة) after the double handful. And likewise during the date harvest (الصرام), and likewise during sowing (البذر). And do not sow seeds at night, because you give from the seeds as you give in the harvest. And narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: You give the destitute person on the day of your harvest the handful (الضغث), then when it reaches the threshing floor (البيذر), then when it reaches the Ṣāʿ (الصّاع) measure, [you give] the tenth (العشر) and half a tenth (نصف العشر). And al-Qummi said: Allah mandated on the day of harvest, from every piece of land, a handful (قبضة) for the destitute. And likewise during the date-picking (جذاذ) of the date-palm and the dates, and likewise during sowing (البذر). And al-Rida (peace be upon him) was asked: If the destitute are not present while he is harvesting, what should he do? He said: Nothing is incumbent upon him. And al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) was asked: Is it proper to give it after he has brought it in? He said: No. It is more generous for his soul before he brings it into his house. وَلا تُسْرِفُوا ** (and do not commit excess) in giving charity, like His saying: وَلا تَبْسُطْها كُلَّ الْبَسْطِ إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ ** (nor extend it with a total extension. Indeed, He does not like the extravagant). He does not approve of their action. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "My father used to say that part of the excess (الإسراف) in harvesting (الحصاد) and date-picking (الجذاذ) is for a man to give charity with both his hands entirely." And when my father was present for something of this nature and saw one of his servants giving charity with both his hands, he would shout at him: "Give with one hand, the handful (القبضة) after the handful, and the ear of grain (الضّغث) after the ear of grain." And narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: "So-and-so son of So-and-so al-Ansari—and he named him—had a crop, and when he took it, he would give it all in charity, leaving himself and his family with nothing. So, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made that an excess (سرفاً)." And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) in a hadith, he said: "And in more than one Ayah of the Book of Allah, He says: إِنَّهُ لا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ (Indeed, He does not like the extravagant). So, He forbade them from excess (الإسراف) and forbade them from stinginess (التقتير). Rather, He commanded a middle way (أمر بين أمرين): that he should not give away everything he possesses and then call upon Allah to provide for him, for He will not answer Him."

(1) الضغث (al-Dighth/al-Dhaghth), with kasrah and fatḥah, is a handful of mixed grass, both wet and dry. It is also said to be a palmful of stalks, grass, and date-spathes.

(2) I broke (جذذت) the thing, jadhdhan (جذاً) from the category of qatala (قتل), meaning I shattered it and cut it, so it is shattered (مجذوذ). Al-Jidhādh (الجذاذ), with ḍammah and kasrah—the ḍammah is more eloquent—is the cutting of what is broken. Al-Jidād (الجداد), with fatḥah and kasrah, is the date harvest (صرام النخل), which is the cutting of its fruit.

(3) Al-Ḥafnah (الحفنة), with fatḥah then sukūn, is the filling of both hands with food. The plural is ḥafanāt (حفنات), like sajdah (سجدة) and sajadāt (سجدات). And I gave a little (ḥafantu) to so-and-so, from the category of ḍaraba (ضرب).

(1) Al-Ṣirām (الصرام) and the date-picking (جذاذ النخل). This is the beginning of the date harvest (الصرام), with fatḥah and kasrah. And al-Ṣirmah (الصرمة) is a cluster of date-palms, about thirty [trees].

(١٤٢) وَمِنَ الْأَنْعامِ حَمُولَةً وَفَرْشاً ** (142) And among the cattle [He created] beasts of burden and those for bedding. And He brought forth from the cattle (الأنعام) that which carries heavy loads (الأثقال) and that from whose wool (وَبَر), fleece (صُوف), and hair (شَعْر) bedding (الْفَرْش) is woven. كُلُوا مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللهُ ** Eat of what Allah has provided you from them (the cattle). وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا خُطُواتِ الشَّيْطانِ ** and do not follow in the footsteps of Satan. regarding the prohibition (تَحْريم) of anything from them by yourselves. إِنَّهُ لَكُمْ عَدُوٌّ مُبِينٌ ** Indeed, he is your manifest enemy. whose enmity (الْعَداوَة) is manifest (ظاهِر).

(١٤٣) ثَمانِيَةَ أَزْواجٍ ** (eight pairs) is a substitute (بدل) for حَمُولَةً وَفَرْشاً ** (burden-bearing and floor-covering), or it is the object (مفعول) of كُلُوا وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا ** (Eat and do not follow), which is parenthetical (معترض). And the pair (الزّوج) is that which has another of its kind that pairs with it (يزاوجه), and sometimes the term is applied to the combination of the two. مِنَ الضَّأْنِ اثْنَيْنِ ** (of the sheep two) the domesticated (الأهليّ) and the wild (الوحشِي). وَمِنَ الْمَعْزِ اثْنَيْنِ ** (and of the goats two) the domesticated (الأهلي) and the wild (الوحشِيّ). And it was recited (قريء) with the opening of the ʿayn (بفتح العين). قُلْ آلذَّكَرَيْنِ ** (Say, 'Is it the two males) the male of the sheep (ذكر الضأن) and the male of the goats (ذكر المعز). حَرَّمَ أَمِ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ ** (that He has forbidden, or the two females) or their two females. أَمَّا اشْتَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَرْحامُ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ ** (or what the wombs of the two females contain?) or what the females (أناث) of the two kinds (الجنسين) carried, whether male (ذكراً) or female (أنثى). نَبِّئُونِي بِعِلْمٍ (Inform Me with knowledge), meaning: with a known matter (بأمر معلوم) that indicates that Allah has forbidden something from that. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ ** (if you are truthful) in the claim of attributing the prohibition (التحريم) to Him.

(١٤٤) وَمِنَ الْإِبِلِ اثْنَيْنِ العراب (١) والبخاتي (٢) وَمِنَ الْبَقَرِ اثْنَيْنِ (144) and from the camels two, and from the cattle two.

The al-ʿIrāb (العراب) (1) and the al-Bakhātī (البخاتي) (2). And from the cattle, two: the domestic (الأهليّ) and the wild (الوحشِي). And it was said that by "two" is meant the male and the female of every kind. But the correct view is what We have stated, as its clarification will follow. قُلْ آلذَّكَرَيْنِ حَرَّمَ أَمِ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ أَمَّا اشْتَمَلَتْ عَلَيْهِ أَرْحامُ الْأُنْثَيَيْنِ Say, 'Has He forbidden the two males, or the two females, or what the wombs of the two females contain?' As passed previously. The meaning is a denial that Allah forbade anything from the four kinds—whether domestic or wild, male or female, or what their females carry—as a refutation against them. For they used to forbid the males of the livestock sometimes, and their females sometimes, and their offspring, whatever they were, sometimes, claiming that Allah had forbidden them. أَمْ كُنْتُمْ شُهَداءَ Were you witnesses Rather, were you present, witnessing, إِذْ وَصَّاكُمُ اللهُ بِهذا when Allah charged you with this? when He charged you with this prohibition? For you do not believe in the Messengers, so you have no way to know such things except through direct observation (المشاهدة) or hearing (السماع). فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah by attributing to Him the prohibition of what is not forbidden? The intent is their leaders who established that [prohibition], or ʿAmr ibn Luḥayy (عمرو بن لحيّ), the founder of it, who slit the ears of the Baḥā’ir (البحائر) and set free the Sawā’ib (السّوائب), لِيُضِلَّ النَّاسَ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ إِنَّ اللهَ لا يَهْدِي الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ. (144) to mislead the people without any knowledge. Indeed, Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ): These are what Allah made lawful in His Book in His saying: وَأَنْزَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَنْعامِ ثَمانِيَةَ أَزْواجٍ (143) and He sent down for you eight pairs of the cattle. Then He explained them in this verse, saying: مِنَ الضَّأْنِ اثْنَيْنِ—meaning the domestic (الأهليّ) and the mountain (الجبليّ)—وَمِنَ الْمَعْزِ اثْنَيْنِ—meaning the domestic (الأهليّ) and the wild mountain (الوحشِيّ الجبليّ)—وَمِنَ الْبَقَرِ اثْنَيْنِ—meaning the domestic (الأهليّ) and the wild mountain (الوحشِيّ الجبليّ)—وَمِنَ الْإِبِلِ اثْنَيْنِ—meaning the al-Bakhātī (البخاتيّ) and the al-ʿIrāb (العراب). These are what Allah made lawful.

In al-Kāfī, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Noah (peace be upon him) carried in the Ark the eight pairs which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, mentioned in His saying: ثَمانِيَةَ أَزْواجٍ مِنَ الضَّأْنِ اثْنَيْنِ—the verse. So, from the sheep two were a domestic pair (زوج داجنة) (1) which people raise, and the other pair was the sheep that are in the wild mountains, the hunting of which He made lawful for them. وَمِنَ الْمَعْزِ اثْنَيْنِ were a domestic pair which people raise, and the other pair was the gazelles (الضّباء) that are in the caves. وَمِنَ الْإِبِلِ اثْنَيْنِ were the al-Bakhātī (البخاتي) and the al-ʿIrāb (العراب). وَمِنَ الْبَقَرِ اثْنَيْنِ were a domestic pair for the people, and the other pair was the wild cattle. And every good bird, wild and tame.

And in it (al-Kāfī) and in al-Faqīh, narrated from Dāwūd al-Ruqqī (داود الرقيّ), who said: Some of the Khawārij (الخوارج) asked me about this verse: مِنَ الضَّأْنِ اثْنَيْنِ—the verse—what Allah made lawful from that and what He forbade. I had no knowledge regarding it. So, I entered upon Abū ʿAbdillāh (peace be upon him) while I was performing Hajj, and I informed Him of what had happened. He said: "Indeed, Allah, the Exalted, made lawful for sacrifice (الأضحية) in Minā the domestic (الأهليّة) sheep and goats, and forbade sacrificing the mountain (الجبليّة) ones. As for His saying: وَمِنَ الْإِبِلِ اثْنَيْنِ وَمِنَ الْبَقَرِ اثْنَيْنِ, Allah, the Exalted, made lawful for sacrifice the al-ʿIrāb (العراب) camels, and forbade the al-Bakhātī (البخاتي) (2) from them. And He made lawful the domestic cattle for sacrifice and forbade the mountain ones." So, I returned to the man and informed him of this answer. He said: "This is something the camels carried from the Ḥijāz (الحجاز)."

I say: Perhaps the Khārijī (الخارجي) had heard of the prohibition of sacrificing some of these eight pairs, despite all of them being lawful [to eat], and he wanted to test Dāwūd's knowledge. And perhaps the reason for prohibiting the sacrifice of the mountain (الجَبَليّة) ones in Minā is that they are game (صيداً), and the prohibition of the al-Bukht (البخت) is due to another reason.

قُلْ لا أَجِدُ فِي ما أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ مُحَرَّماً Say, 'I do not find in what has been revealed to me anything forbidden a food forbidden عَلى طاعِمٍ يَطْعَمُهُ to a consumer who eats it.' In this is an indication that prohibition is established only by revelation (الوحي), not by caprice (الهوى). إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ except that it be the food مَيْتَةً أَوْ دَماً مَسْفُوحاً carrion, or spilled blood, poured out, like the blood in the veins, not like the liver (الكبد) and the spleen (الطحال), or that which is mixed with the meat and cannot be separated from it, أَوْ لَحْمَ خِنزِيرٍ فَإِنَّهُ رِجْسٌ defilement (قذر). أَوْ فِسْقاً أُهِلَّ لِغَيْرِ اللهِ بِهِ or a transgression over which the Name of Allah has not been invoked.' What was slaughtered in the name of an idol was called a transgression (فِسْقاً) due to its deep involvement (توغله) (1) in transgression. فَمَنِ اضْطُرَّ—meaning, whoever necessity calls upon to consume something of that—غَيْرَ باغٍ وَلا عادٍ فَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ But should someone be compelled, without desiring it or exceeding the limit, your Lord is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. He will not hold him accountable for eating it. The explanation of al-Bāghī (الباغي) and al-ʿĀdī (العادي) has already passed in Sūrat al-Baqarah (سورة البقرة).

If it is asked: Why were these four things specifically mentioned here as forbidden, even though others are also forbidden? For He, glory be to Him, mentioned in al-Mā’idah (المائِدة) the prohibition of the strangled animal (المنخنقة), the beaten animal (الموقوذة), the fallen animal (المتردية), and others. And sound narrations (الأخبار الصحيحة) have been reported concerning the prohibition of every bird with a talon (مخلب) (2), every beast with a canine tooth (ناب), and fish without scales, and so on. We reply: As for those mentioned in al-Mā’idah, the name of carrion (الميتة) applies to all of them, so they fall under its ruling. Thus, He summarized here and detailed there. As for others, they do not hold the same status in terms of prohibition (الحُرمة). So, He specified these things for prohibition to magnify their sanctity, and the prohibition of what is besides them was clarified by the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

And it was reported that it is among what is avoided (يعاف عنه). As for what was said—that this Sūrah is Meccan and al-Mā’idah is Medinan, and thus it is possible that what is forbidden besides what is in this verse was only forbidden later—this is not supported by the narrations reported concerning that from the People of the House (أهل البيت) (peace be upon them). Likewise, what al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said, for he stated: "A group of people argued using this verse that nothing is forbidden except this, and they made lawful every beast: monkeys (القردة), dogs (الكلاب), predatory animals (السباع), wolves (الذئاب), lions (الأسد), mules (البغال), donkeys (الحمير), and beasts of burden (الدّواب), claiming that all of that is lawful. And they made a clear mistake (غلطاً بيّناً) in this. Rather, this verse is a refutation of what the Arabs made lawful and what they forbade, because the Arabs used to make things lawful and forbidden for themselves. So, Allah related to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم what they said, and He said: وَقالُوا ما فِي بُطُونِ هذِهِ الْأَنْعامِ خالِصَةٌ لِذُكُورِنا وَمُحَرَّمٌ عَلى أَزْواجِنا (139) And they say, 'What is in the bellies of these cattle is exclusively for our males, and forbidden to our wives.'—the verse. Thus, if the fetus was dropped, the men ate it and it was forbidden to the women; and if it was dead, the men and women ate it." His statement ends here.

His statement ends here. We only said that the two opinions are not supported by the narrations because they were reported: that the forbidden is only what Allah has forbidden. And this verse was recited when they asked about the prohibition of animals not mentioned therein.

In al-Tahdhīb, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), and in al-ʿAyyāshī, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the al-Jurrī (الجرّي) (1), the al-Mār (المار) (2)—what are they?—and the al-Zimmīr (الزِّمّير) (3), and fish that have no scales: are they forbidden? He said to me: "O Muḥammad, recite this verse in al-Anʿām: قُلْ لا أَجِدُ فِي ما أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ مُحَرَّماً عَلى طاعِمٍ يَطْعَمُهُ" (145) Say, 'I do not find in what has been revealed to me anything forbidden to a consumer who eats it.' He said: So I recited it until I finished it. Then He said: "The forbidden is only what Allah and His Messenger have forbidden in His Book, but they used to avoid certain things, so we avoid them."

And from Imam al-Baqir, and in al-ʿAyyāshī from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both): He was asked about predatory birds (سباع الطير) and beasts (الوحش), until hedgehogs (القنافذ), bats (الوطواط) (4), donkeys (الحمير), mules (البغال), and horses (الخيل) were mentioned to Him. He said: "The forbidden is only what Allah has forbidden in His Book. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم forbade the eating of donkey meat on the Day of Khaybar (يوم خيبر), but He only forbade them because of their backs, lest they destroy them [by eating them], and donkeys are not forbidden." Then He said: "Recite this verse: قُلْ لا أَجِدُ—the verse." And from Him (peace be upon him): He was asked about the al-Jurrīth (الجرّيث). He asked: "What is the al-Jurrīth?" So it was described to Him. He said: لا أَجِدُ—the verse. Then He said: "Allah has not forbidden anything of the animals in the Quran except the swine specifically. And everything from the sea that does not have scales like paper (الورق) is disliked (يكره) but is not forbidden; rather, it is makrūh (مكروه)." And from one of them (peace be upon them both): The eating of the crow (الغراب) is not forbidden. The forbidden is only what Allah has forbidden in His Book, but souls abstain from much of that out of revulsion (تقززاً) (5). The author of al-Tahdhīb (صاحب التهذيب) said: His saying, "The forbidden is only what Allah has forbidden in His Book," means that the specific, severe, and highly dangerous forbidden thing (الحرام المخصوص المغلظ الشديد الخطر) is only what Allah mentioned in the Quran, even though there are also many forbidden things besides that, but they are less severe in prohibition (التغليظ).
