(١٤٦) وَعَلَى الَّذِينَ هادُوا حَرَّمْنا كُلَّ ذِي ظُفُرٍ ** (146) And to those who became Jews We forbade every animal with claws whether beast or bird وَمِنَ الْبَقَرِ and of the cattle وَالْغَنَمِ حَرَّمْنا عَلَيْهِمْ شُحُومَهُما ** and the sheep We forbade them their fat the caul fat (الثروب) and the fat of the kidneys إِلَّا ما حَمَلَتْ ظُهُورُهُما meaning, what adhered to their backs أَوِ الْحَوايا (١) or what the intestines (الأمعاء) contain أَوْ مَا اخْتَلَطَ بِعَظْمٍ which is the fat of the tail (شحم الإلية), for it is connected to the coccyx (العصعص) (2) ذلِكَ جَزَيْناهُمْ بِبَغْيِهِمْ ** Thus did We requite them for their transgression because of their injustice (ظلمهم) وَإِنَّا لَصادِقُونَ ** and We are indeed truthful. in the reports, the promise, and the threat.

(١٤٧) فَإِنْ كَذَّبُوكَ ** (147) If they deny you regarding what you say فَقُلْ رَبُّكُمْ ذُو رَحْمَةٍ واسِعَةٍ ** then say, 'Your Lord is the possessor of vast mercy.' He does not hasten the punishment, so do not be deceived by His respite. He does not grant respite when its time comes **وَلا يُرَدُّ بَأْسُهُ عَنِ الْقَوْمِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** 'And His might will not be averted from the guilty lot.' when it descends.

(١٤٨) سَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا لَوْ شاءَ اللهُ ما أَشْرَكْنا وَلا آباؤُنا وَلا حَرَّمْنا مِنْ شَيْءٍ كَذلِكَ كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِمْ ** (148) The polytheists will say, 'Had Allah willed, we would not have ascribed any partners [to Him], nor would our fathers, nor would we have forbidden anything.' Thus did those before them deny [the truth]. Meaning, like this denial to you (regarding the fact that Allah forbade shirk and did not forbid what they forbade), those before them denied the Messengers, حَتَّى ذاقُوا بَأْسَنا ** (until they tasted Our might), which We sent down upon them due to their denial. قُلْ هَلْ عِنْدَكُمْ مِنْ عِلْمٍ ** (Say, 'Do you have any knowledge) of a known matter by which it is valid to argue concerning what you claimed, فَتُخْرِجُوهُ لَنا ** (that you can produce for us?'), so you make it manifest for us. إِنْ تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا الظَّنَ ** (You follow nothing but conjecture), You follow in that nothing but conjecture (الظنّ), وَإِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا تَخْرُصُونَ ** (and you do nothing but surmise), You lie against Allah the Almighty.

(١٤٩) قُلْ فَلِلَّهِ الْحُجَّةُ الْبالِغَةُ (٣) Say, 'Allah's is the conclusive argument.' The clear evidence (البينة الواضحة) which has reached the utmost limit of firmness (المتانة) and strength (القوّة) regarding proof (الإثبات). فَلَوْ شاءَ لَهَداكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ** So had He wished, He would have guided you all. by granting success (التوفيق) for it and compelling (الحمل) towards it. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: لَوْ شاءَ ** Had He wished, He would have made all of you upon one matter (أمر واحد), but He made you upon difference (الاختلاف). In al-Kafi (الكافي), narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah has two arguments (حجتين) against people: an outward argument (حجة ظاهرة) and an inward argument (حجة باطنة). As for the outward one, it is the Messengers (الرسل), the Prophets (الأنبياء), and the Imams (الأئمّة). And as for the inward one, it is the intellects (العقول). And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): We are الْحُجَّةُ الْبالِغَةُ ** the conclusive argument over whoever is beneath the sky and above the earth. And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated the like of it from him (peace be upon him). And in al-Amali (الأمالي), narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He was asked about the Almighty's saying: فَلِلَّهِ الْحُجَّةُ الْبالِغَةُ ** Say, 'Allah's is the conclusive argument.' He said: "Indeed, Allah the Almighty will say to the servant on the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة): 'My servant, were you knowledgeable (عالماً)?' If he says yes, He will say to him: 'Then why did you not act upon what you knew?' And if he was ignorant (جاهلاً), He will say to him: 'Then why did you not learn (تعلّمت) so that you could act?' Thus, He defeats him in argument (فيخصمه). That is the conclusive argument (الحجة البالغة)." And in a narration from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْحُجَّةُ الْبالِغَةُ ** The conclusive argument is that which reaches the ignorant (الْجاهِل) among the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب), so he knows it through his ignorance (بجهله) just as the knowledgeable person (العالم) knows it through his knowledge (بعلمه).

(150) قُلْ هَلُمَّ شُهَداءَكُمُ (150) Say, 'Produce your witnesses Bring them forth الَّذِينَ يَشْهَدُونَ أَنَّ اللهَ حَرَّمَ هذا who testify that Allah has forbidden this.' meaning their leaders (قدوتهم) concerning it. He summons them forth so that He may establish the proof (الحجة) against them, and so that their misguidance (ضلالتهم) may become manifest through their inability (to produce proof), and that they have no support (متمسّك) just like those who follow them (in blind imitation). And for this reason, He restricted the witnesses by attribution (بالإضافة) and described them with what necessitates familiarity (العهد) with them. فَإِنْ شَهِدُوا فَلا تَشْهَدْ مَعَهُمْ Then if they testify, do not testify with them, so do not believe them concerning it, and make clear to them its corruption. وَلا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْواءَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا and do not follow the desires of those who deny Our signs, In this is an indication that denial (التكذيب) is caused by following desire (الهوى), and affirmation (التصديق) is caused by following the proof (الحجة). وَالَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِالْآخِرَةِ and those who do not believe in the Hereafter, like the idol worshipers وَهُمْ بِرَبِّهِمْ يَعْدِلُونَ and equate others with their Lord.' they set up an equal (عديلاً) to Him.

(١٥١) قُلْ تَعالَوْا أَتْلُ I recite ما حَرَّمَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ أَلَّا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئاً When He made obligatory the abandonment of polytheism (الشّرك) and kindness (الإحسان) to the parents, He has thereby forbidden polytheism (الشرك) and ill-treatment (الإساءة) towards them, because the obligation of a thing is a prohibition of its opposite (ضِدّه). Thus, it is correct that this occurs as a detailed explanation (تَفْصِيلاً) of what He has forbidden. وَبِالْوالِدَيْنِ إِحْساناً He placed this in the position of prohibiting ill-treatment (الإِساءة) towards them, for the sake of emphasis (لِلْمُبَالَغَة) and to indicate that merely abandoning ill-treatment in their regard is not sufficient. Al-Qummi narrated, disconnectedly (مَقْطُوعاً), saying: بِالْوالِدَيْنِ refers to the Messenger of Allah and the Commander of the Faithful (may the blessings of Allah be upon them both). وَلا تَقْتُلُوا أَوْلادَكُمْ مِنْ إِمْلاقٍ due to poverty or out of fear of poverty, because of His saying: خَشْيَةَ إِمْلاقٍ نَحْنُ نَرْزُقُكُمْ وَإِيَّاهُمْ (for fear of poverty. We provide for you and for them). وَلا تَقْرَبُوا الْفَواحِشَ the major sins (كبائر الذّنوب) or fornication (الزنا) ما ظَهَرَ مِنْها وَما بَطَنَ ** In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): ما ظَهَرَ ** (what is manifest) is marrying the father's wife (نكاح امرأة الأب), and وَما بَطَنَ ** (what is hidden) is fornication (الزنا). And in al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): ما ظَهَرَ ** (what is manifest) is fornication (الزّنا), and وَما بَطَنَ ** (what is hidden) is al-mukhālah (المخالة) (1). وَلا تَقْتُلُوا النَّفْسَ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللهُ إِلَّا بِالْحَقِ such as retaliation (الْقَوَد), killing the apostate (قَتْل الْمُرْتَدّ), and stoning the married adulterer (رَجْم الْمُحْصَن). ذلِكُمْ This is a reference to what has been mentioned in detail. وَصَّاكُمْ بِهِ (He enjoins you concerning it) means: to preserve it (بِحِفْظِه). لَعَلَّكُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ.**

(1) Al-Mukhālah (المخالة) with tashdid (بالتشديد) is derived from al-khullah (الخلّة), meaning the taking of a close friend (اتخاذ الخليل). Allah the Almighty said: وَلا مُتَّخِذاتِ أَخْدانٍ (nor those who take secret lovers). May Allah have mercy on him. And khālah (خاله) mukhālah (مخالة) and khilāl (خلالاً) (and it may be pronounced with fath (ويفتح)). And He is the Generous al-khill (الخلّ) and al-khullah (الخلّة) (both with kasrah (بكسرهما)), meaning friendship (المصادقة) and brotherhood (الإخاء). Al-khullah (الخلة) is also the friend, used for male and female, singular and plural.

(١٥٢) وَلا تَقْرَبُوا مالَ الْيَتِيمِ إِلَّا بِالَّتِي هِيَ أَحْسَنُ ** (152) Do not approach the orphan’s property except in the best manner The exception is by the characteristic (الخصلة) which is better than what is usually done with his property, such as preserving it and making it grow (تثميره). حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ أَشُدَّهُ ** until he comes of age. meaning his strength (قوّته), which is reaching puberty (بلوغ الحلم) and the perfection of intellect (كمال العقل).

In al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The cessation of the orphan's state of orphanhood (يُتْم) is puberty (الاحتلام), and that is his coming of age (أشدّه). But if he reaches puberty and his sound judgment (رشده) is not perceived from him, and he is foolish (سفيهاً) or weak, then his guardian (وليّه) must withhold his property from him.

And in both of them (al-Faqih and al-Tahdhib), and in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When he reaches his coming of age (أشدّه) at thirteen years and enters the fourteenth, what is obligatory upon those who have reached puberty (المحتلمين) becomes obligatory upon him, whether he has reached puberty or not. Evil deeds (السيّئات) are recorded against him, and good deeds (الحسنات) are recorded for him, and everything becomes permissible for him, unless he is weak or foolish (سفيهاً).

وَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ وَالْمِيزانَ بِالْقِسْطِ ** and fill up the measure and the balance with justice. meaning with justice (بالعدل) and equity (التسوية). لا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْساً إِلَّا وُسْعَها ** We task no soul except according to its capacity. meaning except what is within its capacity (يسعها) and is not difficult (يعسر) for it in adhering to the fulfillment of measuring and weighing. This is an alert (تنبيه) regarding its difficulty (تعسيره) and that what is beyond one's capacity (الوسع) in it is pardoned (معفوّ).

وَإِذا قُلْتُمْ ** When you speak, concerning a judgment (حكومة) or similar matter, فَاعْدِلُوا ** be just, in it, وَلَوْ كانَ ذا قُرْبى ** even if it involves a relative, meaning even if the one spoken to or against is among your relatives (ذوي قرابتكم). وَبِعَهْدِ اللهِ أَوْفُوا and fulfill Allah’s covenant. meaning what He entrusted to you concerning adherence to justice (ملازمة العدل) and the performance of the rulings of the Sacred Law (احكام الشرع). ذلِكُمْ وَصَّاكُمْ بِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ ** This is what He has enjoined upon you so that you may take admonition. meaning that you may be admonished (تتّعظون) by it. And it was recited with the takhfif (lightening) of the dhāl (تَذْكُرُونَ).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was reclining on His couch when He recited the firm verses (الآيات المحكمات) from al-An'am (Chapter 6) which nothing has abrogated (ينسخهن). He then said: Seventy thousand angels accompanied them. **قُلْ تَعالَوْا أَتْلُ ما حَرَّمَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ أَلَّا تُشْرِكُوا بِهِ شَيْئاً ** (Say, 'Come, I will recite what your Lord has forbidden you: that you do not ascribe any partners to Him.')—the verses.

And in al-Majma', narrated from Ibn Abbas: These verses are firm (محكمات); nothing from any of the Books has abrogated them. They are prohibitions (محرمات) upon all the children of Adam, and they are the Mother of the Book (ام الكتاب). Whoever acts upon them shall enter Paradise, and whoever abandons them shall enter the Fire. Ka'b al-Ahbar said: "By Him in Whose Hand is the soul of Ka'b, this is the first thing in the Torah: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful) قُلْ تَعالَوْا أَتْلُ ما حَرَّمَ رَبُّكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ (Say, 'Come, I will recite what your Lord has forbidden you:')"—the verses.

(١٥٣) وَأَنَّ هَذَا صِرَاطِي مُسْتَقِيمًا فَاتَّبِعُوهُ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا السُّبُلَ فَتَفَرَّقَ بِكُمْ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ ذَلِكُمْ وَصَّاكُمْ بِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (153) (153) And that this is My straight path, so follow it, and do not follow [other] ways, for they will separate you from His way. This is what He has enjoined you, so that you may be Godwary.

And the an (أَنَّ) is a justification (تَعْلِيل) for the command to follow Him. هذا صِراطِي مُسْتَقِيماً (This is My straight path). It was said that the reference (الإشارة) therein is to what was mentioned in the Surah, for it is entirely about establishing Monotheism (التّوحيد), Prophethood (النّبوة), and clarifying the Law (الشريعة). And inna (إنّ) was recited with a kasrah (بالكسر) based on starting a new sentence (الاستيناف), and with a fathah (بالفتح) and takhfif (التخفيف), and sirati (صِراطِي) with a fathah on the ya (الياءِ) and with the sin (بالسّين). فَاتَّبِعُوهُ وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا السُّبُلَ (so follow it, and do not follow [other] ways)—meaning: the different religions (الأديان المختلفة) branching out from divergent desires (الأهوية المتباينة). فَتَفَرَّقَ بِكُمْ (for they will separate you)—meaning: they will separate you (فَتفرّقكم) and remove you (وتُزِيلَكُمْ) عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ (from His way)—which is the following of Revelation (الوحي) and the tracing of the proof (البرهان). ذلِكُمْ (This)—meaning: the following (الإتباع). وَصَّاكُمْ بِهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ (what He has enjoined you, so that you may be Godwary)—meaning: you may guard against misguidance (الضّلال) and separation from the Truth (الحق).

في روضة الواعظين In Rawdat al-Wa'izin, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Regarding this verse, I asked Allah to make it for Ali عليه السلام, and He did.

وفي الاحتِجاج عنه And in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him عليه السلام, in the Sermon of Ghadir (خطبة الغدير): O people, Allah has commanded me and forbidden me, and I have commanded Ali and forbidden him, so he knows the command and the prohibition from his Lord. Listen to his command, and you will be safe; obey him, and you will be guided; desist from his prohibition, and you will be rightly guided; and proceed toward his desire, and let not the ways separate you from his path. O people, I am the Straight Path (الصراط المستقيم) which I command you to follow, then Ali after me, then my children from his loins, Imams (أئمّة): يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ. (who guide by the truth and dispense justice thereby.)

والعيّاشيّ عن And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: That He said to Burayd al-Ijli: Do you know what is meant by "My straight path" (صِراطِي مُسْتَقِيماً)? I said: No. He said: The Wilayah (ولاية) of Ali and the Successors (الأوصياء) عليهم السلام. He said: And do you know what is meant by فَاتَّبِعُوهُ (so follow it)? I said: No. He said: He means Ali ibn Abi Talib. He said: And do you know what is meant by وَلا تَتَّبِعُوا السُّبُلَ (and do not follow [other] ways)? I said: No. He said: The Wilayah (ولاية) of so-and-so and so-and-so, by Allah. He said: And do you know what is meant by فَتَفَرَّقَ بِكُمْ عَنْ سَبِيلِهِ (for they will separate you from His way)? I said: No. He said: He means the path (سبيل) of Ali عليه السلام.

(١٥٤) ثُمَّ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ ** (Then We gave Moses the Book) is a conjunction (عطف) upon وَصَّاكُمْ . And thumma (ثُمَّ) is for remoteness (التراخي) in the narration or for difference in rank (الرتبة). As if it were said: ذلِكُمْ وَصَّاكُمْ بِهِ ** (That is what He has enjoined upon you) anciently and recently; then, greater than that, We آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ تَماماً ** (gave Moses the Book, complete) for the honor (الكرامة) and the blessing (النعمة). عَلَى الَّذِي أَحْسَنَ ** (for him who excels)—meaning, upon him who excels in upholding it. وَتَفْصِيلاً لِكُلِّ شَيْءٍ (and a detailed explanation of everything)—meaning, a detailed clarification (بياناً مفصّلاً) of everything needed in the religion. وَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةً لَعَلَّهُمْ (and a guidance and a mercy, so that perhaps they)—perhaps the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل)—بِلِقاءِ رَبِّهِمْ يُؤْمِنُونَ (may believe in the meeting with their Lord), meaning, in meeting Him for the recompense (للجزاء).

(١٥٥) وَهذا كِتابٌ أَنْزَلْناهُ مُبارَكٌ فَاتَّبِعُوهُ وَاتَّقُوا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (155) And this is a Book We have sent it down, blessed. So follow it and be wary, so that you may receive mercy. وَهذا كِتابٌ ** meaning the Quran أَنْزَلْناهُ مُبارَكٌ ** abundant in benefit فَاتَّبِعُوهُ وَاتَّقُوا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ by following it and acting upon what is in it. (١٥٦) أَنْ تَقُولُوا إِنَّما أُنْزِلَ الْكِتابُ عَلى طائِفَتَيْنِ مِنْ قَبْلِنا وَإِنْ كُنَّا عَنْ دِراسَتِهِمْ لَغافِلِينَ (156) lest you should say, 'The Book was sent down only to two groups before us, and we were indeed unaware of their study.' أَنْ تَقُولُوا ** We sent it down lest you should say إِنَّما أُنْزِلَ الْكِتابُ عَلى طائِفَتَيْنِ مِنْ قَبْلِنا ** the Jews and the Christians وَإِنْ كُنَّا ** and that we were indeed عَنْ دِراسَتِهِمْ ** their reading (قرائتهم) **لَغافِلِينَ ** we do not know what it is.

(١٥٧) أَوْ تَقُولُوا لَوْ أَنَّا أُنْزِلَ عَلَيْنَا الْكِتابُ لَكُنَّا أَهْدى مِنْهُمْ ** (157) lest you should say, 'Had the Book been sent down to us, we would have been better guided than them.' (This is) due to the sharpness (لحدة) of our minds and the penetration (ثقابة) of our understandings. For this reason, we have grasped various branches (فنوناً) of knowledge, such as stories (القصص), poetry (الأشعار), and sermons (الخطب), despite the fact that we are unlettered (امّيّون). فَقَدْ جاءَكُمْ بَيِّنَةٌ مِنْ رَبِّكُمْ ** Now a manifest proof has certainly come to you from your Lord, (Meaning) a clear argument (حجة واضحة) which you recognize, وَهُدىً وَرَحْمَةٌ ** and a guidance and a mercy. (Meaning) for whoever reflects upon it and acts upon it. فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنْ كَذَّبَ بِآياتِ اللهِ ** So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who denies the signs of Allah (This is) after he has known their authenticity or was able to know them, وَصَدَفَ ** and turns away from them? (Meaning) he turned away (أعرض) and averted (وصد) (1). Al-Qummi said: meaning, he repelled (دفع). عَنْها ** from them? (Meaning) he went astray (فضل) and led others astray (واضلّ). سَنَجْزِي الَّذِينَ يَصْدِفُونَ عَنْ آياتِنا سُوءَ الْعَذابِ ** Soon We shall requite those who turn away from Our signs with the worst of torments (Meaning) its severity (شدته), بِما كانُوا يَصْدِفُونَ because of their turning away. (Meaning) due to their aversion (باعراضهم) and their turning away (وصدهم).

هَلْ يَنْظُرُونَ (١٥٨) إِلَّا أَنْ تَأْتِيَهُمُ الْمَلائِكَةُ أَوْ يَأْتِيَ رَبُّكَ أَوْ يَأْتِيَ بَعْضُ آياتِ رَبِّكَ. (158) Do they await except that the angels should come to them, or your Lord should come, or some of the signs of your Lord should come?

This is a denial, meaning: What are they awaiting except that the angels should come to them—the angels of death or the angels of punishment—or your Lord should come—meaning, His command for punishment—or some of the signs of your Lord should come?

In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) regarding the meaning of this Ayah: He only addressed our Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): Do the hypocrites (المنافقون) and the polytheists (المشركون) await except that the angels should come to them so they may witness them, or your Lord should come, or some of the signs of your Lord should come? He means by that the command of your Lord, and the signs (الآيات) are the punishment (العذاب) in the abode of this world (دار الدنيا), just as He punished the preceding nations (الأمم السالفة) and the bygone generations (٢) (القرون الخالية).

And in it (al-Ihtijaj) and in al-Tawhid, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He informs Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) about the polytheists (المشركون) and the hypocrites (المنافقون) who did not respond to Allah and His Messenger, so He said: Do they await except that the angels should come to them? since they did not respond to Allah and His Messenger, or your Lord should come, or some of the signs of your Lord should come? He means by that the punishment (العذاب) that comes to them in the abode of this world (دار الدنيا), just as He punished the first generations (القرون الأولى). **يَوْمَ يَأْتِي بَعْضُ آياتِ رَبِّكَ لا يَنْفَعُ نَفْساً إِيمانُها لَمْ تَكُنْ آمَنَتْ مِنْ قَبْلُ أَوْ كَسَبَتْ فِي إِيمانِها خَيْراً ** (The day when some of the signs of your Lord come, its faith shall not benefit any soul which had not believed before, or earned some good in its faith.) The meaning was that faith will not benefit at that time a soul that had not previously offered its faith, or a soul that had previously offered its faith but had not earned good in its faith.

In al-Tawhid, in the preceding narration: from before means before this sign (الآية) comes, and this sign is the rising of the sun from its setting place (طلوع الشّمس من مغربها). And similarly in al-Ihtijaj, narrated from him (peace be upon him).

And al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was revealed as "or earned" (أو اكتسبت) some good in its faith. He said: When the sun rises from its setting place (مغربها), whoever believes on that day, his faith will never benefit him.

And in al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the sun rises from its setting place (مغربها), all people will believe on that day, and on that day its faith shall not benefit any soul.

And similarly in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them) regarding His saying: The day when some of the signs of your Lord come. He said: The rising of the sun from the West (المغرب), the emergence of the Dajjal (الدّجال), the Smoke (الدخان), and the man who is persistent [in sin] (مصرّاً) and has not performed the deeds of faith (عمل الإِيمان); then the signs (الآيات) come, and his faith does not benefit him.

And narrated from one of the two of them (peace be upon them) regarding His saying: or earned some good in its faith. He said: The disobedient believer (المؤمن العاصي); the multitude of his sins (كثرة ذنوبه) and the paucity of his good deeds (قِلّة حَسَناته) intervened between him and his faith, so he did not earn good in his faith.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): from before means in the Covenant (الميثاق). or earned some good in its faith. He said: The affirmation of the Prophets (الأنبياء), the Successors (الأوصياء), and the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them) specifically. He said: Her faith will not benefit her because she was stripped [of it] (سلبَت).

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: It means the emergence of the Awaited Riser (القائم المنتظر).

And narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The signs (الآيات) are the Imams (peace be upon them), and the Awaited Sign (الآية المنتظرة) is the Riser (القائم) (peace be upon him). On that day its faith shall not benefit any soul.

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a narration wherein he mentions the emergence of the Dajjal (الدجال) and his killer. He says at the end of it: "Except that after that will be the Greatest Calamity (الطّامّة الكبرى)." It was asked: "And what is that, O Commander of the Faithful?" He said: "The emergence of the Beast of the Earth (دابّة الأرض) from near al-Safa, having with it the ring of Solomon (خاتم سليمان) and the staff of Moses (عصا موسى) (peace be upon him). It places the ring upon the face of every believer (مؤمن), and imprinted upon it is: 'This is truly a believer (مؤمن حقّاً).' And it places it upon the face of every disbeliever (كافر), and marked upon it is: 'This is truly a disbeliever (كافر حقّاً).' Until the believer calls out: 'Woe unto you, O disbeliever!' and the disbeliever calls out: 'Blessedness (طوبى) is for you, O believer! I wish I were like you, so I might achieve a great triumph (فوزاً عظيماً).' Then the Beast (الدابّة) raises its head, and it is seen between the two horizons (١) (الخافقين) by the permission of Allah, Majestic is His Glory. And that is after the rising of the sun from its setting place (مغربها). At that time, repentance (التوبة) is lifted, so no repentance is accepted, and no deed is raised, and its faith shall not benefit any soul which had not believed before, or earned some good in its faith." Then Sa'sa'ah (صعصعة), the narrator of this tradition, interpreted the rising of the sun from its setting place (مغربها) as the emergence of the Riser (القائم) (peace be upon him).

قُلِ انْتَظِرُوا (٢) إِنَّا مُنْتَظِرُونَ ** (Say, 'Wait! We too are waiting.') This is a threat (وعيد) and a warning (تهديد) to them, meaning: Wait for the coming of one of the three [events], We too are waiting for it, and at that time, the triumph (الفوز) will be ours, and the woe (الويل) will be yours.

(١٥٩) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا دِينَهُمْ ** (159) Indeed, those who split up their religion they scattered it (بددوه) (3), so they believed in some of it and disbelieved in some of it, and they differed concerning it. And it was recited as فارقوا (they separated), meaning they dissociated themselves (باينوا). And he attributed this reading in al-Majma' to the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: Ali (peace be upon him) used to recite it as فارقوا دينهم (they separated from their religion). He said: By Allah, the people separated.

وَكانُوا شِيَعاً ** and became sects groups (فرقاً), every group following an Imam.

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): that they are the people of misguidance (أهل الضّلال) and the followers of doubts (الشبهات) and innovations (البدع) from this nation (الأمّة).

And al-Qummi said: They separated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and became factions (أحزاباً).

And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): regarding this verse, By Allah, the people separated from their religion.

And in the Prophetic tradition (الحديث النبوي): My nation (أمتي) will split into seventy-three sects, all of them in the Fire except one, which is the one that follows my successor (وصيّي), Ali.

لَسْتَ مِنْهُمْ فِي شَيْءٍ ** you have nothing to do with them. It was said: meaning, from questioning about them and about their splitting. And it was said: its meaning is that you are in complete dissociation (المباعدة التامة) from joining them in any of their corrupt doctrines (مذاهبهم الفاسدة).

إِنَّما أَمْرُهُمْ Their matter rests only with Allah; Their matter and the judgment between them regarding their differences rests with Allah;

ثُمَّ يُنَبِّئُهُمْ بِما كانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ then He will inform them concerning what they used to do. by way of recompense (بالمجازاة).

(١٦٠) The Almighty's saying: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثالِها (Whoever brings a good deed shall receive ten times its like) meaning ten good deeds like it, as a favor (فضلاً) from Allah, the Almighty.

In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When this Ayah was revealed: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْها (Whoever brings a good deed shall receive better than it), the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "O Lord, increase me." So Allah, the Glorified, revealed: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثالِها (Whoever brings a good deed shall receive ten times its like) — the rest of the narration.

Al-Qummi (said): So this (Ayah) abrogates (ناسخة) His saying: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ خَيْرٌ مِنْها. (Whoever brings a good deed shall receive better than it.)

I say: This is the least of what was promised regarding multiplication (الإضعاف), and the promise has also come regarding seventy, seven hundred, and without measure (بغير حساب).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked whether the believer (المؤمن) has any superiority (فضل) over the Muslim (المسلم) in any of the merits (الفضائل), rulings (الأحكام), legal punishments (الحدود), or other matters. He replied: "No, they both proceed in that regard in the same manner. However, the believer has superiority over the Muslim in their deeds (أعمالها) and what they use to draw near to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic." It was asked: "Does not Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, say: مَنْ جاءَ بِالْحَسَنَةِ فَلَهُ عَشْرُ أَمْثالِها (Whoever brings a good deed shall receive ten times its like)? And you claimed that they (Muslims) share with the believer in prayer (الصلوة), Zakat (الزكوة), fasting (الصوم), and Hajj (الحجّ)." He (peace be upon him) said: "Has not Allah also said: فَيُضاعِفَهُ لَهُ أَضْعافاً كَثِيرَةً (and multiply it for him manifold)? The believers are those for whom Allah multiplies their good deeds, seventyfold for every good deed. This is the superiority of the believer. And Allah increases him in his good deeds manifold, according to the soundness of his faith (إيمانه). And Allah does with the believers whatever He wills of good."

And Al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: It is general for all Muslims. He said: If there is no Wilayah (ولاية), then what he did of good deeds in this world will be used to ward off (punishment) from him, and he will have no share (خلاق) in the Hereafter. وَمَنْ جاءَ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ فَلا يُجْزى إِلَّا مِثْلَها (But whoever brings an evil deed shall be requited only with its like), as justice (عدلاً) from Allah, the Glorified. وَهُمْ لا يُظْلَمُونَ (and they will not be wronged), by the reduction of reward (الثواب) or the increase of punishment (العقاب).

Al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When Allah, the Glorified, gave Iblis what He gave him of power, Adam said: "O Lord, You have given him authority over my children and made him flow through them like blood in the veins, and You have given him what You have given him. So what is for me and my children?" He said: "For you and your children, the evil deed (السّيئة) is counted as one, and the good deed (الحسنة) as ten times its like." He said: "O Lord, increase me." He said: "Repentance (التوبة) is extended until the soul reaches the throat (الحلقوم)." He said: "O Lord, increase me." He said: "I forgive and I do not care (ولا أبالي)." He said: "That is enough for me (حسبي)."

I say: Perhaps the secret (السّرّ) behind the good deed being ten times its like and the evil deed being only its like is that the believing human essence (الجوهر الإنساني المؤمن) is naturally inclined toward the higher world (العالم العلوّي) because it is derived from it, and its descent to the physical mold (القالب الجسماني) is alien to its nature. The good deed only ascends to that which conforms to the nature of that essence because it is of its kind. The power that moves a stone upward one cubit (ذراعاً واحداً) is exactly the same power that, if used to move it downward, moves it ten cubits or more. Therefore, the good deed is ten times its like, up to seven hundredfold, and some of them have their reward fulfilled without measure (بغير حساب). And the good deed whose effect is not repelled by seeking fame (سمعة) or showing off (رياء) or vanity (عجب) is like a stone that rolls down from a high peak (شاهق) and meets no resistance, because the measure of its descent (هويته) cannot be quantified by calculation until it reaches its limit (الغاية).

قُلْ إِنَّنِي هَدانِي رَبِّي إِلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** (161) Say, 'Indeed my Lord has guided me to a straight path, (by means of) revelation (الوَحْي) and guidance (الإرشاد). دِيناً ** He guided me دِيناً قِيَماً ** (a religion that is) upright. It is derived (فَيُعِل) from (قام) like (السَّيِّد) and (الهَيِّن). And it was recited (قِيَماً) with a kasrah on the qāf (قِ) and a light yā’ (ي) as a verbal noun (المَصْدَر). مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ حَنِيفاً ** He guided me and made known to me مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ ** (the creed of Abraham) in the state of his uprightness (حَنِيفِيَّتِهِ) (1). وَما كانَ مِنَ الْمُشْرِكِينَ ** the upright creed of Abraham, and he was not one of the polytheists.'

Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The upright religion (الحَنِيفِيَّة) has left nothing out, even trimming the nails (قَصّ الأظفار), taking from the mustache (الأَخْذ مِن الشَّارِب), and circumcision (الخِتَان) are part of it.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): There is no one from this nation (الأُمَّة) who follows the religion (دِين) of Abraham except us and our followers (شِيعَتُنَا).

And narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): No one is upon the creed مِلَّةَ إِبْراهِيمَ ** (peace be upon him) except us and our followers (شِيعَتُنَا), and the rest of the people are innocent (بَرَآء) of it.

footnote (١) *Al-Hanīf* (*الحَنِيف*): The Muslim (*المُسْلِم*) who inclines toward the straight religion (*الدِّين المُسْتَقِيم*). The plural is *Hunafā’* (*حُنَفَاء*). *Al-Hanīf* (*الحَنِيف*) is the Muslim because he does not *taḥannaf* (*تَحَنَّف*), meaning he seeks the straight religion. *Al-Ḥanaf* (*الحَنَف*) (with a *fatḥah* on the *ḥā’* and *nūn*) is uprightness (*الاسْتِقَامَة*).

(١٦٢) قُلْ إِنَّ صَلاتِي وَنُسُكِي ** (Say, 'Indeed my prayer and my worship) My worship (عبادتي) and my sacrifice (وقرباني). وَمَحْيايَ وَمَماتِي ** (and my life and my death) And that which I am upon in my life and that upon which I die, consisting of faith (الإيمان) and obedience (الطاعة), are for Allah, the Lord of all the worlds (لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ), purely for Him (خالصة له). (١٦٣) لا شَرِيكَ لَهُ ** (He has no partner) I do not associate anyone other than Him in them. وَبِذلِكَ ** (and I have been commanded [to follow] this) Meaning, this sincerity (الإِخلاص) to Allah, أُمِرْتُ وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُسْلِمِينَ (and I am the first of those who submit). It was said: Because the submission (إسلام) of every prophet precedes the submission (إسلام) of his nation (أمته). I say: Rather, it is because He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was the first to respond in the Covenant (الميثاق) in the world of particles (عالم الذر), as has been narrated from them (the Imams) عليهم السلام. Thus, His submission (إسلامه) precedes the submission (إسلام) of all creation (الخلائق كلهم). Al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: In a hadith in which he mentioned Abraham عليه السلام, he said: "His religion is my religion, and my religion is his religion. His tradition (سنّته) is my tradition (سنّتي), and my tradition (سنّتي) is his tradition (سنّته). My merit (فضلي) is his merit (فضله), and I am more meritorious than him."

(١٦٤) قُلْ أَغَيْرَ اللهِ أَبْغِي رَبًّا ** (164) Say, 'Shall I seek a lord other than Allah so that I associate Him in my worship? This is a response to their call for him to worship their deities. وَهُوَ رَبُّ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ ** —while He is the Lord of all things? while everything besides Him is created and governed (مربوب) just like me, and is not fit for Lordship (الربوبية). وَلا تَكْسِبُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ** No soul does evil but that it is against it, the recompense for an action, whether obedience or disobedience, إِلَّا عَلَيْها (but that it is against it). Thus, upon it is the punishment for its disobedience, and for it is the reward for its obedience. وَلا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى ** and no bearer shall bear another's burden. No soul shall carry the sin (إثم) of another soul. This is a response to their saying: اتَّبِعُوا سَبِيلَنا وَلْنَحْمِلْ خَطاياكُمْ. (Follow our way, and we will bear your sins.)

In al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked: "What do you say regarding a narration reported from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that when al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) appears, He will kill the descendants (ذراري) of the murderers of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) due to the actions of their fathers?" He (peace be upon him) said: "It is so." It was said: "What is the meaning of the Almighty's saying: وَلا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى ** (and no bearer shall bear another's burden)?" He said: "Allah speaks the truth in all His sayings. However, the descendants (ذراري) of the murderers of al-Husayn (peace be upon him) are pleased with the actions of their fathers and boast about them. And whoever is pleased with something is like the one who committed it. If a man were killed in the East, and a man in the West were pleased with his killing, the one who is pleased would be a partner to the killer in the sight of Allah. Al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) will only kill them when He appears because of their pleasure with the actions of their fathers."

And in it (al-Uyun), concerning what He (peace be upon him) wrote to al-Ma'mun regarding the essence of Islam and the laws (شرائع) of the religion: Allah does not seize the innocent for the sick (السقيم), nor does Allah punish children for the sins of their fathers. وَلا تَزِرُ وازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرى ثُمَّ إِلى رَبِّكُمْ مَرْجِعُكُمْ ** and no bearer shall bear another's burden. Then to your Lord will be your return, on the Day of Resurrection, فَيُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِما كُنْتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (whereat He will inform you concerning that about which you used to differ,) by clarifying guidance (الرشد) from error (الغيّ) and distinguishing the truthful (المحق) from the false (المبطِل).

(165) وَهُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَكُمْ خَلائِفَ الْأَرْضِ (165) And He is the One who made you successors on the earth It was said: meaning, some of you succeed others; whenever a generation passes, another generation succeeds them, and this proceeds in an orderly and consistent manner until the Day of Resurrection. Or, [it means] the vicegerents (خلفاء) of Allah on His earth, acting therein. وَرَفَعَ بَعْضَكُمْ فَوْقَ بَعْضٍ دَرَجاتٍ and raised some of you in rank above others in honor (الشرف), wealth (الغنى), intellect (العقل), and other things, لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ that He may test you to try you فِي ما آتاكُمْ in what He has given you. of status (الجاه) and wealth (المال), [to see] how you show gratitude for His favors. إِنَّ رَبَّكَ سَرِيعُ الْعِقابِ Indeed your Lord is swift in retribution for whoever disbelieves in His favors, وَإِنَّهُ لَغَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ and He is indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. for whoever fulfills the gratitude for them.

In al-Kafi and Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, Surat al-An'am was revealed all at once (جملة واحدة), accompanied by seventy thousand angels until it descended upon Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم. So, venerate it and honor it, for the Name of Allah is in it in seventy places, and if people knew what is in its recitation, they would not abandon it.

And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Al-An'am was revealed all at once (جملة واحدة) and was accompanied by seventy thousand angels who had a clamor (زَجَلٌ) (1) of glorification (التسبيح), declaration of oneness (التهليل), and magnification (التكبير). So, whoever recites it, they glorify Him on his behalf until the Day of Resurrection.

footnote (1) *Al-Zajal* (*الزَّجل*) with the *harakah* (vowel movement) is the sound (*الصّوت*). It is said: a cloud is *zajal* (*سحاب زَجَل*) meaning it possesses thunder (*ذو رعد*). And from this [meaning comes the phrase]: they had a clamor (*زَجَلٌ*) of glorification (*بالتسبيح*).
